title: The Creak and John Steven Morgan: Original Indie Folk
description:
Five-piece string band the Creak, who blend folk, pop, and Americana with intriguing songwriting, makes their Red Poppy debut with mostly original music from their latest release, You Got Eyes, as well as select covers. Joined by composer/pianist John Steven Morgan, the evening includes a hard-swinging rhythm section and memorable melodies, along with instrumental interplay honed on San Francisco's vibrant street-corner busking circuit.rnrnFeaturing:rnJohn Steven Morgan - pianornJoe Readel - guitar, vocalsrnKorey Kassir - fiddle, mandolin, vocalsrnRyan Lim - bass, vocalsrnJoe Boone - guitar, vocalsrnAmy Levine - fiddle, vocalsrnrnJohn Steven Morgan has performed at the 54th and 56th Monterey Jazz Festival, Monterey First City Festival, The Wattis Room at SF Davies Symphony Hall, and has also toured internationally. Along with composing and performing his solo piano pieces, he writes music for Wreche, an experimental black metal collaboration with drummer Barret Baumgart. John has scored various short films, most notably The Perfect Hello, by Oakland director/writer Zack Kasten (music co-written with Greg Ashley of The Gris Gris).rnrnLearn more: The Creak | YouTube | Bandcamp | John Steven Morganrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: August 5th MAPP: Music of the Banned
description: Art can be used to bring awareness. Through art and culture, we can learn to identify with each other and explore what we all have in common. In a journey of peace and understanding, we can explore the six countries on Donald Trump's travel ban, which are a source of rich music and art. We are inviting the artistic ambassadors of these Muslim countries to lay down the drawbridge to their countries’ soundscape and beauty, and invite us all in.rnrnIn today’s America, there is a lack of awareness of the unknown. Put in front of a map, most Americans cannot identify the location of the countries currently listed as America’s number-one enemies and the source of terror. This will be the first showcase of many to come later this year. Art will break down the barriers that isolate us. The barriers that either governments or media have worked hard to create.rnrnSo many have been suffering from a lack of understanding and from this travel ban in particular. This is the year for us to make art in order to make a stand. Let’s stand to welcome the culture of the unknown, make ourselves aware of those that came to this country for a better life just as our ancestors.rnrnFinally, since we will have ongoing events, we are asking artists from Iran, Syria, Libya, Sudan, Yemen, or Somalia to please get in touch with us!rn rnABOUT MAPP:rnLaunched in 2003, the Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP) is a homegrown bi-monthly, multidisciplinary, intercultural happening that takes place in the Mission District of San Francisco. On the first Saturday of every even month of the year, the MAPP transforms ordinary spaces, such as private garages, gardens, living rooms, studios, street corners, and small businesses into pop-up performance and exhibition sites for a day/night of intimate-scale artistic and cultural exchange among a kaleidoscope of individuals and communities.rn rnRED POPPY SCHEDULE:rn7:00-7:15 - Sirvan ManhoubirnKurdish & Persian traditional music, with tombak, oud & setarrn

title: Sandy Cressman and Homenagem Brasileira: Brazilian Jazz Celebration
description:
Homenagem Brasileira plays the full spectrum of Brazilian jazz, from Rio-based bossa nova and samba to baião and frevo from Brazil’s northeast. At their Red Poppy show, they’ll present music from classic composers like Jobim and Caymmi, as well as music from Cressman's most recent album, Entre Amigos, which features collaborations with Jovino Santos Neto, Ian Faquini, Antonio Adolfo, Spok, and other contemporary composers.rnrnFeaturing:rnSandy Cressman - vocals, percussionrnJeff Cressman - trombonernMurray Low - pianornFred Randolph - bassrnPhil Hawkins - drumsrnrnSandy Cressman is a San Francisco-based jazz vocalist whose passion for Brazilian music led her to form Homenagem Brasileira in 1998. She has toured the U.S., Europe, and Brazil, and she has released three critically acclaimed CDs: Homenagem Brasileira, Brasil-Sempre no Coração, and Entre Amigos. She will play Monterey Jazz Festival in September 2017.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTubern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Douyé Trio: A Night of Jazz
description:
Los Angeles-based singer Douyé brings her trio to the Poppy to celebrate her first album of jazz standards, Daddy Said So. After two well-received R&B releases, the Nigerian-born vocalist goes back to her roots with an evening of classics inspired by legends such as Ella Fitzgerald, Billie Holiday, and Dinah Washington.rn rnFeaturing:rnTBArn rnHer upbeat R&B track “Life Is Good” made it to #9 on the UK soul chart, but Douyé, born in Lagos, Nigeria, is right at home as a jazz singer. Daddy Said So is packed with heavyweights, including bassist Ron Carter (“Nature Boy”), pianist Kenny Barron (“Mood Indigo” and “I Loves You Porgy”), guitarist Russell Malone (“Body And Soul”), as well as pianist-arranger John Beasley, saxophonist Bob Sheppard, bassist John Clayton, and drummer Roy McCurdy (“Round Midnight” and “In A Sentimental Mood”).rnrnLearn more: Website | YouTubern rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Fula Brothers: Multilayered African Roots & Blues
description:
Fula Brothers is the high-spirited meeting of three seasoned touring performers, each of whom has spent decades pursuing the shared heartbeat in music from around the globe. All three musicians creatively weave a history filled with intercontinental collaborations–from West Africa and Scotland to Haiti and the U.S.–into a rich tapestry of sound and rhythm. The result? An ecstatic and intricate groove-based dialogue which the heart (and the feet) cannot resist.rn rnFeaturing:rnMamadou Sidibe - Kamale NgonirnWalter Strauss - guitarrnKendrick Freeman - percussionrnrnLearn more: Websitern rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Michael Zilber: Trane, Wayne, and Other Originals
description:
On the heels of a celebrated new album, acclaimed saxophonist/composer Michael Zilber brings in some of his favorite musicians (and some of the Bay's very best) for an evening of modern acoustic jazz steeped in the tradition of John Coltrane and Wayne Shorter but firmly in the present, too.rnrnFeaturing:rnMichael Zilber - saxophonesrnPeter Horvath - pianornPeter Barshay - bassrnJason Lewis - drumsrnrnBay Area-based saxophonist and composer Michael Zilber has been described by NEA Jazz Master David Liebman as “one of the best players and composers around anywhere. Period!” The Canadian-born ex-New Yorker has 11 albums to his name as a leader or co-leader, and he has performed or recorded with everyone, from Liebman and Miroslav Vitous to Mike Clark, Dizzy Gillespie, and Bob Berg. His current project, Originals for the Originals, was hailed by Jazz DJ Brad Stone as "one of the premier releases of all time on Origin Records.”rnrnLearn more: Websitern rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Rumberos de Radio Habana: Monthly Community Rumba
description:
Rumba music originated in the Afro-Cuban neighborhoods of Havana and Matanzas as the soundtrack to community, partnered dancing and get-togethers as a means of cultural expression. Its global diaspora means rumba rhythms have found their way into other genres of music such as country, rock, and blues, and continue to impact the genres of today. Rumberos de Radio Habana are bringing their own contribution to the Red Poppy for a free afternoon of beat-making, hip-shaking, and good company.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Honey Whiskey Trio: Folk, Bluegrass, and Melody
description:
The Honey Whiskey Trio celebrates the release of their new album, Rye Woman, by exploring harmony in folk, bluegrass, and melody on a Friday night at the Poppy. With body percussion, haunting melodies, and powerful yet sweet harmonies, this show promises to be a special one. rn rnFeaturing:rnCourtney Politano - vocals, mandolin, body percussionrnAnn Louise Thaiss - vocals, guitar, body percussionrnChristina Wilson - vocals, banjo, body percussionrn rnIn 2013, after singing together for only five months, Honey Whiskey Trio won the Harmony Sweepstakes National Competition, also winning Audience Favorite at both the regional and national sweepstakes. They have gone on to headline the Los Angeles A Cappella Festival, the Women’s A Cappella Association‘s SheSings Festival, and the South Eastern Minnesota A Cappella Festival. They have been featured artists at the FAR-West Folk Festival, the Rogue Valley Roots Festival, the Long Beach Folk Revival Festival, and the Shedd Institute for the Arts. rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Cellista/Joshua Icban/Ransom Dance: “Testimony”
description: “Testimony,” a reprise of Cellista’s performance art piece “Wants,” confronts issues of identity, loss, displacement, and acceptance within the Bay Area arts community, is an avant-garde response to the experiences of artists during these uncertain, sour times. The evening features sound design and performance by Cellista, choreography by Lilith Ransom of Ransom Dance, and the ambient music of composer and guitarist Joshua Icban.rn rnFeaturing:rnCellista - cello, live scorernLilith Ransom - choreography, dancingrnJoshua Icban - guitarrn rnCellista is an acclaimed American cellist and the artistic director of the for-profit performing arts company Juxtapositions. She is known for her multidisciplinary installations, which involve collaborations with artists across various media and across a range of genres including pop, hip-hop, and classical. Her live performances incorporate elements of classical music, theater, improvisation, and visual art, sometimes in unconventional spaces.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | BandcamprnrnJoshua Icban, winner of Vallejo High’s Louis Armstrong Award for Excellence in Jazz, earned an associate’s degree in music at Solano Community College under the mentorship of local music great Delbert Bump. He has performed with numerous music projects, including the Savages, the Jazz Cartel, and Blood Sweat and Funk. Both of his recent groups, Joey Ramone and the Dynamics, perform around the Bay Area.rnrnLilith Ransom grew up training and choreographing with the Santa Cruz Ballet Theater, moving on to study with the RDA/P Craft of Choreography Conference, Pacific Northwest Ballet, American Ballet Theater, and LINES Ballet. After a career that included work with the Nashville Ballet, Los Angeles Ballet, and the Missouri Contemporary Ballet, she began teaching youth at the Presser Performing Arts Center in Mexico, Missouri, as well as choreographing for the Missouri Contemporary Ballet and her students. She now teaches and choreographs with Kid

title: Matt Munisteri w/ special guest Rob Reich: Virtuosic Guitar, Accordion & Piano
description:
Guitarist, singer, and songwriter Matt Munisteri brings Red Poppy favorite Rob Reich back to the Poppy for an evening of accordion, piano, guitar, and vocals. Whether singing his own songs, presenting his unearthed and discarded treasures of the American Songbook, or simply playing guitar, Munisteri will demonstrate a distinctly pre-rock sensibility that allows for emotional directness without the gauzy veil of retro-chic.rn rnFeaturing:rnMatt Munisteri - guitar, vocalsrnRob Reich - accordion, pianorn rnCalled one of “New York’s finest vintage guitar stylists” by Downbeat magazine, Matt Munisteri is a Brooklyn native who has worked with singer Catherine Russell, violinist Mark O’Connor, and Steven Bernstein’s Millennial Territory Orchestra over the last few years. Besides collaborating with a long list of notables such as Matt Glaser, Vince Giordano, Jenny Scheinman, Bucky Pizzarelli, Dick Hyman, Frank Vignola, Loudon Wainwright, Holly Cole, Madeline Peyroux, Diana Krall, “Little” Jimmy Scott, and Geoff Muldaur, Munisteri has played and sung on a wide range of movie soundtracks, television shows, and commercials.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTubern rn rnEVENT DETAILS

description:
New York-based singer, composer, and violinist Rini comes to the Poppy with her cadre of international musicians, who draw inspiration from Indian classical music, electronica, art rock, and jazz, presenting groovy, pulsing music.rn rnFeaturing:rnRini - vocals, violin, electronicsrnAleif Hamdan - guitarrnAchal Murthy - bassrnYogev Gabay - drumsrnÍñigo Galdeano Lasheras - saxophone, ewirn rnHarini “Rini” Raghavan has recorded and performed with Grammy-winning composer A.R. Rahman, headlined festivals in the U.S. and India, and performed as a soloist and core member of the Berklee Indian Ensemble. The South Indian musician’s two albums, Kural and Alone in the Blue, have garnered critical acclaim, including a feature in Rolling Stone India. title: Renewal: Artist Reception w/ Mimi Herrera-Pease
description: The Red Poppy invites you to join the artist and curators of Renewal, its third exhibition of 2017, which will be on view until August 21. For her first solo exhibition at the Red Poppy Art House, Mimi Herrera-Pease picks up the tempo with spring and summer shades on canvas. Come by and feel the harmony between the sonic and visual arts!rn rnABOUT THE ARTIST:rnMimi Herrera-Pease is a San Francisco-based painter who was born in Mexico City. Under the tutelage of the late Carlos Villa, a Guggenheim Fellow, Herrera-Pease learned technique and self-expression through oil painting at the San Francisco Art Institute. The immediacy of action painting and abstract expressionism became her vocation. Influenced by the New York School of Abstract Expressionism, Bay Area Figurative, and Neo-Expressionist movements, she honed her skills as an abstract painter.rn rnABOUT THE CURATORS:rnElena Mencarelli moved to San Francisco after concluding her Master Degree in Visual Arts at the University of Bologna, Italy. Previously, she was the artistic director of the Make Your Mark Art Gallery in Melbourne, Australia. In addition to her artistic practice and curatorial background, she is also engaged in art criticism. Elena is the co-writer of the catalogue of the double exhibition Maria Rebecca Ballestra – Alan Sonfist, a better landscape (Unimedia Modern Contemporary Art, Genova, Italy) and is the author of Maria Rebecca Ballestra, a phenomenology of posthumanism, soon to be published in both Italian and English.rn rnZena Carlota is a multi-instrumentalist, singer, and visual artist based in the Bay Area. Her traditional background in kora and West African harp has inspired her to combine her love of classical West African string music with Appalachian folk, Delta blues, and soul to create narratives of memory and migration in the African Diaspora. An accomplished visual artist, Zena finds it a natural progression to apply in her own art the same themes of ritual and tradition found in

title: Hafez Modirzadeh/Mark Dresser/Diane Moser/Vijay Anderson: Creative Music Quartet
description:
The Red Poppy is proud to present an evening of works by four masterful composer/improvisers, each of whom has developed original concepts towards creative music, informed through a lifetime of exploration and reaching beyond an array of musical traditions.rn rnFeaturing:rnHafez Modirzadeh - saxophonesrnMark Dresser - bassrnDiane Moser - pianornVijay Anderson - drumsrn rnMark Dresser is a Grammy-nominated, internationally renowned bass player, improviser, composer, and interdisciplinary collaborator.rnrnDiane Moser has been a featured performer and composer throughout the U.S. with jazz ensembles, big bands, orchestras, chamber music ensembles, dance companies, and theater companies.rnrnVijay Anderson is a freelance drummer, bandleader, composer, and teacher who lives and works in Oakland, California.rnrnSaxophonist/theorist Hafez Modirzadeh has performed, recorded, published and lectured internationally on original cross-cultural musical concepts.rn rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Trio Colibrí: Classic & Original Latin American Music
description: Trio Colibri is comprised of John Santos, Jose Roberto Hernandez, and Manuel Constancio, three stalwart Bay Area musicians drawn together by a common passion for the lyrical and poetic music of Latin America. Using guitars, Afro-Latin percussion, and beautiful vocal arrangements, they transport audiences to other countries, realms, and eras – always with an element of originality – while honoring profound musical traditions and innovators. Mambo, bolero, son, joropo, and guaracha are some of the rhythms they use, igniting dancers and daring concert audiences to stay still in their seats.rn rnFeaturing:rnJose Roberto Hernandez - vocals, guitarrnManuel Constancio - vocals, guitarrnJohn Santos - percussionrn rnJose Roberto Hernandez is a highly respected multi-instrumentalist, singer, composer, arranger, producer, musical educator, and ethnomusicologist. He was born in the city of Cardenas, Tabasco, Mexico, surrounded by master folklorists. He is a unique artist whose devotion, creativity, and studies in the genres of traditional music from Mexico, Venezuela, Colombia, Bolivia, Peru, Cuba, Puerto Rico, Brazil, and Argentina have earned him great recognition and fan appreciation.rnrnLearn more: WebsiternrnBay Area native Manuel Constancio Jr. is an accomplished and versatile guitarist/vocalist whose 25-year association with the Navy Band found him directing the NATO Band in Naples, Italy; on Treasure Island, in San Francisco; and in San Diego, California. He was the Navy's first "minority" band director. He co-directs the band Sabrosito with his brother José.rnrnJohn Santos, a multi-Grammy nominee and San Francisco Latino Heritage Arts Awardee, is one of the foremost bandleaders, composers, producers, percussionists, and educators in Afro-Latin music. He has worked with acknowledged masters, including Tito Puente, Dizzy Gillespie, Max Roach, Cachao, Eddie Palmieri, and Omar Sosa. He has lectured and taught throughout the United States, Europe, and Latin America since 19

title: Eileen Torrez/Maya Hall/Dyllan Hersey: Acoustic Night
description:
Local singer/songwriter/guitarists Eileen Torrez, Maya Hall, and Dyllan Hersey present a night of soulful, folky acoustic originals.rn rnFeaturing:rnEileen Torrez - guitar, vocalsrnMaya Hall - guitar, vocalsrnDyllan Hersey - guitar, vocalsrn rnEileen Torrez performs a powerful and unexpected blend of folk-pop and neo-soul. Her music builds on themes of resilience, community, anti-capitalism, and women's empowerment.rnrnVocalist Maya Hall, born in Fukuoka, Japan, moved to San Francisco in 2014 in search of bigger musical pastures. The self-taught guitarist/songwriter’s chord progressions, dainty riffs, and interesting rhythms are a perfect match for her silky, sweet vocal style.rnrnLearn more: WebsiternrnFairfax native Dyllan Hersey released her first full-length album, Dyllan Hersey, in 2012. The Bay Bridged called it “a compelling album with just a single voice and guitar… Her guitar work is more intricate than typical folk-singer strumming, yet avoids taking attention away from the beautiful simplicity of the arrangements.”rnrnLearn more: Facebookrn rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Brian Andres & the Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel ft. Alexa Morales: Latin Jazz
description:
Grammy-winning singer Alexa Morales joins Brian Andres and his Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel for a night of original compositions and classic Latin jazz standards.rn rnFeaturing:rnTBArn rnThe Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel, rooted firmly in a Bay Area Latin jazz tradition that includes artists from Cal Tjader to Armando Peraza and John Santos to Wayne Wallace, was formed to contribute its own mark to that storied history. Led by Brian Andres and featuring Grammy Award-winning musicians, as well as esteemed music educators, the ACJC never fails to perform its music at the highest level.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnSince 2004, Oakland-based songwriter Alexa Weber Morales has made 12 studio recordings, including three solo albums, Jazzmérica, Vagabundeo, and I Wanna Work For You, and a solo EP, From Me To Yule. In 2010, her vocals graced the Grammy-nominated Latin jazz album Bien! Bien! In 2012, she was commissioned to write lyrics and sing on Pacific Mambo Orchestra’s debut release, which won the Grammy for Best Tropical Latin Album, marking a historic triumph for the Bay Area Latin music scene and for indie musicians everywhere.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rn rnEVENT DETAILS

title: Amanda Abizaid: Bridging East and West With Song
description:
Los Angeles-based singer, songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist Amanda Abizaid makes her Red Poppy debut with an evening of sophisticated pop, lyrics for peace and equality, touches of her Lebanese heritage, and a special appearance by San Francisco pop band Anna Karney and Beautiful Losers.rnrnFeaturing:rnAmanda Abizaid - acoustic guitar, flute, vocalsrnLindsay Gillis - acoustic guitarrnKim Manolius - bassrnJeff Herrera - drumsrnrnLebanese-American artist Amanda Abizaid, best known as the vocalist on the theme song "A Place In Time" from sci-fi show The 4400, released her latest EP, Walking In Twos, in support of HelpPhilippineSchools.org, a nonprofit organization that helps children get the best education possible through the betterment of existing schools. The project’s goal is to create a self- sufficient revenue stream using proceeds from the song's downloads and streams to buy school supplies for the Aeta children of the Philippines.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTube | Bandcamprn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

description:

Having played together in a variety of punk, blues, and rock bands, they decided to put together an act that could play a sultry love song as well as tear through a two-minute surf rocker. Shortly thereafter, Tim Eggerding joined on bass, and ever since, they've been doing their part to keep romance alive and well.rnrnLearn more: Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Molly Holm and Her Trio: Extended Jazz & Improvised Music
description:
Vocalist/composer Molly Holm, with her all-star jazz trio, makes her Red Poppy debut exploring the multicultural crossroads of improvised music through original compositions, straight-ahead and modal jazz tunes, and traditional folk songs. Molly's music, often intertwined with the elements of North Indian raga, experimental vocals, or playful free-form improvisation, also reveals the rhythmic and note-bending influences inherited from the music of the African diaspora.rnrnFeaturing:rnFrank Martin - piano/arrangementsrnJeff Chambers - bassrnDeszon Claiborne - drumsrnrnMolly Holm is an unconventional singer/composer who blends jazz, improvisation, and North Indian raga, reflecting her studies with master vocalist Pandit Pran Nath. Singing with Bobby McFerrin's original Voicestra, Molly has also performed with composer Terry Riley, tabla virtuoso Zakir Hussain, choreographer June Watanabe, and George Coates Performance Works. Other projects include collaborations with African-American roots-music vocalist Linda Tillery and Indo-fusion saxophonist George Brooks. Molly has also composed music for Marcus Gardley’s plays, most notably “This World in a Woman’s Hands.” Her 2013 CD, Permission, described as “musically left-leaning,” was produced by pianist/arranger Frank Martin and features Famoudou Don Moye, Jeff Chambers, Larry Schneider, Deszon Claiborne, and Wayne Wallace.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: David James's GPS: Jazzy Border Crossings
description:
David James (Afrofunk Experience, Beth Custer Ensemble) brings his rhythm-horns-and-strings sextet back to the Poppy for an evening of stylistic border-crossing. This performance will feature songs from the acclaimed album Billionaire Blues, as well as new compositions and arrangements.rnrnFeaturing:rnDavid James - guitar, voicernBeth Custer - clarinetsrnAlan Williams - trombonernEla Polak - violarnLisa Mezzacappa - bassrnJohn Hanes - drumsrnrnGPS (Good People, Son) is a jazz-meets-chamber rock sextet led by celebrated guitarist and composer David James and featuring local luminaries Beth Custer, Alan Williams, Lisa Mezzacappa, Ela Polak, and John Hanes. A lifelong Bay Area resident, David has been involved with seminal local groups for decades, sculpting guitar feedback and ambient sounds with Thessalonians, touring and recording hip-hop with Spearhead and the Coup, playing Afrobeat with Afrofunk Experience, and presenting funky art-song with the Beth Custer Ensemble, all of which has led to GPS, whose 2016 album, Billionaire Blues, was highlighted as an outstanding debut by NPR.rnrnLearn more: Websitern

title: Jazz Mafia's NOT a Brass Band: Chamber Jazz/Groove
description:
Bandleader Adam Theis and this five-piece ensemble will present a night of new original compositions and arrangements, as well as tributes to artists who have influenced them. Expect a wide dynamic range, rock and EDM rhythms, plus melodies, harmony, and phrasing more common to chamber, jazz, and music from around the world – and a hint of New Orleans second line that occasionally surfaces for a wild horn romp.rnrnFeaturing:rnMax Miller-Loran - trumpet/piano rnCharlie Gurke - baritone sax rnAdam Theis - trombone rnJonathan Seiberlich - tuba rnAaron Kierbel - drums/percussionrnrnAdam Theis & NOT a Brass Band, the latest offshoot of the 17-year-old Jazz Mafia collective, was formed by five musicians with extensive experience in New Orleans-style brass groups who wanted to take the instrumentation into new and unexplored territories.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Naomi Wachira: Afro-Folk
description:
Kenyan singer-songwriter/guitarist Naomi Wachira comes to the Red Poppy from her home in Seattle for an evening of folk music influenced by gospel, the music of Tracy Chapman and Miriam Makeba, and a desire to create music that is poignant, hopeful, and life-giving. Expect brand-new songs from her latest release, Song of Lament.rnrnFeaturing:rnNaomi Wachira - vocals, acoustic guitarrnrnSince her arrival on the Northwest music scene, Naomi Wachira has received numerous accolades, including being named “Best Folk Artist” by Seattle Weekly. She has released three recordings to date: African Girl (2012), Naomi Wachira (2014), and I am Because You Are (2015). Her fourth album, Song of Lament, is scheduled for release in June 2017.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTube | SoundCloudrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Kylland/Cinnamon Lips: Cinematic, Soulful Singer-Songwriters
description:
Kylland and Cinnamon Lips present a night of all-original music that promises to take listeners from the green knolls of Ireland to the golden hills of Laurel Canyon. These two singer-songwriters blend flavors of traditional folk music with harmonically rich songwriting and cinematic storytelling, beautifully buoyed in the velvety sea of their strong vocals.rnrnFeaturing:rnKylland - vocalsrnLila Oleszkiewicz - vocalsrnrnKylland is an American-Norwegian-Japanese composer, singer-songwriter, and multi-instrumentalist currently based in Oakland. She will be releasing her debut album in late 2017.rnrnLearn more: Facebook | YouTubernrnLila Oleszkiewicz is a Polish/Roma singer, songwriter, and composer who has lived in England, Ireland, Australia, Chicago, New York City, and the Bay Area. A recent graduate of Mills College with a BA in composition, she focuses on themes derived from Eastern European folklore, as well as American popular music. Cinnamon Lips is a project created around Lila’s love for American folk traditions.rn rn

title: Mari Black & the World Fiddle Ensemble: A Fiery Fiddle Odyssey
description:
Multistyle violinist and champion fiddler Mari Black comes to the Poppy with a spirited musical adventure featuring dance music from around the globe: Celtic, American, and Canadian fiddling, jazz, tango, klezmer, folk, original works, and more.rnrnFeaturing:rnTBArnrnMari Black’s energetic playing, engaging stage presence, and commitment to bringing people together through music have made her a favorite with audiences across the country and around the world. Equally at home in a wide variety of musical traditions, Mari is a two-time U.S. National Scottish Fiddle Champion, Glenfiddich Fiddle Champion of Scotland, two-time Canadian Maritime Fiddle Champion, Canadian Novelty Fiddle Champion, and 1st Prize winner at the 2010 American Protégé International String Competition. rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

Her trio includes Santana bassist/musical director Benny Rietveld, who produced her debut album, Someone Else, which features Cindy Blackman, Ronnie Foster, and Rietveld.rnrnFeaturing:rnAlicyn Yaffee - guitar, vocalsrnHamir Atwal - drumsrnJeff Cressman - trombonernrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | SoundCloud | YouTubern rn

title: Redwood Tango Ensemble: Modern Tango and Original Chamber Music
description:
Oakland-based Redwood Tango Ensemble presents a concert of new works written by bandoneonist Charles Gorczynski. The sextet focuses on writing that is both fundamentally based in tango and reaching towards a personal and imaginative sound. The new works are influenced by contemporary tango groups in Argentina, modern chamber music, film music, and improvised music. rnrnFeaturing:rnCharles Gorczynski - bandoneónrnKendra Vernon - violinrnMia Bella D'Augelli - violinrnAnton Estaniel - cellornElyse Weakley - pianornDaniel Fabricant - double bassrnrnRedwood Tango Ensemble is dedicated to performing and studying traditional Argentine tango music, which has made them popular with dance communities in the Bay Area and on tour. Their arrangements are inspired by the styles of Anibal Troilo, Carlos DiSarli, Osvaldo Pugliese, and other master orchestras in tango. The group has been a consistent and strong presence on dance floors, playing for hundreds of milongas since their inception in 2011. The sextet, the featured orchestra for Chicago Tango Week in 2013 and 2015, has performed for milongas in San Francisco, Los Angeles, Portland, Seattle, Chicago, Montreal, NYC, and dozens of cities in-between. The ensemble keeps a highly active presence in the Bay Area tango scene, and tours regularly as the full sextet or as the Redwood Tango Trio. They have released two albums, including the critically acclaimed and fully original Unison (2016).rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Ricardo Peixoto and Felipe Senna: An Evening of Brazilian Music
description:
Bay Area-based Brazilian guitarist Ricardo Peixoto welcomes São Paulo pianist/arranger/composer Felipe Senna for an evening of original compositions, as well as selections of Brazilian classics, including choro, samba, baião, and bossa nova.rnrnFeaturing:rnRicardo Peixoto - guitarrnFelipe Senna - pianornrnOriginally from Rio de Janeiro, guitarist/composer Ricardo Peixoto is among the top representatives of Brazilian guitar in the U.S. today. His fluid, melodic style, original harmonic approach, and keen compositional sense explore Brazil’s rich and diverse traditions, both in his original work as well as in arrangements of Brazilian classics. His recording and performance credits include Claudia Villela, Flora Purim and Airto, Bud Shank, Dom Um Romão, Sivuca, Claudio Roditi, Toots Thielemans, Dori Caymmi, Guinga, Arturo Sandoval, Spok, Jovino Santos Neto, Paul MCCandless, and Terra Sul. He has performed throughout the U.S., Europe, Canada, Japan, and Brazil.rnrnFelipe Senna was born in São Paulo, where he began his musical education at the age of 6. Surrounded by music lovers and amateur players in his German-Brazilian family, Senna was the first to follow the path professionally, initiating his career as a pianist at age 14. Senna has performed as music director, arranger, and pianist for many artists, including Toquinho, Claudete Soares, Jane Duboc, Airto Moreira, Nailor Proveta, and Filó Machado. He is the winner of many composition awards, including the prestigious Camargo Guarniere Composition Competition, and he has composed for and conducted various symphonic and chamber groups in Brazil.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

description:
Bay Area performance troupe Opera on the Spot celebrates intense and passionate love, the sublime, the morbid, the beautiful, and the strange with a show dedicated to art songs and arias from the late 18th-century/early 19th-century Romantic era of Western classical music.rnrnFeaturing:rnTBArnrnOpera on the Spot is a group of young, classically trained singers embarking on professional careers in opera. With no set venue, they choose less-conventional places for performances, including bars, restaurants, and community spaces.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Girl Named Nino: Indie, Soul, and Jazz
description:

For her Red Poppy debut, Nino will be joined by frequent collaborator Pablo Gordy on guitar and vocals.rnrnFeaturing:rnNino Arobelidze - vocals, guitar, keysrnPablo Gordy - electric guitar, vocalsrn(Special guests TBA)rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTubern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: STOOP: Nosotros, los de a pie
description:
“Nosotros, los de a pie”rnHomenajeando a los ciudadanos de a pie, a los ambulantes, los invisibles a la sociedad. Al inmigrante europeo, latino, africano, a ellos y ellas, los ciudadanos del mundo, sosten del planeta. ¡A quiene nadie les canta, ni dedica poemas, a quienes ningún pintor inspira!rnrn“We, The Ordinary Ones”rnWe honor the ordinary citizens, the street people, those invisible to society. We honor the citizens of the world—from Europeans to Latinos to Africans—and the immigrants who sustain the planet. We honor those who do not inspire painters, and to whom no one dedicates a song or a poem.rn rnABOUT STOOPrnMission Stoop Fest (STOOP) is a civil society arts experiment that transpires on the front stoops of local residences, where traditional and contemporary artists perform and where local residents share stories and histories. The front stoop represents a literal threshold between the public and private spheres and is a historic site of spontaneous community interaction and exchange. Occurring during the day on the same bi-monthly dates as sister-project, Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP), STOOP will provide a program and a map of multiple neighborhood stoops where performance and storytelling will unfold simultaneously.rn rnPARTICIPANTSrnAlejandro Meza - collaborative drawing and poetryrnClaudio Talavera-Ballon - live street painting of Mission residentsrnNaomi Quiñoez - bilingual poetryrn(Plus Lotería family game!)rnrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS
rn rnTime: Event 12:00PMrnAdmission: FreernLocation: 1018A Shotwell StreetrnAll ages are welcome.rn

title: MAPP: Mission Arts & Performance Project
description:
Launched in 2003, the Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP) is a homegrown bi-monthly, multidisciplinary, intercultural happening that takes place in the Mission District of San Francisco. On the first Saturday of every even-numbered month, MAPP transforms ordinary spaces – such as private garages, gardens, living rooms, studios, street corners, and small businesses – into pop-up performance and exhibition sites for a day of artistic and cultural exchange.rnrnRED POPPY SCHEDULErnStay tuned for details!rnrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: RHQuartet ft. Richard Howell: This Is What Love Sounds Like
description:
Returning to the roots of improvisation, saxophonist Richard Howell reunites with fellow improvisers from the acclaimed group RHQ, which featured phenomenal pianist Frederick Harris and spirited bassist Gary Brown. Elé Howell, a disciple of the great E.W. Wainwright, brings an energetic flow and a powerhouse approach.rnrnDrawing upon the ancestry of jazz, the essence of soul, and the sensibility of pop, award-winning saxophonist Richard Howell and his group RHQuartet push the boundaries of the genre while honoring the roots of the music. Howell honed his craft performing alongside artists like Etta James, Chaka Khan, and Maurice White, and his latest CD, Burkina (with guest Christian Scott), reflects the passion and joy Howell brings to his music.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Tom Griesser 3: Embracing Tradition and Sedition
description:
Saxophonist Tom Griesser and his longtime collaborators, bassist Ollie Dudek and drummer Dan Foltz, present selections performed during their three-year stint at the Burritt Room in San Francisco, as well as new compositions to honor visionaries and rebels in a variety of endeavors. The trio draws from a wide range of influences, including 1930s Benny Goodman, 1940s Ellingtonia, the Sonny Rollins Trio recordings of the 1950s, hard bop classics, and avant-garde figures such as Odean Pope and Roswell Rudd.rnrnFeaturing:rnTom Griesser - tenor saxophonernOllie Dudek - bassrnDan Foltz - drumsrnrnSaxophonist Tom Griesser has been performing on the San Francisco scene for 25 years, starting with the swing revival of the 1990s and branching out with a straight-ahead jazz quintet, the Section 8 Project. Since then, he has been a longtime member of both the Marcus Shelby Jazz Orchestra and Brass Monkey Brass Band, recording several albums with both ensembles. In addition to his own trio, Tom plays in B3B4, a jazz organ quartet, and frequently plays both tenor saxophone and clarinet with the Cottontails, as well as other early jazz and swing groups in the Bay Area.rnrnLearn more: Website | FacebookrnPhoto credit: Eric Voglerrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Ali Dineen & Hannah Sassoon: Resistance & Healing in Shadow Puppetry & Song
description:
New York City-based musicians and visual artists Ali Dineen and Hannah Sassoon present an evening of music and shadow puppetry, telling personal and historical tales of healing and resistance. They will perform original music, including new songs from Ali's recent album Light Comes In, as well as selections from different folk music traditions with guitar, accordion, cello, fiddle, vocal harmony, and harmonium.rnrnFeaturing:rnAli Dineen - vocals, guitar, accordion, harmoniumrnHannah Sassoon - vocals, cello, fiddlernrnAli Dineen is a singer, multi-instrumentalist, and visual artist based in Brooklyn. She performs original, acoustic folk music, as well as songs from U.S. folk traditions, and she incorporates historical and sociopolitical commentary into her work. Her music, deeply personal and also political, explores the intersections of personal stories with larger histories. In her work, Ali navigates the forces that divide human beings and cause conflict, and she seeks to dismantle them through honest lyricism and intimate acoustic music. She has worked with Bread and Puppet Theater as puppeteer and musician, and performs regularly in NYC.rnrnHannah Sassoon is a visual artist, multi-instrumentalist, and environmentalist resistor living in Nashville, TN. Ali met Hannah in 2014 and has collaborated with her closely ever since.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | Bandcamprn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Rumberos de Radio Habana: Monthly Community Rumba
description:
Rumba music originated in the Afro-Cuban neighborhoods of Havana and Matanzas as the soundtrack to community, partnered dancing and get-togethers as a means of cultural expression. Its global diaspora means rumba rhythms have found their way into other genres of music such as country, rock, and blues, and continue to impact the genres of today. Rumberos de Radio Habana are bringing their own contribution to the Red Poppy for a free afternoon of beat-making, hip-shaking, and good company.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS
rn rnTime: Event 2:00PMrnAdmission: Free; donations accepted for musicians ($5-10)rnAll ages are welcome.rn

description:
Best known for being co-frontman of The Head and The Heart, Josiah Johnson heads an evening of songwriting based in a folk tradition that emphasizes connection to the internal source while recognizing the importance of communion and the connection to the light of others.rnrnFeaturing: rn(Full lineup TBA)rnrnSouthern California native, current Bay Area resident, former Seattleite, one-time Torontonian, and longtime tour-bus resident Josiah Johnson’s solo performances highlight a stripped-down, emotive, lyrics-first kind of songcraft, inimitable in sincerity.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Pamela Parker's Fantastic Machine: Rock Funk Soul Love
description:
Standout Bay Area producer/songwriter/guitarist/vocalist Pamela Parker brings her trio to the Poppy for a dynamic performance that will feature selections from her upcoming album, Fantastic Machine, a fresh rock & roll landscape that evokes Alabama Shakes, Thin Lizzy, and Funkadelic.rnrnFeaturing:rnPamela Parker - vocals, piano, guitarrnSpence Murray - bassrnChris McGrew - drumsrnrnPamela Parker studied opera and commercial music production before establishing herself as a solo performer in the Washington D.C. area. After touring nationally and internationally, she moved to San Francisco in 2007 and released her self-produced debut, Beautiful Day. Parker and her handpicked band completed her second album, Evolutionary Process, while she earned studio time working as an engineer at Hyde Street Studios in 2013. The band's infectious blend of funk, rock, and soul touched a nerve in Vancouver, British Columbia on the radio station CHLY 101.7FM, where the album debuted at No. 1 and was up for Album of the Year. Fantastic Machine has opened for acts such as Heart and Big Brother & the Holding Company and headlined festivals like the Haight-Ashbury Street Fair, Earth Day SF, and Bay Vibes Summer Fest. rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTubern rn

title: Dina Maccabee/Ron Shalom: Viola Ballads & Baroque Death Pop
description:
Dina Maccabee performs lush, ethereal music using viola, vocals, and electronic processing. Taking an experimental approach to folk sensibilities, she crafts a singular, intimate sound world around each of her original and often personal songs. Ron Shalom presents songs from the peripheries of consciousness utilizing electronics, video, and storytelling.rnrnFeaturing:rnDina Maccabee - viola, vocals, laptoprnRon Shalom - guitar, vocalsrnrnDina Maccabee is a multifaceted string player, songwriter, composer, and performer who was deeply involved in the Bay Area live music scene for many years. Best known locally for her work with Ramon & Jessica and Real Vocal String Quartet, she has recently toured extensively with Julia Holter, while putting creative energy into imagining her solo set. Her viola playing and vocals can be heard on records by Feist, Vetiver, Beats Antique, We Are the West, Vienna Teng, Silian Rail, Michael Musika, Aaron Novik, and many more.rnrnRon Shalom is a composer-instrumentalist and leader of the Cult of the Illuminated Orifice. His work attempts to seek out the boundaries between forms of performance in public life, as well as between the musical and the theatrical, through the creation of spectacle. One of his recent projects includes a third full-length album of songs with his baroque death pop ensemble.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Three Poets and Their Music: LOAN, Contraband, and Fried Axolotl
description:
Music, poetry, history, and future converge powerfully when three poets–Tongo Eisen-Martin, Bonnie Kwong, and Josiah Luis Alderete–explore themes of immigration, migration, language, cultural identity, social justice, revolution, and love through collaborations with guitarists Peck the Town Crier, Oliver Mok, and Eli Carlton-Pearson.rnrnFeaturing:rnLOAN (Tongo Eisen-Martin, poetry; Peck the Town Crier, guitar)rnContraband (Bonnie Kwong, poetry; Oliver Mok, guitar) rnFried Axolotl (Josiah Luis Alderete, poetry; Eli Carlton-Pearson, guitar)rnrnTongo Eisen-Martin is a movement worker, educator, and poet who has organized against mass incarceration and extra judicial killing of black people throughout the United States. His latest curriculum, We Charge Genocide Again, has been used as an educational and organizing tool throughout the country.rnrnPeck The Town Crier has been recording and performing “hist-hop” and “song-rap” since 1994. His music has been featured on MTV UK, Current TV, and the Wall Street Journal online. Peck teaches guitar in San Rafael and can often be found crying a capella on the corner of 16th and Mission.rnrnBonnie Wai-Lee Kwong is an artist in many mediums and languages: Cantonese, English, Mandarin, Japanese, Ruby, and Javascript. Her first book of poetry, ravel, is a finalist for New Rivers Press’ Many Voices Project and the White Pine Press Poetry Prize. Her work has garnered a number of Pushcart nominations and an artist residency at Stanford University.rnrnJosiah Luis Alderete is a full-blooded Pocho Indio who refries his beans and poems in Spanglish. He was a founding member of outspoken word group the Molotov Mouths and is currently living on the border of Oakland and Berkeley waiting for Quetzalcoatl to arrive.rnrnEli Carlton-Pearson is an instrumentalist extraordinaire, musical visionary, and inventor of many strange musical objects.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Édessa: The Heart of Balkan Music
description:
Often-rambunctious Balkan quartet Édessa, known for playing intricate time signatures and highly ornamented modal music with authority and joy, brings to the Poppy a night of wide-ranging, non-dance repertoire and new material.rnrnFeaturing:rnGeorge Chittenden – clarinet, gaida, zurna, guitarrnAri Langer – violinrnLise Liepman – santouri, accordionrnSean Tergis – davul, darabuka, defirnrnUsing both traditional and modern instruments, Édessa plays a variety of styles that interweave melodies and improvisation with a big Balkan beat, including the ancient and mesmerizing music of the zurna and davul; the lyrical and sweet music of the Greek islands; the haunting, trance-like pentatonic music of Epirus; and — at the other end of the acoustic spectrum — the contemporary Bulgarian genre known simply as "wedding music."rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Naina & Salma: In Search of Eternal Love
description:
Performance artist Naina Shastri and visual artist Salma Arastu collaborate on a show that uses Indian classical dance, poetry, video projection, and live music to connect 13th-century Persian mystic Mevlana Rumi and 16th-century Indian saint Meera Bai, who both spoke of finding salvation in love. rnrnFeaturing:rnNaina Shastri - dancernSalma Arastu - Rumi poetry recitationrnGhalib Grooves Carnatic Band - musicrnrnWith over 20 years of experience as a performer, choreographer, and teacher of the Mysore style of Bharatanatyam dance, Naina Shastri runs Ushanjali School of Dance in the East Bay. Naina, a RAW residency artist at SAFEhouse and artist-in-residence at OACC, was chosen to perform at the APERTURE Festival in 2016.rnrnEast Bay visual artist Salma Arastu practices Eastern spirituality through Western techniques of painting using mixed media. She’s had over 30 years of experience, 40 solo shows, and three books published, and she’s a recipient of the East Bay Community Foundation's Fund for Artists award and the City of Berkeley Individual Artists grant.rnrnPhoto credit: Swagato Basumalickrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Darren Johnston's Broken Shadows: Love, Loss, and Resistance
description:
With songs emphasizing lyrics and storytelling, as well as world-class musicianship that prizes spontaneity and dialogue, this highly adaptable ensemble led by trumpeter/composer Darren Johnston brings poetry, interplay, vocal harmonies, and a cross-section of musical interests into a curious new blend of roots/art/dance music.rnrnFeaturing:rnDarren Johnston - trumpet, vocalsrnDavid Phillips - pedals, steel guitar, vocalsrnJordan Samuels - guitar, vocalsrnMiles Wick - upright bass, vocalsrnJordan Glenn - drumsrnrnSince settling in San Francisco in 1997, Canada-born trumpeter/composer/songwriter Darren Johnston has collaborated and recorded with an extremely diverse cross-section of artists. His interests rotate around composing instrumental music, writing songs, and performing all styles of jazz, experimental, and purely improvised music, as well as traditional music of the Balkans, Greece, Macedonia, Turkey, and the Arab world. These interests have coalesced into his primary ensemble of late, the Americana-influenced Broken Shadows. He has performed and/or recorded with luminaries such as Fred Frith, ROVA Sax Quartet, Myra Melford, Ben Goldberg, Matt Wilson, Mark Dresser, Marshall Allen, Hamid Drake, Marcus Shelby, the Electric Squeezebox Orchestra, and many others.rnrnLearn more: Website | FacebookrnPhoto credit: Julie Cainern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: San Francisco Youth Theatre Presents: Homegrown One Act Festival
description:
Written, directed, acted, and produced by San Francisco Youth Theatre students, Homegrown is a brilliant collection of original, one-act plays that tells stories of having an incarcerated parent, Black Lives Matter, the beauty standard, LGBT relationships, being an artist, and yes, our changing San Francisco. This project is supported by The Youth Empowerment Fund.rnrnPlaywrights:rnAbraham Frankfurter DavisrnYasmina EldredgernKenyah EvansrnMateo FriedenbachrnLauren HernandezrnXitlally LupianrnCamila RaimondrnDaniel VigilrnrnSan Francisco Youth Theatre is dedicated to creating cutting-edge theater for children and youth ages 3-24. SFYT offers classes and performance ensembles, and produces adult-performed productions for young audiences, school residencies, and a repertoire of original productions that speak to the unique sensibilities of youth. The organization fosters personal expression through the arts, creating and performing theater that tells the compelling stories of our community. Classes are accessible to all children and youth, and all classes are offered on a sliding scale tuition basis; no student is turned away for lack of funds. SFYT is changing the world, one play at a time.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: John Wubbenhorst and Facing East: India Meets Jazz
description:
Led by bansuri flutist John Wubbenhorst, a composer influenced by decades of studying classical Indian music, Facing East combines Hindustani and Carnatic sounds with modal jazz in the vein of icons such as Miles Davis, John Coltrane, Weather Report, John McLaughlin, and Oregon. rnrnFeaturing:rnJohn Wubbenhorst - flutes, keyboards, vocalsrnEric “Doc” Smith - Zendrum rnSteve McQuarry - keyboards rnJason Everett - 7-string bassrnrnThe compositions of John Wubbenhorst, a disciple of the great Indian bansuri flute maestro Hariprasad Chaurasia, have been heralded as a "fresh new approach to East/West music." His illustrious list of collaborators has included Victor Wooten, Jack DeJohnette, and Paul Horn. rnrnLearn more: Facebook | YouTubern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Kathleen Grace: American Song
description:
Ready for authentic storytelling, original compositions, and selections from the Great American Songbook? Globetrotting Arizona native and Los Angeles resident Kathleen Grace brings a pure sound and her honest and soaring voice to the Poppy for an evening of jazz, folk, and Americana.rnrnFeaturing:rnKathleen Grace - vocalsrnStorm Nilsson - guitarrnJeff Denson - bassrnrnKathleen Grace’s latest album, No Place To Fall, is a sun-drenched landscape of music rooted in her desert upbringing and elegant country spirit. Recorded at Carriage House Studios and produced by Sheldon Gomberg (Ryan Adams, Ben Harper, Lucinda Williams), No Place To Fall is the undeniable evolution of Grace as a naturally exquisite singer and songwriter, whose soulful instincts are skillfully honed by her prior experience in jazz and blues.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTube | Bandcamprn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Bret Higgins' Atlas Revolt: Cinematic Jazz, Global Sounds
description:
This Canadian quartet explores instrumental music that draws inspiration largely from film scores, jazz, rock, surf, and elements of Eastern European, African, and South American music. At the Poppy, the only San Francisco stop on their first U.S. tour, Atlas Revolt promises music from their acclaimed debut alongside selections from their upcoming album, due this year.rnrnFeaturing:rnBret Higgins - upright bassrnAleksandar Gajic - violinrnTom Juhas - electric guitarrnRobbie Grunwald - electric pianornMax Senitt - drumsrnrnUpright bassist Bret Higgins formed Atlas Revolt in 2013 as a vehicle for his original instrumental compositions, and the band’s self-titled debut, released on John Zorn’s Tzadik Records in 2015, has been met with glowing reviews. Based in Toronto, Higgins performs regularly with Canadian folk-rock groups Great Lake Swimmers, Beyond The Pale, Zebrina, and Ozere.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | Bandcamprn rn

title: McKasson & McDonald: A Modern Take on Traditional Folk
description:
The Poppy warmly welcomes Ryan McKasson and Eric McDonald, masters of tradition who purposefully explore the dark corners floating on its edges. Pulling from parallel strands of influence, the duo creates a swirling level of intensity that traverses darkness, light, and everywhere in between. Their new album focuses on the relatability of music hundreds of years old, weaving new ideas and tunes with the old to create a timeless sound.rnrnFeaturing:rnRyan McKasson - fiddle, vocalsrnEric McDonald - acoustic & electric guitar, vocalsrnrnDespite living active musical lives thousands of miles apart, Ryan McKasson and Eric McDonald are committed to working together to forge a new musical path. Ryan, an acclaimed performer, composer, collaborator, and teacher, was the youngest ever to win the U.S. National Scottish Fiddle Championship. Eric, who studied at Berklee College of Music under world-renowned musicians such as guitarist/mandolinist John McGann and cellist Eugene Friesene, is now considered one of the United States’ premier accompanists.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Kristine Barrett: Songs of Sanctuary
description:
Kristine Barrett presents an evening of experimental folk melodies exploring the concept of refuge: a place that nurtures, extends, bridges; that asks us to appeal to our highest selves, to seek deeply the difficult questions and to be brave in the face of them. Her setlist: traditional music and ritual song from Norway, Iran, Estonia, Ireland, Sephardic Spain, Hungary, Brazil, and the Republic of Georgia.rnrnFeaturing:rnKristine Barrett - vocals, harmonium, pianornDavid Robert Johnson - guitarrnTemple of Light Georgian Community Choir - vocalsrnrnKristine Barrett is a transmedia artist, composer, and vocalist specializing in traditional music and ritual song from the Republic of Georgia, the Caucasus, Scandinavia, the Baltics, Eastern Europe, Ireland, and the Americas. Balanced between conservation and experimentation, Barrett acts as both monk and medium, honoring tradition while concurrently allowing the music to be alive in present bodies, reflecting the shape of the living. In addition to her solo work, she currently performs with the acclaimed Kitka Women’s Vocal Ensemble, Cantadora Sephardia with David Robert Johnson, and is the Artistic Director of the Temple of Light Georgian Community Choir.rnrnLearn more: Website | Bandcamprn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Spectrums & Prisms, Bodies & Realms: Artist Reception w/ Lise Silva
description:
The Poppy invites you to join the artist and curators behind its second exhibition of 2017, Spectrums & Prisms, Bodies & Realms, on view until June 24. For her first exhibition at the Red Poppy Art House, Lise Silva Gomes has created a fiber art installation that serves as a metaphysical exploration where woven knots of various shapes and colors invade the Red Poppy's performative space, creating an ambiance of other-worldliness that evokes wonder, deep mystery, possibility, fantasy, and curiosity.rnrnABOUT THE ARTIST:rnLise Silva lives in Oakland and works from her studio in Berkeley. Her artistic practice is strongly influenced by meditation, mysticism, classic film, psychedelic illustration, Surrealism, and Art Deco design. She explores the power of symbols through her Sacred Knots line of fiber jewelry and wall hangings.rnrnABOUT THE CURATORS:rnMusician, composer, and visual artist Zena Carlota is the executive artistic director of the Red Poppy; Elena Mencarelli, a visual artist, is currently collaborating with the Poppy as a lead host and art curator. Spectrums & Prisms, Bodies & Realms is the result of Lise, Elena, and Zena working together to realize the multidisciplinary vision of the Red Poppy Art House, a dynamic space where visual and performing arts, cultural diversity, and creativity come together to enrich community experience.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

description:
Jazz vocalist/composer/arranger Andrea Claburn will be joined by an all-star band to celebrate the release of her debut, Nightshade, a collection of original compositions and new arrangements of lesser-known standards by Duke Ellington, Betty Carter, and Pat Metheny.rnrnFeaturing:rnAndrea Claburn - vocalsrnDan Zemelman - pianornAaron Germain - bassrnGreg Wyser-Pratt - drumsrnTerrence Brewer - guitarrnErik Jekabson - trumpet, flugelhornrnrnSan Francisco-based Andrea Claburn, born in Baltimore and raised in the classical tradition, studied both piano and violin before turning to jazz. She is a graduate of the California Jazz Conservatory and a recipient of the CJC's Mark Murphy Vocal Scholarship.rnrnLearn more: Website | SoundCloudrn rn

title: The Poppies: Pop-Up Shop
description: Join artists Jason Wyman, Donna Marchesano, Andrea Fuenzalida, Ginger Snap, Keegan Roberson, Steve Piasecki, and senseofplace LAB for a special one day only pop-up shop. It's the perfect spot to check out fine art works and handcrafted merchandise. Pick up something for yourself or a loved one. Items for sale include metal prints, Voodoo Kitties, photographic magnets, paintings, coloring zines, Psychogeographic Art 'Kerchiefs, and more! You can even order a custom neon pop art portrait and participate in an art project. Entry is free and donations are accepted. All donations will be split evenly among the artists! All ages are welcome too!rnrnThe Poppies is a part of Wyman's Artist-in-Residency, A Feast of Communities, at the Red Poppy Art House. Over 2017, Wyman, in community with Zena Carlota and Shree Shyam Das, will be hosting otherworldly dinner parties that engage diverse communities in San Francisco—queers and gender rebels, born and bred Mission District residents, and international migrants who work at tech companies. The purpose of A Feast of Communities is to foster relationships, conjure magic, and share stories. Crystal Mason and Stella Adelman join Wyman, Carlota, and Das as co-hosts of individual dinners to ensure each one has its own flavor and aesthetic rooted in the communities identified by the core team.rnrnA Feast of Communities is rooted in the Red Poppy Art House’s core practices of facilitating socially-engaged professional development, producing vigorous and multidisciplinary programming, playing matchmaker among friends and strangers, and honoring that which is slow. Another root comes from Wyman’s approach of cultivating circle-based models of creation and exchange that foster understanding, empathy, and complexity across age, identity, culture, and expression. Together, this unique residency will weave a tapestry of talents, traditions, and tales representing a community of communities.rnrnAs part of Wyman's residency, the Red Poppy Art H

title: Kelly/Szemela/Muñoz Trio: Cello, Violin & Percussion Improvisations
description:
A trio of acclaimed Bay Area musicians makes its Red Poppy debut with an evening of looped and plugged-in improvisations. Expect daring solo, duo, and trio hijinks, as well as originals and reimagined covers.rnrnFeaturing:rnAlex Kelly - cellornMatthew Szemela - violinrnPC Muñoz - drums, percussionrnrnDr. Alex Kelly is a San Francisco-based cellist, composer, and educator. He composes and performs with major symphony orchestras, chamber orchestras, new music ensembles, string quartets, cello quartets, big bands, jazz combos, rock bands, world music ensembles, theater companies, radio productions, ballet companies, modern dance companies, and circus troupes.rnrnPraised by the New York Times for his “outrageous fiddling,” violinist Matthew Szemela crosses genres with ease. He has performed in venues as diverse as Carnegie Hall and the Montreux Jazz Festival, and recorded and shared the stage with artists ranging from Jay-Z and Sting to Savion Glover and Placido Domingo.rnrnSan Francisco recording artist/producer/drummer PC Muñoz's projects are stylistically broad and deep, revealing a sonic explorer equally at home with contemporary classical, funk, hip-hop, folk, poetry, pure improv, art installations, and modern dance productions.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Jason Yeager Trio: Chamber Jazz Inspired by Latin, Classical & Literature
description:
New York-based pianist Jason Yeager returns to the Red Poppy for an evening of catchy and quirky melodies inspired by Kurt Vonnegut's fictional characters, re-imagined standards by the likes of Thelonious Monk and Duke Ellington, grooves from Argentina and Brazil, and musings on Messiaen. He will also perform music from his forthcoming album with violin/mandolin virtuoso Jason Anick, as well as music by Billy Strayhorn and Stevie Wonder.rnrnFeaturing:rnJason Yeager - pianornDoug Stewart - bassrnMike Quigg - drumsrnrnPianist/composer and Inner Circle Music recording artist Jason Yeager's music is expressive and multifaceted, incorporating the blues, 20th-century classical harmonic language, Latin folkloric rhythms, and more. A faculty member in the piano department at Berklee College of Music and an honors graduate of the Tufts University/New England Conservatory Double Degree Program, Yeager was awarded the Hot House Jazz Fans Decision 2014 Awards for best pianist and best organist.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTube | SoundCloudrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Minsky Duo: Exploring the Music of Kapustin and Prokofiev
description:
Minsky Duo makes its Red Poppy debut with an all-Russian/Soviet program that includes the first San Francisco performance of living composer Nikolai Kapustin’s rollicking “Sonata for Violin and Piano,” Prokofiev’s “Violin Sonata No. 1 in F minor” (described by the composer as "wind whistling through a graveyard"), Tchaikovsky's beautifully lyric “Meditation,” Scriabin's explosive “Piano Sonata No. 4,” and Schnittke’s recently-discovered “Fuga for Solo Violin.”rnrnFeaturing:rnNato - violinrnMonica Chew - pianornrnMonica Chew is a Bay Area pianist who prefers chamber music to concerti, Haydn to Mozart, and lately, Bartok above all things. She produces solo and collaborative concerts with a strong narrative arc.rnrnViolinist Nathanael Bartley (Nato) made his solo orchestral debut at five and was awarded a career scholarship and the loan of a Nicolo Amati violin at the age of nine. He went on to win prizes at the International Menuhin Competition for Young Violinists, the Orchestre Symphonique de Montréal’s Standard Life Competition (1990, 1992, 1994), the International Beethoven Competition, and the International J.S. Bach Competition. He was a laureate of the 2012 Canada Council of the Arts Musical Instrument Bank Competition.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: The Muted Jewel Tones & Anne Carol Mitchell: Folk, Jazz, and Three-Part Harmony
description:
Guitarist/singer Anne Carol Mitchell and violinist Nissa Gustafson perform beautiful melodies that invite listeners to reconnect with the natural world. The Muted Jewel Tones, a vocal harmony trio, perform songs from the sophisticated to the ridiculous, bringing a little jazz, a little folk, a little pop–and hopefully, a little smile–to the audience.rnrnFeaturing:rnSandy Lindop - guitar, ukulele, vocalsrnRaena Frohlich - guitar, ukulele, vocalsrnRose Koron - ukulele, vocalsrnAnne Carol Mitchell - guitar, vocalsrnNissa Gustafson - violinrnrnAnne Carol Mitchell, a nature-based composer and collaborator with filmmakers working for social change, has worked extensively with LGBTQ poet Judy Grahn. In addition to her own work as a nationally touring songwriter, Anne has toured in an opening ensemble for Ani DiFranco. rnrnThree-part vocal harmony group the Muted Jewel Tones creates songs with an old-timey feel reminiscent of the Andrews Sisters. The trio of Bay Area natives, all alumni of the Ruth Asawa San Francisco School of the Arts, have been performing together since 2015.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Janam: Balkan & Appalachian Groove
description:
Combining funky Balkan rhythms, gritty Appalachian ballads, arresting vocal harmonies, and enchanting acoustic textures, Janam unites some of the Bay Area's most playful and imaginative devotees of southern Balkan and American roots music to weave together folk tunes and their own colorful compositions, taking their audiences on a labyrinthine musical ride.rnrnFeaturing:rnDan Auvil - percussion, vocalsrnTom Farris - laouto, guitarrnJuliana Graffagna - vocals, accordionrnGari Hegedus - oud, saz, mandocello, mandolinrnShira Kammen - violin, viola, vocalsrnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | SoundCloudrn rn

title: The Jimmy Grant Ensemble: The Music of Django Reinhardt
description:
The Jimmy Grant Ensemble infuses swing-era pieces, originals, and traditional Roma tunes with Django Reinhardt’s crunchy rhythms and hot harmonies, creating a signature mix that includes Russian folk, bluegrass, Celtic, and classical music while remaining true to Django’s innovative guitar technique.rnrnFeaturing:rnJimmy Grant - guitarrnJavi Jiménez - guitarrnSascha Jacobsen - bassrnrnNorthern California native Jimmy Grant has performed with artists such as David Grisman, Gonzalo Bergara, Ludovic Beier, Andreas Oberg, Tim Kliphuis, Paulus Schafer, Robin Nolan, Trio Dinicu, Rhythm Future Quartet, and the Hot Club of San Francisco. Jimmy, who has recorded two albums under his own name, was a finalist in the 2016 John Lennon Songwriting Contest for his original song “Insomnia.”rnrnLearn more: Website | YouTubern rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: MAPP: N.E.W. Experiences
description: The next edition of MAPP, on April 1, highlights the work of an international collective of producing artists who will be featured in the New Experimental Works Festival at CounterPulse on May 4-7th, 2017. The N.E.W. Festival, dedicated to radical theater-making and performance, is part of a collaborative effort between the California Institute of Integral Studies and the University of Chichester in West Sussex, England. The mission of the festival is to break traditional norms for both audience and performers, and to introduce groundbreaking live art to San Francisco audiences while consistently redefining what it means to be N.E.W. (For more information, go to www.thenewfestival.com.)rn rnOn April 1, MAPP’s schedule of experimental live art and innovative collaboration begins at 7:15pm and ends at 10pm. Seating is on a first-come, first-served basis, and space is limited.rn rnABOUT THE MAPP:rnLaunched in 2003, the Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP) is a homegrown bi-monthly, multidisciplinary, intercultural happening that takes place in the Mission District of San Francisco. On the first Saturday of every even-numbered month, MAPP transforms ordinary spaces – such as private garages, gardens, living rooms, studios, street corners, and small businesses – into pop-up performance and exhibition sites for a day of artistic and cultural exchange.rn rnRED POPPY SCHEDULE:rn7:15pm: A RETROSPECTIVErnA collection of one-to-one performances that is partially scripted; how you should go about crafting your experience is up to you. Featuring Marie Marcovic and collaborators Jeffrey Hayes, Xiaotian Liu, Nina, and Alice Phipps.rnrn7:45pm: They Say Love is Blind(folded)rnAn interactive performance event that’s similar to a blind date. Featuring Mariah Castle in collaboration with Matthew Marcum.rnrn8:00pm: Cabaret Olga: Songs from the Sister, and One Soldier, Who Were Left BehindrnFeaturing Beth Wilmurt and Rob Avila.rnrn8:15pm: The Ocean is a Chance We Take: A Vocal Poem

title: STOOP: "Our Spring" in the Mission
description:
"Our Spring" in the Mission: Through music, art, and song, we reflect traditions of protest and resilience inspired by the popular demonstrations in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya, Syria, Yemen, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Jordan between 2010 and 2013.rn rnABOUT STOOP:rnMission Stoop Fest (STOOP) is a civil society arts experiment that transpires on the front stoops of local residences, where traditional and contemporary artists perform and where local residents share stories and histories. The front stoop represents a literal threshold between the public and private spheres and is a historic site of spontaneous community interaction and exchange. Occurring during the day on the same bi-monthly dates as sister-project, Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP), STOOP will provide a program and a map of multiple neighborhood stoops where performance and storytelling will unfold simultaneously.rn rnSCHEDULE & LOCATIONS:rnSTOOP 1 @ 901 Alabama St (Curator: Jasmine Douville)rn12:00pm: Mission Drumline (Brazilian Dance & Drumming)rn12:00-3:00pm: Boys & Girls Club (Live Music)rn1:00-3:00pm: Nuestra primavera en el barrio: (Grafitti Art Installation)rnrnrnSTOOP 2 @ 3338 25th St (Curator: Bianca Catalan)rn Schedule TBArnrnSTOOP 3 @ 2754 Harrison St (Curator: Windsong)rn12:00pm: Family Art (Music & Art with Local Community)rn1:00pm: Raíz de Aguarn2:00pm: Singing to Our Spring: Kata of LoCurarn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Gari & Faisal: Middle Eastern Melody & Rhythm
description:
Multi-string player Gari Hegedus and Arabic percussionist Faisal Zedan return to the Poppy with a concert of original compositions by Hegedus and traditional instrumental music from various regions in the Middle East. Their focus: Dialogue and interplay between melody and rhythm.rn rnGari Hegedus began devoting his life to music more than 25 years ago with the study of early European, Celtic, and Bretagne music. From there, he was led east into the intense practice and performance of Turkish classical and Mevlevi ceremonial music. His repertoire and playing styles span outward from Turkey to Greece and the Arab lands, as well as to Iran and India. He plays the oud, violin, and saz, and has expressed his talent on many other instruments, including mandocello, sarod, and setar. As a composer and musician, Gari utilizes maqam, the various Eastern modal systems, and has developed the art of improvisation (taqsim) to a soulful level for which he has become highly recognized.rnrnBorn in Lebanon and raised in Syria, Faisal Zedan grew up impassioned with the derbakki (Arabic drum). At the age of 15, after intensive study with a local drummer, Faisal became exposed to a wide repertoire of Arabic classical and contemporary music. In addition to his collaborations with local and international artists, Faisal has taught at music camps, conducted workshops around the world, and produced instructional DVDs on derbakki fundamentals and Arabic percussion. He created the percussion group Ajyal to serve as a music platform for making Arabic and Middle Eastern music.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

description:

Their setlist is comprised of standards with new arrangements and original tunes by the band, all featuring pianist/composer/vocalist Mike Greensill. rnrnFeaturing:rnMike Greensill - piano, vocalsrnCharlie McCarthy - saxophonernJoe Cohen - alto saxophonernJohn Clark - bassrnJack Dorsey - drumsrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Rumberos de Radio Habana: Monthly Community Rumba
description:
Rumba music originated in the Afro-Cuban neighborhoods of Havana and Matanzas as the soundtrack to community, partnered dancing and get-togethers as a means of cultural expression. Its global diaspora means rumba rhythms have found their way into other genres of music such as country, rock, and blues, and continue to impact the genres of today. Rumberos de Radio Habana are bringing their own contribution to the Red Poppy for a free afternoon of beat-making, hip-shaking, and good company.rn rnrnEVENT DETAILS
rn rnTime: Event 2:00PMrnAdmission: Free; donations accepted for musicians ($5-10)rnAll ages are welcome.rn

title: Barrio Manouche & Mallar Bhattacharya Band: A Musical Journey from India to Spain
description:
The Red Poppy is proud to present an evening with Spanish guitarist/composer Javier Jimenez’s Barrio Manouche, purveyors of original, instrumental jazz manouche, and the Mallar Bhattacharya Band, a Bay Area-based traditional North Indian ensemble. Each band will perform a set and then play together, merging both cultures to become one.rnrnFeaturing:rnJavier Jiménez - guitarrnAlex Zelnick - guitarrnLuis Jiménez - cajónrnMagali Sans-Cartier - violinrnCyril Guiraud - soprano saxophonernSascha Jacobsen - bassrnMarcos Rodrigues - percussionrnrnMallar Bhattacharya - sarodernNilan Chaudhuri - tablasrnSatish Pillai - guitar, pianorn rnAcoustic, San Francisco-based international septet Barrio Manouche is deeply influenced by a range of musical traditions, from the swing of Louis Armstrong and Sidney Bechet and the hot jazz of Django Reinhardt and Stéphane Grappelli to the flamenco of Paco de Lucía and the avant-garde sounds of Miles Davis, Ornette Coleman, and John Coltrane. rnrnMallar Bhattacharya, a disciple of sarode legend Aashish Khan of the Senia Maihar Gharan, plays the sarode, a 25-stringed lute that is a major instrument of the North Indian classical style of solo performance. Mallar teaches and performs in the Bay Area and, in recent years, has been drawn to the sounds and rhythms of global music, including collaborations with guitarist/pianist Satish Pillai and percussionist Nilan Chaudhuri.rn rn

title: Masha Campagne & Ian Faquini: Intimate Brazilian Encounters
description:
Vocalist, songwriter, and producer Masha Campagne once again collaborates with young guitar star Ian Faquini to explore the treasure trove of Brazilian music. Expect their reverent tributes to Tom Jobim, Ivan Lins, and Guinga–as well as their own original compositions–to transport you to a sensuous dreamland rich with quiet fire and deep soul.rnrnFeaturing: rnMasha Campagne - vocals, melodica, percussionrnIan Faquini - acoustic guitarrnHarvey Wainapel - reedsrnBrian Rice - percussionrnrnWith two internationally acclaimed CDs to her credit and performances at legendary venues such Yoshi’s, Kuumbwa, Monterey Jazz, and Zinc Bar, Masha Campagne is considered one of most lauded new voices in San Francisco. Her gorgeous interpretations of soulful Brazilian bossas effectively convey the blend of happiness and intimacy of this music.rnrnIan Faquini, a veteran of Berkeley High’s renowned jazz program, became an apprentice to the great Brazilian guitarist/composer Guinga at age 16. His 2016 debut, Metal na Madeira (“Metal on Wood”), showcases his rare chordal technique and singular compositional skills while reflecting the exuberant yet wistful aspects of music from the northeast part of Brazil.rnrnLearn more: Websitern rn

title: Mean To Me: Jazz & Soul, Old & New
description:
Bay Area-based quintet Mean To Me resurrects classic sounds of the 1920s through 1940s–sometimes including the modern stylings of Thelonious Monk, Charles Mingus, and Ahmad Jamal–and a wide variety of soul- and Latin-influenced jazz. rnrnThe three core members of Mean to Me are Judy Butterfield, trained as a cabaret singer but whose love is for soul music; Ben Slater, a pianist who became interested in exploring jazz's roots after living in New Orleans; and Dave Shaff, a trumpeter drawn to more modern and funky sounds. All three grew up in San Francisco and have played together on and off for ten years.rnrnLearn more: Website | SoundCloudrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: Kat Morgan: Songs for Farewell and So Long
description:
Drawing from folk and R&B traditions, Kat Morgan sings slow and powerful, metaphor-laced melodies about loving and living. Her Red Poppy debut promises to conjure moods from defiant to melancholic, from soppy sweet to broken and blue. rnrnFeaturing:rnKat Morgan - vocals, guitar, keyboardsrnAlena Coons - bass rnLien Do - drumsrnCaitlin Ross - vocalsrnrnKat Morgan’s soulful, honeyed voice has been compared to Amelia Meath of Sylvan Esso or Sharon Van Etten, and she has released two albums, Homegrown and Kam, both produced by Los Angeles-based artist Boas. Morgan, who has performed throughout the U.S. and in South America, has been featured on KPFA's Full Circle and BFF Radio's Astral Projection Radio Hour.rnrnLearn more: Website | Facebookrn rnrnEVENT DETAILS

title: MoonCandy: Live Soulful House Music
description:
In the spirit of the black church, jazz, the Freedom Singers, and disco sensation Sylvester, MoonCandy creates an environment for survival music to live and thrive through live performance. The eight-piece ensemble, which features five of the East Bay’s most underrated jazz, soul, blues, and gospel singers, specializes in inspirational lyrics and transfixing grooves, giving birth to a “house church” where everyone is welcome to dance for love.rnrnFeaturing:rnScott Keller - drumsrnShree Shyam Das - bassrnLamar Green - keysrnValerie Troutt - vocalsrnSolas B. Lalgee - vocalsrnShamont Hussey - vocalsrnPaulynn Brown - vocalsrnRashida Chase - vocalsrnrnMoonCandy, on the Bay Area music scene since 2010, has an illustrious performance history that includes Oakland Pride, Yerba Buena Gardens Festival, Malcolm X Jazz Fest, the Laurel Street Fair, the Oakland Museum, and the People Party. Their most reflective work has been as the musical soundtrack for Nicole Klaymoon's "Embodiment Project," providing original music documenting the struggle of African-Americans, systemic racism, and sexism in the United States. Their 2016 EP, The Struggle is Real: Walk Together Children, is available on iTunes, Amazon, and at cdbaby.com.rn rn

title: Colm Ó Riain: Irish Fission (Double Show!)
description:
Irish-born violinist Colm Ó Riain and some stellar musical friends combine the music of Ireland with rhythms and melodies from all over the musical world – jazz, blues, Roma, India, Cuba, Brazil, and much more.rnrnFeaturing:rnColm Ó Riain - violinrnJoe Kyle Jr - bassrnGeoff van Lienden - guitarrnWade Peterson - percussionrnrnAn accomplished and fiery improviser in many genres, as well as a composer for multiple modern dance companies, Colm has enthralled audiences on stages as diverse as CBGB's in New York and Davies Symphony Hall in San Francisco. About.com called his 2016 album, Bridge Across the Blue, co-produced with poet Pireeni Sundaralingam, "one of the Top 7 recordings of poetry and music of all time."rnrnLearn more: Websitern

title: Yale Women’s Slavic Chorus: Traditional Women’s Choral Music from Eastern Europe
description:
The Yale Slavic Chorus was founded in 1969, the first year that women were admitted to Yale University. Since then, the “Slavs,” as they are affectionately known on campus, have been sharing Slavic and Eastern European music with the Yale and New Haven community, as well as communities across the country and internationally. Their recent international tours have included Bulgaria and Georgia.rnrnFeaturing:rnAmanda Crego-Emley - vocalsrnCharlotte Finegold - vocalsrnClaire Gottsegen - vocalsrnAthena Wheaton - vocalsrnKimberly Lai - vocalsrnJola Pach - vocalsrnKelly Fu - vocalsrnOlivia Noble - vocalsrnJana Lohrova - vocalsrnAnna McNeil - vocalsrnNadia Grisaru - vocalsrnFiona Kurylowicz - vocalsrnrnLearn more: Website | Facebook | YouTubern rnrnEVENT DETAILS
