title: Will Bernard & Beth Custer: Melodic Chamber Jazz Duets
description: Musicians Will Bernard and Beth Custer come together for an intimate evening of groove-filled duets.rnrnGuitarist Will Bernard is a Grammy-nominated bandleader and founding member of all-star Bay Area jazz conglomerate T.J. Kirk, as well as Galactic drummer Stanton Moore’s rollicking trio. Bernard is a virtuosic performer whose bone-deep, groove-heavy playing was a vital part of Peter Apfelbaum’s Hieroglyphics Ensemble and the guitarist’s own funky quartet, Motherbug. Bernard’s latest Posi-Tone release, Just Like Downtown, is his most audacious to date, featuring originals and covers running the gamut from Richard Rogers to Led Zeppelin.rnrnWith a musical palette that seamlessly combines vivid shades of jazz, blues, Afro-Caribbean and contemporary classical music, clarinetist Beth Custer is one of the most vital and productive composers on the Bay Area scene, having recorded over 30 albums with her various ensembles. The New York native first gained attention in the early 1980s composing for Club Foot Orchestra, a San Francisco ensemble known for performing innovative scores for classic silent films. Custer pilots a series of bracing ensembles, including Clarinet Thing, Trance Mission, and Beth Custer Ensemble.rnrnFeaturing:rnWill Bernard - guitarrnBeth Custer - clarinets, vocalsrnrnrnrnAdmission: $20-25. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets. No one will be turned away for lack of funds.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Jacqui Sutton with the Frontier Jazz String Trio: Jazz and Bluegrass Swing Together
description: Imagine if Billie Holiday, Dolly Parton and Aaron Copland came together to create music for a night. This would be the sound of Jacqui Sutton described by music/arts critic C. Michael Bailey as "where jazz meets the American frontier." Drawing on over 20 years of musical and stage experience, Jacqui interweaves jazz and bluegrass with orchestral, pop and Americana influences. In her ears, the banjo, mandolin and fiddle continually intermingled with jazz, eventually coalescing into her distinctive frontier jazz sound. She has recently been featured in Chris Becker's Freedom of Expression: Interviews with Women in Jazz (November 2015), a book discussing the contributions made by women in the jazz world.rnrn*This performance is proudly sponsored by Ifshin Violins.rnrnFeaturing:rnJacqui Sutton - vocalsrnPaul Chester - banjo, guitarrnAnthony Sapp - bassrnMax Dyer - cellorn rnFollow Jacqui on Facebook.rnLearn more about "frontier jazz" here and preview the music here.rn rn"The latest one by Jacqui Sutton, vocalist extraordinaire, is a kind of giant leap forward into a realm of creative Americana. Notes from the Frontier (Toy Blue Typewriter 002) brings together Jacqui's very fully enacted vocals with the unusual instrumentation and sonic mix of her Frontier Jazz Orchestra—including cello, banjo, electric bass, trumpet, trombone, percussion, piano, and so forth. … It's a generally excellent selection of surprises that straddle jazz, roots, country and all kinds of American elements juxtaposed with rather extraordinary arrangements. And a vocal performance by Jacqui that does the same with the vocals that the arrangements do to the instrumental backdrop—she combines ways of singing that extend our heritage and make it very new. It’s the opposite of a ho-hum standards "jazz vocalist" release. It's vivid, exciting and very innovative."rn- G. Edwards, Grego Applegate EdwardsrnrnrnrnAdmission: $20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold i

title: The Mark Growden Quintet: Soulful Chamber Folk
description: For a rare Bay Area performance, Mark Growden and his long-time collaborators bring to the Red Poppy a combination of soulful vocals with elements of chamber, folk, and jazz.rnrnMark Growden is a composer, conductor, multi-instrumentalist and the director/founder of The Calling All Choir. Raised in a small mountain community in Northeast California, his work emanates earthy, soulful and poetic tones. Growden has ten years of experience teaching music in schools and currently leads singing workshops across the country. He has devoted his life to making music for other people and to helping other people make music for themselves.rnrnFeaturing:rnMark Growden - vocals, saxophones, accordion, banjo, sruti box, bicycle handlebarsrnChris Grady - trumpet, cornetrnDaniel Wood - cellornMyles Boisen - guitarrnSchuyler Karr - upright bassrn rn"Rich, haunting and personal song cycles that incorporate avant-garde, rock, cabaret, blues, country, contemporary classical and Tin Pan Alley." - Tucson WeeklyrnrnFollow Mark on Facebook and YouTube.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $20-25. title: Guarandinga: Latin Afro-Pop: Venezuela to Oakland
description:
With a sound described as Latin Afro-Pop, Guarandinga's music is propelled by heavy Afro-Caribbean beats from the central coast of Venezuela. The band hails from the San Francisco Bay, integrating these rhythms with the gritty East Bay funk that runs deep from Oakland to Richmond. Fronted by the dynamic and charismatic singer/percussionist Rowan Jimenez, Guarandinga plays high-energy music that is alluring and infectious.rnrnFeaturing:rnRowan Jimenez - vocals, cuatro, percussionrnJeremy Allen - bass, vocals, cuatro, percussionrnLuis Rodrigues - percussion, vocals

title: Uxía & Narf: A Night of Galician Music
description:
Uxía & Narf unite for the first time to present a joint musical project that features reinterpretations of classics, including poems by Rosalía de Castro and García Lorca, original compositions, and adaptations of traditional alalás, the oldest and best-known form of Galician music. Building bridges across cultures through music, they create their respective repertoires after more than 20 years of traveling and exchanging experiences with artists from Brazil, Angola, Mozambique, Cape Verde, and Guinea-Bissau. The duo unites harmonious vocals and intimate melodies through the shared pursuit of originality and cultural crossroads.rnrnFeaturing:rnUxía Domínguez Senlle - vocals, Galician tambourinernFrancisco Xavier Pérez Vázquez - vocals, acoustic and electric guitarrnrnPreview their music here and here.rn

description: Join MoAD Stories in the exploration of black femininity and empowerment through a short film by a local female filmmaker, followed by an invigorating panel discussion. The journey will bear witness to the history and emerging identities of women artists from MoAD’s current exhibitions, featuring Alison Saar and Kenyatta A.C. title: Allison Au Quartet: Traditional Jazz in Modern Times
description: The Allison Au Quartet is a Toronto-based contemporary jazz ensemble. Since its formation in 2009, the group has performed at major jazz venues across Canada and have earned a JUNO award nomination for their debut release, The Sky Was Pale Blue, Then Grey. The quartet was also awarded the TD Jazz Fellowship Scholarship (2011), The Alfred and Phyllis Balm Scholarship (2012), and The Margareta and Benno Nigg Scholarship (2013) to conduct artist residencies at the acclaimed Banff Centre. Celebrating the release of their latest album, Forest Grove, the quartet marks their debut performance at the Red Poppy.rnrnFeaturing:rnAllison Au ‒ saxophonernTodd Pentney ‒ pianornDan Fortin ‒ bassrnFabio Ragnelli ‒ drumsrn rnLearn more about Allison Au here, and follow her on Facebook.rnDive into her music on Bandcamp, SoundCloud, and YouTube.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. title: The Sherri Roberts / Harvie S Quartet w/ Special Guest Sheryl Bailey: Spring is Just Around the Corner
description:
A rare live performance of two simpatico artists, Sherri Roberts reunites with longtime collaborator bassist Harvie S to preview songs from their upcoming recording ‒ a jazz tribute to springtime ‒ and to revisit material from their earlier acclaimed CDs. Sherri is heavily influenced by the West Coast cool vocal style of the 50’s, and Harvie first cut his musical teeth on the bandstand with countless East Coast jazz heavyweights ‒ most notably in his longstanding duo project with vocal legend, Sheila Jordan. New York guitarist Sheryl Bailey joins the quartet as a special guest to promote her new album with Harvie, Plucky Strum.rnrnFeaturing:rnHarvie S - bassrnSherri Roberts - vocalsrnDavid Udolf - pianornAkira Tana - drumsrnSheryl Bailey - guitarrn rnVocalist Sherri Roberts has been performing in the Bay Area for over 25 years and has toured jazz venues up and down the West Coast, as well as in New York and London. Locally, she surrounds herself with top Bay Area musicians including husband David Udolf, Bliss Rodriguez, Chris Amberger, Jim Zimmerman, and Akira Tana. Her past recordings with Harvie S have featured such luminaries as Phil Woods, Lew Soloff, Chris Potter, Mark Soskin and Danny Gottlieb.rn“Stylistically, Roberts bears a significant resemblance to gin ’n’ satin seductress Julie London. But where London’s voice was distant as a winter moon, Roberts is all dappled sunshine…shimmering…Enchanting” ‒ JazzTimes

rnAward-winning bassist Harvie S is a veteran of the jazz and Latin music scenes. He has recorded and toured with the cream of the crop: Kenny Baron, Dexter Gordon, Stan Getz, John Scofield, James Weidman, Pat Martino, Mike Stern and Paul Motian. His decades-long creative partnership with Sheila Joran redefined bass/vocal performance.rn“The bassist’s horn-like, bebop-based phrasing is as engaging as it is inventive…utilizing blistering runs, double stops and chords, leaving no doubt about his stature as one of the pre-eminent bass voices in jazz today.
excerpt: A rare live performance of two simpatico artists, Sherri Roberts reunites with longtime collaborator bassist Harvie S to preview songs from their upcoming recording ‒ a jazz tribute to springtime ‒ an...

title: Big Strings, Small World: Strings Without Borders - ft. Maria Cleary, Diana Rowan, Winnie Wong & Colm Ó Riain
description: A Welsh harp, an Irish harp, a guzheng and a violin. Four string virtuosos with their hands and feet in multiple worlds come together for an evening of musical exchange. Drawing threads and strings from a rich tapestry of histories and geographies – Chinese, Irish, period, classical, jazz, blues, Middle Eastern, Balkan – Maria Cleary, Diana Rowan, Winnie Wong and Colm Ó Riain, all expert improvisers in their fields, explore our world through music.rnrnFeaturing:rnColm Ó Riain - violinrnWinnie Wong - guzhengrnDiana Rowan - Irish harp, pianornMaria Cleary - Welsh harprnrnHarpist Maria Cleary is noted for her improvisatory skills and ingenious continuo playing, combined with a particular care to creating a beautiful sound on a perilous instrument. Specializing in historical harps, Maria performs on medieval harps, the Italian arpa doppia, the Spanish arpa de dos ordenes, and the 18th century harpe organisée. She promotes a pedal technique that was exclusively used on harps with a single-action pedal mechanism and has developed several innovative tunings for performing medieval and renaissance music. Maria regularly performs with Davide Monti under the name Arparla, a violin and harp duo specializing in repertoire from the 17th to 19th centuries.rnrnBorn in Ireland and raised the child of an Irish diplomat, Diana Rowan has traveled, lived and performed on six continents. As a natural outgrowth of this inspiration, her music interweaves global music, art, spirituality and mythology. Diana is now based in the San Francisco Bay Area and holds a PhD in Music Theory (world harp composition techniques), plus an MM in Piano Performance (classical). She presents rave-review workshops, lectures worldwide, and runs Bright Knowledge Academy, an online college for dedicated musicians.rnrnWinnie Wong began her training on the guzheng, a Chinese plucked zither, in 1982 at the age of four and a half under the expertise of guzheng virtuoso, Ms. Weishan Liu. At the age of five, she had
excerpt: A Welsh harp, an Irish harp, a guzheng and a violin. Four string virtuosos with their hands and feet in multiple worlds come together for an evening of musical exchange. Drawing threads and strings fr...

title: Jazz in the Neighborhood Presents: Guarneri Jazz Quartet
description: In partnership with the Red Poppy Art House, Jazz in the Neighborhood presents the Guarneri Jazz Quartet. Led by trumpeter Mario Guarneri, the concert will feature his own works as well as the music of Thelonious Monk. The Bay Area quartet has been performing together since 2000 and has recorded three CDs, which are available on berp.com. Their first album, From My Heart, has been praised by Herb Alpert for its "wonderful sound, great heart and beautiful ideas."rnrnFeaturing:rnMario Guarneri - trumpetrnRandy Vincent - guitarrnRobb Fisher - bassrnAkira Tana - drumsrn rnAbout Mario Guarneri:rnThe founder of Jazz in the Neighborhood, Mario Guarneri has done everything from playing Dixieland with Louis Armstrong at the age of thirteen to performing in the Los Angeles Philharmonic for fifteen seasons under conductors Zubin Mehta and Carlo Maria Giulini. He was principal trumpet for the Los Angeles Chamber Orchestra for a decade, toured Europe with the Los Angeles Brass Quintet, and has performed with the New York Philharmonic, the San Francisco Symphony, Radio City Music Hall Orchestra, and even the Barnum & Bailey Circus. Mario has taught trumpet for more than twenty-five years, including master classes in Asia and Europe, and is currently an instructor at the San Francisco Conservatory. He performs and records regularly with his jazz quartet in the San Francisco area, and has established a worldwide reputation as a creative musician of the highest level.rnrnAbout Jazz in the Neighborhood:rnJazz in the Neighborhood‘s mission is to celebrate and invigorate the Bay Area’s rich, musical jazz heritage by creating more opportunities for established local musicians to perform, mentor aspiring players, and share this great American art form with the community. Through collaboration with school music programs and adult big bands, the project enables top-caliber artists to push creative boundaries while helping developing musicians “learn on the bandstand” according to the great
excerpt: In partnership with the Red Poppy Art House, Jazz in the Neighborhood presents the Guarneri Jazz Quartet. Led by trumpeter Mario Guarneri, the concert will feature his own works as well as the music o...

title: Rob Reich Quintet: Shadowbox: Accordion-Led Evocative Chamber Jazz
description: Accordion player and pianist Rob Reich is quintessentially what makes the San Francisco music scene a vibrant and vital mecca for independent music; he’s become one of its stalwart underground artists. Shadowbox, the musician's debut album, marks a fully-realized collection of original compositions. The tunes branch off from his work with revered Bay Area ensemble Tin Hat Trio, mixing improvisational romps with refined and melodic ensemble arrangements. Doses of wry humor and elements of spontaneous revelation keep the music characteristically Reich, whose idiosyncratic accordion prowess helps propel the instrument’s modern renaissance.rnrnFeaturing:rnRob Reich ‒ accordion, pianornIla Cantor ‒ guitarrnBen Goldberg ‒ clarinetsrnEric Garland ‒ drumsrnColin McCuen ‒ bassrn rn"Rob Reich has carved out a niche as a premier avant-roots voice." ... "Like a funhouse mirror, the merriment is refracted in odd, distorting ways, as the band keep the tones sharp and slightly askew. Like the best carnival attractions, it scares you just enough to make you want to visit it again." ..."like Nino Rota writing for a small jazz group." ‒ JAZZIZ Magazinernrn"Reich creates soundscapes brimming with half-buried feelings and memories just out of reach."rn‒ Andrew Gilbert, KQEDrnrnFollow Rob Reich on Facebook and YouTube.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: [DOUBLE SHOW] Alex Conde: Descarga for Monk: Latin Jazz Thelonious Monk Tribute
description: Spanish pianist Alex Conde finds his own path to the music of the great jazz musician, Thelonious Monk. Named in "Best of 2015" by DownBeat Magazine and All About Jazz Magazine, Conde's album Descarga for Monk incorporates flamenco, swing and Latin rhythms with unmistakable virtuosity and lyricism.rnrnAfter graduating at the conservatory of music in Valencia, Spain, Alex Conde came to study at Berklee College in Boston with Danilo Perez, Joe Lovano, and George Garzone. He has toured around the globe with flamenco companies and has appeared in various jazz festivals. His compositions for flamenco guitar and orchestra have been premiered in Miami with Jose Luis Rodriquez, Federico Britos and Omid Zoufonoun. Additionally, his arrangements on Schubert have been performed at the Symphony Space in NYC.rnrnFeaturing:rnAlex Conde ‒ pianornJohn Santos ‒ percussionrnJeff Chambers ‒ bassrnrnDescarga for Monk - "[Conde's..] the uprising star taps into Monk’s intense but dreamy lyricism on a flamenco tour de force" ‒ Andrew Gilbert, KQEDrnrnFollow Alex Conde on Facebook and Twitter.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $15-20. First show at 7:00pm, second show at 8:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnrnrn*Additional tickets will be available for purchase at the door at 6:30pm the day of the event.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Tejido Social / Social Fabric: Exposition on the Mexican Rebozo
description: The project Social Fabric (Tejido Social) presents a small exposition about the Mexican rebozo: an emblematic piece that incorporates technique, culture, time, and ancestral knowledge that stay interlocked like a code within woven fibers. Those that make rebozos not only are determined to maintain the technique, but also confront competition against neo-liberal markets, a government that does not support artistic activity and creation, and violence that impacts Mexico today.rnrnThe exposition will have a presentation of different techniques and styles of Mexican rebozos to teach us how to identify its origin and discontinue economic spending on cheap global knock-offs from industrial manufactures.rnrnEntrance is free. Join the event page here!rnrnSchedule:rn1:00-2:30pm: Paper Weaving Workshop (ages 5+)rn2:30-3:00pm: Presentation/Videorn2:00-6:00pm: Mezcal tasting from 5 states producersrnrn

title: Amaranth Quartet: Dichotomies of Love
description: With a love of tackling the string quartet canon and as fierce a passion for discovering newly composed works, Amaranth Quartet was founded in 2013 and proudly calls the Bay Area home. Members Katie von Braun, Abigail Shiman, Erica Zappia, and Helen Newby strive to present classical music to divergent audiences in a variety of locales. Upcoming projects include a residency and performance with the Antelope Valley Symphony Orchestra as well as appearances on Switchboard Presents and SF Presidio Sessions. Amaranth Quartet is fiscally sponsored by the San Francisco Friends of Chamber Music.rnrnFeaturing:rnKatie von Braun (Amaranth) - violinrnAbigail Shiman (Amaranth) - violinrnErica Zappia (Amaranth) - violarnHelen Newby (Amaranth) - cellornDanielle Sampson-­Reutter­-Harrah - mezzo­sopranornrnFollow Amaranth on Facebook and SoundCloud.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: PC Muñoz: SMASH/HITS - Curated by Derek Lassiter
description: After nearly a decade focusing on producing and playing drums for other artists, Bay Area maverick PC Muñoz returns as a songwriter/leader in 2016, with a new album of original songs, Echo, and a new duo with longtime guitarist/collaborator Marc Weibel. The evening will feature two sets of material ranging from angular, instrumental free-jazz deconstructions (SMASH) to stripped-down pop-influenced songs (HITS).rnrnPC Muñoz is a musician and producer based in San Francisco. His body of work includes recordings across multiple mediums, from Grammy-nominated contemporary classical music to CMJ-charting funk songs to modern dance/multimedia productions. A frequent and enthusiastic collaborator, PC has recorded with rock legend Jackson Browne, dream-pop artist Ingrid Chavez, longtime Kronos Quartet cellist Joan Jeanrenaud, Oakland hip-hop artist Kev Choice, and countless other musicians and recording artists across a wide range of genres. He was a 2015 Dresher Ensemble artist-in-residence in Oakland, CA and a 2013 Loft Literary Center Equilibrium Fellow in Minneapolis, MN.rnrnFeaturing:rnPC Muñoz - drums, vocalsrnMarc Weibel - electric guitarrn rnAbout Derek Lassiter:rnLassiter, a mainstay on the San Francisco arts scene, has received accolades for both his recordings and live performances featuring Bay Area notables such as pianist/composer Tammy Hall, producer/percussionist PC Muñoz, and composer/pianist Art Khu. Lassiter is profiled in Julian C. R. Okwu’s book, Face Forward: Young African American Men in a Critical Age.rn rn"PC Muñoz is an artist that knows no boundaries. His output straddles myriad genres, influences and art forms. From funk to classical, pop to hip-hop, experimental to jazz, globally-renowned artists in all these realms consider Muñoz a first-call percussionist, producer, composer, and songwriter. He's also an artist with a deep social conscience -- one who who uses music to connect cultures and communities together." – Anil Prasad, InnerviewsrnrnFo

title: Moy Eng with Bill Murphy and John Stowell: An Exploration of the Great American Songbook
description: Jazz luminary Miles Davis was asked to describe his music and responded quickly with “call it anything.” Describing a performance by vocalist and composer Moy Eng with her long-time duet partner, guitarist Bill Murphy, and with guitarist John Stowell, one could take the expression a step further. “Call it everything” is an apt description of an evening of song with this trio. An exploration of the Great American Songbook intersects classical lyricism and then winds down through the plains of Africa and fields of the blues to Ellington, Jobim, Led Zeppelin, and Hitchcock.rnrnFeaturing:rnMoy Eng - vocalsrnBill Murphy - guitarrnJohn Stowell - guitarrn rnMoy Eng is a singer and lyricist. Known for her “beautiful and soulful voice,” Moy has been fortunate to have performed with many wonderful artists, including Anthony Brown, Brenda Wong Aoki, Mark Izu, Mas Koga, John-Carlos Perea, Jon Jang, Steve Nakano, Paul Eastburn, and Darrell Green. As a lyricist, she principally collaborates with Grammy Award-nominated composer Wayne Wallace. Known for her visionary ability to identify and support progressive ideas, Moy has over three decades of experience in the philanthropic sector as a grantmaker, consultant and senior manager in areas as diverse as arts, education, renewable energy, lesbian and gay rights, immigrant rights, and international human rights.rnrnJazz guitarist Bill Murphy plays throughout the Bay Area, honoring the tradition of solo guitar legend Joe Pass. Bill has studied with master guitarists John Stowell, Tuck Andress and Mimi Fox, and works regularly in duos with vocalist Moy Eng as well as performing solo guitar in both jazz and Celtic styles. Featured annually at Burning Man, his art project Your Very Own Theme Music Written Right Here, Right Now shapes original guitar compositions as a gift to passersby while they talk about their lives, dreams and demons.rnrnJohn Stowell is a jazz guitarist influenced as much by pianists and horn players as he is by guita

title: ARTE by Adrian: Celebrating Dreams, Friends, Poetry, Love - Closing Reception
description:
Bay Area artist Adrian Arias presents his latest mixed media works on paper and canvas with themes related to dreams and metamorphosis. Sunday afternoon of February 7th will be dedicated to Silvia Parra, great friend and "comadre" of Adrian, who left this world only a few weeks ago. Silvia and Adrian worked together in the development of games and psycho-magical rituals, as well as in several video-art and art installations in both San Francisco and Mexico. Programming for the afternoon includes LoCura acustiKo, poems by Adrian, his ceviche-poem, and more.rnrnLearn more about the current exhibition here.rnrnAbout Adrian:rnBased in the San Francisco Bay Area since 2000, Adrian Arias is an American-Peruvian prize-winning poet, visual artist, curator and cultural promoter. His visual art, like his poetry, explores the absurd, the sensuality of elements, and the connection between reality and dreams. Arias has participated in international exhibitions, showing his video-installations and visual poetry objects and drawings in Japan, France, Spain, Italy, Poland, Canada, Chile, Argentina, Brazil, Dominican Republic, Peru, and the US.rnrnrnAdmission: Free. Reception at 1:26pm. All ages welcome.

title: February 6th MAPP: Stories of Revolt
description:
The Red Poppy Art House presents Stories of Revolt, a trans-Bay, interdisciplinary exploration in narratives of belonging and displacement, culturally generative creative practices, and restorative justice. In collaboration with Dance Mission's annual festival, D.I.R.T. - Dance in Revolt(ing) Times, the Red Poppy will feature original works from music, dance, film and storytelling artists that investigate how image, body and language become tools for transformation in a sacred space.rnMAPP Schedule:

rn3:00-4:00pm: From Azonto to Zouk: Afro-Urban Dance Stories with Nkei OruchernPresented by Dance Mission Theater's D.I.R.T. Festival - Featuring a sampler of a few Afro-urban dance styles, Nkei Oruche shares personal narratives as a way to discuss the context of music and dance created by people of African descent in urban settings. (More info below!)rnrn5:00-6:30pm: Classical RevolutionrnChamber Music Sight Reading Sessionrnrn6:30-6:40pm: Anne CarolrnSound Installation - Anne Carol uses simplicity, spaciousness, and beauty to evoke a sense of belonging with her music.rnrn6:45-7:05pm: Valerie Troutt ft. Khalil AnthonyrnOdd Times JazzSoul Musicrnrn7:10-7:40pm: Intricate and Dopey Fresh ft. Amelia RomanornTurf Dancing and Electric Harp Improvisations - Amelia Romano performs fiery world and original works using the electric harp as her vehicle to express new and dynamic perspectives in the changing landscape of her environment.rnrn7:45-8:00pm: AsturnAfro-Electric Soulrnrn8:00-8:10pm: Anne CarolrnSound Installation - Anne Carol uses simplicity, spaciousness, and beauty to evoke a sense of belonging with her music.rnrn8:10-8:30pm: Adrian Arias and Jackie RamosrnLove is Our Revolution: PerfoPoetica - Peeling the layers of love with performative poetry in times of change, metamorphosis and revolution.rnrn8:35-8:45pm: Jason WymanrnThe Seedling, A Coming out Fable / Film: Primordial Projectionsrnrn8:50-9:15pm: Diana GamerosrnSongs of Love, Migration and Identity - Through her

title: Tod Dickow with Charged Particles: A Tribute to Jazz Great Michael Brecker
description: Charged Particles joins tenor saxophonist Tod Dickow in tribute to one of the most influential musicians in the history of jazz, Michael Brecker. This performance marks the San Francisco premiere for the quartet celebrating Michael Brecker the composer, Brecker the saxophonist, and Brecker the bandleader. The group first performed the tribute concert at THE Jazz Club in Cloverdale, CA to a standing-room-only and standing-ovation audience. Each piece will be preceded by a photo display capturing Michael at the time of the song’s premiere along with a few words detailing Michael’s life then, the significance of the song, the meaning of the title, and the relation of the song to various genres of jazz. Listeners will walk away with a deep understanding of the man, his music and its place in the larger idiom, as well as with smiles after a night of passionate musical energy, interaction, and creativity.rnrnFeaturing:rnTod Dickow - tenor saxophonernMurray Low - keyboardsrnAaron Germain -  acoustic & electric bassesrnJon Krosnick - drumsrn rnTod Dickow has been recognized by outstanding soloist awards at Jazz Festivals (Pacific Coast) and was awarded the Outstanding Soloist Award by DownBeat Magazine. He has appeared with numerous celebrated artists in many genres of music, including Ella Fitzgerald, Nancy Wilson, Joe Henderson, Bobby Hutcherson, Jackie & Roy, Harry Connick Jr, Steve Allen, Terry Gibbs, Frank Sinatra Jr., Dave Brubeck, and many more. In addition to leading his own small groups, he leads the Contemporary Jazz Orchestra, a great 16-piece big band featuring top players from around the Bay Area.rnrnCelebrating their 25th anniversary next year, Bay Area-based jazz trio Charged Particles has performed at hundreds of festivals, clubs, and theaters around the world. Jazz News Magazine describes the band as "fresh, energized contemporary jazz that showcases the trio's individual technical mastery as well as a cooperative, refined approach to the art form." title: Janam: Dark Side of Love - Balkan & Appalachian Groove
description: This special reprise of Janam's 2013 Valentine's Day show will explore the shadowy side of love with tormented love songs, murder ballads, and more ‒ a perfect tone for a dark midwinter evening.rnrnIntegrating funky Balkan rhythms, gritty Appalachian ballads, arresting vocal harmonies and enchanting acoustic textures, the band creates an inspiring musical experience. Janam (a term of endearment) unites some of the Bay Area's most playful and imaginative devotees of southern Balkan and American roots music. Weaving together folk tunes and their own colorful compositions, they take their audience on a stirring and labyrinthine ride.rnrnFeaturing:rnDan Auvil ‒ percussion, vocalsrnTom Farris ‒ laouto, guitarrnJuliana Graffagna ‒ vocals, accordionrnGari Hegedus ‒ oud, saz, mandocello, mandolin, violinrn rn“An innovative ensemble that explores a dizzying range of styles, from Appalachia and Asia Minor to Eastern Europe. They play swirling, hard-driving music that always seems to end up in unexpected places.” ‒ San Francisco ChroniclernrnFollow Janam on Facebook.rnDive into their music on YouTube and SoundCloud.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Redwood Tango Ensemble: "Unison" Album Release - Contemporary Tango
description: Celebrating the release of their new album Unison, Redwood Tango Ensemble is joined by the powerful Rafael Delarue / Scott O’Day duo for an opening set of Argentine tango guitar and vocal duets.rnrnRedwood Tango Ensemble is a sextet from Oakland, CA dedicated to both traditional Argentine tango music and original compositions. The ensemble has taken a large step forward with the release of Unison, developing a contemporary and singular voice within the genre. Informed by the creative and wide-reaching contemporary tango composers in Argentina and around the globe, the album includes a striking set of compositions by bandoneonist Charles Gorczynski. The sextet's arrangements are inspired by the styles of Anibal Troilo, Juan D'Arienzo, Carlos DiSarli, Osvaldo Pugliese, and other master orchestras, which has made Redwood popular among many dance communities in the Bay Area and on tour.rn rnFeaturing:rnCharles Gorczynski - bandoneónrnKendra Vernon - violinrnCelia Harris - violinrnAlexander Lazarev - pianornScott O’Day - guitarrnDaniel Fabricant - double bassrnrnFollow Redwood Tango Ensemble on Facebook.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Paul Psarras: Baroque Spanish and Contemporary Guitar
description: An enthusiast of early and contemporary music alike, guitarist/multi-instrumentalist Paul Psarras performs a vast array of genres ranging from classical to Greek folk. In 2012, Paul earned an invitation to the GRAMMY’S with the group El Mundo, and later that year he performed in the San Francisco Opera's Merola program. His previous performances included an appearance with the San Francisco Symphony and at the Fête de la Musique in Switzerland. As a member of the Pacific Guitar Ensemble, Paul played his composition Alkioni for oud and guitars. As a soloist, he appeared with the Vallejo Symphony and the Bear Valley Orchestra performing the Concierto de Aranjuez.rnrnFeaturing:rnPaul Psarras - classical guitarrn rnFollow Paul on Facebook and YouTube.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: BOLO: Global Groove
description: Joining together rhythms and instruments from many lands, BOLO uplifts audiences with their sacred folk songs and danceable grooves.rnrnBOLO is comprised of Eliyahu Sills (of Qadim Ensemble), Evan Fraser (of Dirtwire), and Surya Prakasha (of Lavay Smith and Her Red Hot Skillet Lickers). With elements of jazz and influences from Africa, India, and the Middle East, the trio incorporates a diversity of instrumentation ranging from flutes, strings, and percussion.rnrnFeaturing:rnEliyahu Sills - Indian & Middle Eastern flutes (bansuri, ney), bass, oud, vocalrnEvan Fraser - ngoni, kalimba, percussion, vocalsrnSurya Prakasha - drums, harmonium, vocalsrn rnDive into BOLO's music on Facebook and Bandcamp.rnrnPC: BOLOrnrnrnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets. No one will be turned away for lack of funds.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Ben Goldberg / Ila Cantor / Hamir Atwal: Jazz Trio: "No telling what would happen!"
description: Interested in the intersection between jazz and the clarinet, Ben Goldberg explores the rich traditions of the woodwind instrument found in klezmer music. Goldberg is widely recognized as one of the most accomplished clarinetists/composers working in today's jazz scene. He studied with jazz legends Steve Lacy and Joe Lovano before going on to establish himself as a creative force alongside Bill Frisell, Nels Cline and John Zorn amongst countless others. He will be joined by SF Bay Area drummer Hamir Atwal and guitarist Ila Cantor.rnrnHamir Atwal is an up-and-coming drummer/composer based around Berkeley, CA. He graduated from Berklee College of Music and has performed with the Glimpse Trio, tUneyArDs, Bill Laswell, and Joe Lovano. He was also a guest clinician/teacher at schools such as Oberlin College, Peabody Conservatory, Stanford University, CalArts, and the California Jazz Conservatory. Atwal is an active endorser of DREAM cymbals.rnrnlla Cantor is a dynamic, award-winning guitarist and composer currently living in Oakland, CA. She has trained in composition and both jazz and classical guitar with the New School University in New York and the Banff Center for the Arts in Alberta, Canada. Additionally, she has worked with renowned Argentine composer/pianist Guillermo Klein and mentors John Scofield, John Abercrombie and Ben Monder. As both a sideman and leader, Cantor has toured internationally throughout the U.S. and Spain. Her first CD, Mother Nebula, was released by Fresh Sound Records in 2006.rn rnFeaturing:rnHamir Atwal - drumsrnIla Cantor - guitarrnBen Goldberg - clarinetrnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. title: Amy LaCour Trio: Music for the Soul-Searching
description: Oakland-based vocalist Amy LaCour returns to the Red Poppy with cellist Misha Khalikulov and percussionist Josh Mellinger for a night of original material inspired by the American traditions of folk, soul, blues, jazz, and bluegrass. Amy and Misha began collaborating in late 2013 and quickly discovered a musical chemistry. Misha brings years of classical training and improvisational experience to Amy's blues and folk-based songwriting. The result is a spare and intimate setting that highlights musicianship, voice, and song. Percussionist Josh Mellinger joined in 2015, bringing his knowledge of various musical traditions to round out the trio.rnrnFeaturing:rnAmy LaCour - vocalsrnMisha Khalikulov - cellornJosh Mellinger - percussionrnrn“The Oakland resident's remarkable new EP, "Yes!" offers LaCour's gorgeously evocative vocals, eloquent lyrics and beautiful, intricate melodies, enhanced by Misha Khalikulov's incredibly inventive cello lines... The blend holds a special magic.” rn- Paul Freeman, Palo Alto Daily Newsrnrn“If Joni Mitchell is waking up to the sound of a fresh drizzling rain, then LaCour is stepping through the door the moment the sun peeks out. It’s the raw cool of folk warmed with resting embers of soul.”rn- John Flynn, Metro Silicon ValleyrnrnFollow Amy on Facebook and get a feel for her music on Bandcamp.rnWatch a couple of past performances here and here.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:45pm. Show at 8:10pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Chris Duffy's You Get A Spoon!: A Variety Show and Prize Giveaway
description:
A positive way to spend a Thursday night in San Francisco, comedian Chris Duffy brings to the Poppy his hit variety show, You Get A Spoon!, for a special one-night-only Bay Area edition. Three talented guests each perform a set and enthusiastically endorse things they love, which are then given to the audience Oprah-style. Previous audience members have taken home candy bars, books, magazine subscriptions, kitchen appliances, nose flutes, and gaff tape. Every show, one lucky audience member receives a Tovolo mixing spoon (the greatest spoon in the universe).rnrnFeaturing:rnChris Duffy, comedian/hostrnAusten Rosenfeld, poet (AGNI, Los Angeles Review)rnDhaya Lakshminarayanan, comedian/storyteller (NPR's Snap Judgment)rnCaille Millner, author (The Golden Road)rn rnChris Duffy is a NYC-based comedian who performs across the country. His shows have been featured in The New York Times, Boston Globe, Washington Post, and in The Onion A.V. Club. Chris is the creator and host of You're the Expert, a live show, podcast, and public radio program on Boston's WBUR, where three comedians try to guess what a scientist studies all day. In addition to comedy, Chris is a writer whose work has appeared in the Boston Globe, Makeshift Magazine, and Wag's Revue. Chris is both a former fifth grade teacher and a former fifth grade student.rnrnrnAdmission: $15. Doors at 7:30pm. Show at 8:00pm. All ages welcome.

rnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Beaucoup Chapeaux: Parisian Bistro, Italian Café and Cirque
description:
Since 2009, Beaucoup Chapeaux has played numerous tours throughout Northern California and the Pacific Northwest and has spent countless Friday evenings in a French café in their hometown of Nevada City, CA. Over 500 concerts later, every show is still a new, lively voyage. The band has been referred to as a talented, astonishing juggernaut with music hauntingly wild and beautiful. Reinterpreting traditional tunes from a wide swath of Europe and the Balkans, Beaucoup Chapeaux also presents original music influenced by Reinhardt, Piaff, Roma, American jazz, folk, and more.rnrnFeaturing:rnMaggie McKaig - accordion, vocalsrnLuke Wilson - tenor guitar, plectrum banjo, vocalsrnMurray Campbell - violin, oboe, English horn, vocalsrnRandy McKean - clarinet, bass clarinet, piccolo, vocalsrn rn"Wonderful lively music, reminiscent of the Greek tavernas I used to visit so long ago. Spirited and very professional, close your eyes and enjoy!" - Goode, CD Baby reviewrnrn"Beaucoup Chapeaux has created a very magical niche with this music. It's a feel, an ambiance, something lovers can whisper behind and get up and dance to. It is so much Parisian Bistro or Italian Cafe, part Circus, and is very eccentric, and all of that, we love about it! Bravo, Beaucoup Chapeaux!"rn- Eleanore MacDonald,  Jan 2013 CD Baby reviewrnrnAlbum cover for Beaucoup Chapeaux's second album, This Music Heals Broken Hearts

rnrnFollow Beaucoup Chapeaux on Facebook and YouTube.rnLearn more about Maggie here.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Concerto Club: Local Classical Musicians As Soloists
description: With a repertoire mainly drawn from the heyday of the string concerto, – the late Baroque period – Concerto Club plays the music of Vivaldi, Bach, Telemann, as well as composers from other periods, such as Arvo Pärt and Alessandro Rolla. All members earn their way into the club by performing a solo concerto in public at a level that meets its standards.rnrnFeaturing:rnMia Bella D'Augelli - violinrnYuri Kye – violinrnJacob Hansen-Joseph – violarnChristina Simpson - violarnLewis Patzner – cellornMosa Tsay - cellornrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. title: Flying Under the Radar Presents: Ricardo Peixoto Quartet: An Evening of Brazilian Music
description: The Red Poppy Art House and Flying Under the Radar join together for a three-night co-production of artists from Brazil and the U.S., featuring Ian Faquini ft. Sandy Cressman & Colin Hogan (Thurs 1/7), Brazil Beat (Fri 1/8), and Ricardo Peixoto Quartet (Sat 1/9).rnrnOriginally from Rio de Janeiro and based in the Bay Area, guitarist/composer Ricardo Peixoto is among the top representatives of Brazilian guitar in the US today. His fluid, melodic style and keen compositional sense explore his country’s rich and diverse traditions, both in original works as well as in arrangements of Brazilian classics. Peixoto has recorded, performed, and collaborated with Claudia Villela, Flora Purim and Airto, saxophonist Bud Shank, percussionist Dom Um Romão, Toots Thielemans, Dori Caymmi, Guinga, Claudio Roditi, guitarist Carlos Oliveira, Marcos Silva and Terra Sul. He has performed throughout the US, Europe, Canada, Japan, and Brazil.rnrnFeaturing:rnRicardo Peixoto - guitarrnColin Hogan - accordionrnAaron Germain - bassrnBrian Rice - percussionrn rnAbout Flying Under the Radar:rnLeading up to Flying Under the Radar’s 2017 Biennial Festival of the Arts, FUTR is partnering with local SF/Bay Area organizations to present a vibrant and eclectic range of artists producing work across disciplines and aesthetics, as well as to broaden and deepen relationships across artistic communities.rnrnThe first Flying Under the Radar Biennial will debut in San Francisco, California over five days in April 2017. A second edition, in 2019, will occur in Rio de Janeiro. The event will feature thirty emerging or career artists with limited or no exposure outside their home countries. Participating artists will be from the metropolitan areas of Rio de Janeiro and San Francisco/Bay Area, and will be known for working in the areas of Film and Video, Music, Dance and Performance, Poetry, and Visual Arts.rnrnrnrnrnrnAdmission: $20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be s

title: Flying Under the Radar Presents: Ian Faquini ft. Sandy Cressman & Colin Hogan
description: The Red Poppy Art House and Flying Under the Radar join together for a three-night co-production of artists from Brazil and the U.S., featuring Ian Faquini ft. Sandy Cressman & Colin Hogan (Thurs 1/7), Brazil Beat (Fri 1/8), and Ricardo Peixoto Quartet (Sat 1/9).rnrnIan Faquini is a Brazilian guitarist and singer who resides in Berkeley, California. At the age of 15, Ian became the mentoree of the legendary Brazilian guitar maestro Guinga. In a short amount of time, Ian had become one of the most respected and exceptional guitar players in the San Francisco Bay Area, demonstrating rare chordal technique and a special delicate touch on very difficult pieces. His last few summers have been spent in Brazil, where he performed and studied with some of the country's highest-level guitar players, including Marcus Tardelli, Guinga, and others. Ian recently joined the faculty at California Brazil Camp, is currently completing a degree at the California Jazz Conservatory in Berkeley, and has just released a new album of all original compositions with flutist Rebecca Kleinmann. Ian plays and sings originals, as well as a large repertoire of sambas, choros, bossa novas, and Brazilian classical guitar pieces.rnrnBorn in New York City and based in the SF Bay Area, vocalist/composer Sandy Cressman began her career recording and performing with the vocal jazz group Pastiche before focusing on her passion for Brazilian jazz. She has toured the US, Europe and Brazil performing with her group Homenagem Brasileira and collaborating with other Brazilian musicians and Brazilian music specialists. Most recently, Sandy appeared as a special guest of SpokFrevo Orquestra on the Main Stage of Carnaval 2015 in Recife, Brazil. She released two CDs of Brazilian jazz and will be releasing her third, Entre Amigos, in 2016.rnrnCurrently residing in Oakland, CA, Colin Hogan was a member of the world-renowned Berkeley High School Jazz Ensemble while also studying with the jazz school’s founder, Susa

title: Seth Ford-Young Quartet: Chamber Music Meets Gypsy Jazz
description:
For a program of gypsy jazz-inflected tunes, Seth Ford-Young gathers a superlative cast featuring accordion master Rob Reich,  violinist Evan Price and rhythm guitarist Jason Vanderford. The evening will include new material combining the transparent textures and dynamic subtlety of chamber music with the ebullient bounce perfected by Django Reinhardt.
rn
rnA rhythmic catalyst in the Bay Area music scene for nearly two decades, Seth Ford-Young now resides in Los Angeles and has been a whirlwind of activity. He has collaborated with a dazzling array of artists including Tom Waits, Edward Sharpe and the Magnetic Zeros, Beats Antique, and The Hot Club of San Francisco.rn
rnFeaturing:rnEvan Price - violin, octave violinrnRob Reich - accordionrnJason Vanderford - guitarrnSeth Ford-Young - bass, arrangements, compositionsrnrn
rnrnFollow Seth on Facebook and catch his music on YouTube.

title: Ramana Vieira and Fátima Santos: An Evening of Portuguese Fado
description:
East Coast duo Fátima Santos and José Luis Iglésias join West Coast vocalist Ramana Vieira for an evening of fado-inspired music. Fado, the most widely recognized music of Portugal, is a passionate, soul-stirring genre with soaring vocals and dramatic tales of love, loss and redemption.
rnrn
rnRamana Vieira’s personal relationship with fado music lies deep in her family history. Her grandfather was a well-known musician and composer from Madeira Island, Portugal, and she grew up singing fado with her mother. Ramana has been described by Mundo Portugues newspaper as the “New Voice of Portuguese World Music.” She has headlined the world's largest Portuguese festival, the New Bedford Portuguese Feast in Massachusetts, and performed at the 2010 Encontro Festival in Macau.rnrnFátima Santos, a native of Albergaria-A-Velha in northern Portugal, began singing in church. Attracted by the operatic vocal technique used by Coimbra fado singers, and inspired by great composers such as José Afonso and Luiz Goes, Fátima began performing this type of fado along with other types of Portuguese vocal music. She is the recipient of two IPMA (International Portuguese Music Awards) for Best World Music and Song of the Year (2014).rnrnFeaturing:rnRamana Vieira - vocalsrnFátima Santos - vocalsrnJosé Luis Iglésias - Portuguese guitarrnPatrick Fahey – mandolinrnSteve Nelson – percussionrnAlberto Ramirez – acoustic bassrnEla Polak – violinrnrn
rnFollow Ramana on Facebook and SoundCloud.rn

title: The Adam Shulman Quintet: Strays: The Music of Billy Strayhorn
description: Leaning toward a bebop aesthetic, Adam Shulman and his band adapt Billy Strayhorn’s repertoire to a small ensemble with an emphasis on soloing and spontaneous group interaction. The music of Strayhorn is lyrical and dramatic with an underpinning of great harmonic depth. Refreshing in an age in jazz where angular and heady music seems to predominate, he succeeds in being both cerebral and soulful at the same time.rnrnAdam Shulman has been a staple of the Bay Area jazz scene since his move in 2002. In addition to leading his own groups, he has played as a sideman with internationally-renowned artists such as Stefon Harris, Willie Jones III, Miguel Zenon, Mark Murphy, Luciana Souza, Paula West, Larry Coryell, Sean Jones, Grant Stewart, and Bobby Hutcherson.rnrnFeaturing:rnPatrick Wolff - tenor saxophonernMike Olmos - trumpetrnJon Arkin - drumsrnAdam Shulman - pianornEric Markowitz - bassrnrn"Shulman’s introspective approach to playing and composing has the potential to reach a wide-ranging audience while remaining mindful of artistic integrity." - John Barron, jazzreview.comrnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.rnrn

description:
Diaspora Arts Connection proudly presents Ghazal Shakeri's Letters Never Written, Songs Never Sung. Join us for an intimate event of drama, nostalgia, and music presented by Ghazal Shakeri and accompanied by Sam Solino Mazahery on piano. Come share the magic on one of the year's longest nights. (This program is entirely in Farsi).rnrnAbout Diaspora Arts Connection:rnDiaspora Arts Connection is an organization dedicated to promoting music, arts, and culture of Iran and beyond, with emphasis on introducing new and up-and-coming artists in diaspora.rnrnrnrnTickets: $30 / Purchase: http://ghazalsanfrancisco.bpt.me/

title: Makrú: Exploring New Musical Paths
description:
Landing in San Francisco from different parts of the world, Makrú takes elements of rumba flamenca, reggae, cumbia, ska, and other global beats to create an uplifting and unifying sound. The group's lyrics in Spanish and English bring reality, fiction, and joy together in the hopes of spreading a message of respect and love.

rn

title: CALI2CUBA Presents: Rhythmic Resistance: The Zen Chaos of Afro-Cuban Drumming - A Lecture/Demonstration by John Santos
description: The Goal:rn20% of the proceeds will go to help fund Juan Bencomo's community project in barrio Jesus Maria, La Habana, such as painting his studio and promoting his business. With the hopes of becoming the first Cali2Cuba Casa, children from all over Habana can come and study the folklore of Cuba with the best teachers and artists in the area.rnrnAbout Cali2Cuba:rnCali2Cuba is an organization dedicated to the promotion and preservation of Afro-Cuban culture. The organization seeks to teach, nourish and engage the community by presenting fundraisers, workshops, and concerts that bridge gaps in musical history and knowledge. C2C believes that promoting Afro-Caribbean music will help create solutions to social, political and economic ills and create community that will make a positive impact in our world.rnrnAbout John Santos:rnSeven-time Grammy nominee and US Artists Fontanals Fellow, John Santos, is one of the foremost exponents of Afro-Latin music in the world today. He is known for his innovative use ofrntraditional forms and instruments in combination with contemporary music, and has earned much respect and recognition as an educator, composer, and record and event producer.rnMr. Santos is widely respected as one of the top writers, teachers and historians in the field and is a member of the Latin Jazz Advisory Committee of the Smithsonian Institution. He has conducted lectures, workshops and clinics in the Americas and Europe since 1973. Additionally, he has contributed to the international magazines Percussive Notes, Modern Drummer, Modern Percussionist, and Latin Percussionist. The San Francisco Bay Area community in which he still lives and works has presented him with numerous awards and honors for artistic excellence and social dedication.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $20. Lecture/Demonstration at 2:00pm.rnrnFor reservations, please call 805-455-8224.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Latin Jazz Youth Ensemble of San Francisco: The Next Generation of Latin Jazz Artists
description:
Performing Latin jazz classics and original compositions, the Latin Jazz Youth Ensemble of San Francisco (LJYE) is comprised of talented musicians ages 10 to 18. The mission of LJYE is to preserve the traditions of Latin jazz and to create role models for aspiring student musicians. The group has recorded three CDs with original material composed by the students themselves, including LJYE Live at Yoshi's (2005), Generaciones (2009), and Con Mis Manos (2015).
rnrn
rn"Far from a kiddie show, this group of young musicians, who range in age from 10 to 18 years old, display a serious professionalism." - Mary Ellen Hunt, San Francisco Chroniclernrn

title: Mark Growden and Chris Grady: Soulful Chamber/Folk
description:
For a rare Bay Area performance, Mark Growden and his long-time collaborator Chris Grady bring to the Red Poppy a combination of soulful vocals with elements of chamber, folk, and jazz.
rn

rnMark Growden is a composer, conductor, multi-instrumentalist and the director/founder of The Calling All Choir. Raised in a small mountain community in Northeast California, his work emanates earthy, soulful and poetic tones. Growden has ten years of experience teaching music in schools and currently leads singing workshops across the country. He has devoted his life to making music for other people, and to helping other people make music for themselves.

rn
rnrnFeaturing:
rnrnrnMark Growden - vocals, saxophones, accordion, banjo, sruti box, bicycle handlebars
rnChris Grady - trumpet, cornet
rnrnrn
rn"His music combines elements from classical jazz ensembles, New Orleans-style brass bands, cabaret fanfare, and circus troupes with the stark honesty of Appalachian folk ballads and African-American prison songs." - Taos Newsrnrn"Rich, haunting and personal song cycles that incorporate avant-garde, rock, cabaret, blues, country, contemporary classical and Tin Pan Alley." - Tucson Weeklyrnrn
rn
rnFollow Mark on Facebook and YouTube.

title: Derek Lassiter Presents: Nick Hours: Poetic Chamber Pop
description: In curatorial partnership with the Red Poppy Art House, Derek Lassiter presents Nick Hours. Backed by a skilled and dynamic group of musicians, Nick creates a distinctly rich chamber pop sound in his performances.rnrnNick Hours is an insightful and ambitious singer-songwriter based in Oakland, CA. Also an accomplished composer for the stage, Nick’s songwriting is mature, playful, and highly atmospheric. Combining poetic lyricism with inspired musicianship and a mixture of classical and jazz instrumentation, his heartfelt music hits deep with a rare sincerity.rnrnFeaturing:rnNick Rattray (Nick Hours) – vocalsrnCristina de Leon – violinrnTim Silva – drums, vocalsrnJary Niebur – guitar, vocalsrnTravis Kindred – bassrn rnAbout Derek Lassiter:rnLassiter, a mainstay on the San Francisco arts scene, has received accolades for both his recordings and live performances featuring Bay Area notables such as pianist/composer Tammy Hall, producer/percussionist PC Muñoz, and composer/pianist Art Khu. Lassiter is profiled in Julian C. R. Okwu's book, Face Forward: Young African American Men in a Critical Age.rn rn"At turns bright and others sinister, the music Nick makes is beautiful. He’s at the center of a maelstrom of musicians, and you should hear what he has created with them!"rn– Oaktown Indie MayhemrnrnFollow Nick Hours on Facebook and SoundCloud.rnWatch the official video for Mission of Dancing here.rnrnPC: Nick HoursrnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets. No one will be turned away for lack of funds.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Marcus Shelby Trio ft. Miss Faye Carol & Joe Warner: The Legacy of the Blues
description: Details TBA!rnrnFeaturing:rnMiss Faye Carol - vocalsrnJoe Warner - pianornMarcus Shelby - bassrn rnMarcus Shelby is a bandleader, composer, arranger, bassist, educator, and activist who currently lives in San Francisco, California. Over the past 25 years he has built a diverse biography as a composer. His work and music has focused on sharing the history, present, and future of African American lives, on social movements in the United States of America, and on early childhood music education. Currently, Shelby is an artist in residence with the Yerba Buena Gardens Festival and the Artistic Director of the Marcus Shelby Orchestra. His series of interactive discussions, Beyond the Blues: Ending the Prison Industrial Complex, delves into our justice system, its flaws, and how music can be used as a tool for reform and change.rnrnFaye Carol is one of the premiere vocalists of her time with a career spanning over five decades. Arranging popular standards with doses of jazz, blues and hip-hop, Miss Carol has managed to cultivate a diverse sound throughout the years. Her divine energy and ability to rock the mic consistently leaves her audience both mesmerized and uplifted. As founder of The School Of The Getdown, Miss Carol has effectively developed two programs to serve the Bay Area community. MITC (Music In The Community) and The Vocal Workshop Series have triumphantly supported hundreds of youth and adults in becoming more confident, skilled musicians. Known for her outwardly-loving embrace and support of all, Faye Carol remains a conduit for bringing together many communities through music.rnrnJoe Warner is a pianist based in the San Francisco Bay Area.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Byb & Kiandanda Dance Theater Present: Ange Aoussou and Chris Babingui - Dance Africa Dance for Peace and Independence
description: In collaboration with the Red Poppy Art House, Byb & Kiandanda Dance Theater present Dance Africa Dance for Peace and Independence. The evening features performances by two African dancers/choreographers, Ange Aoussou from Ivory Coast and Chris Babingui from the Republic of Congo. Performing their respective solo works, J'aime le rouge and Nibo Nano, these pieces touch on the social-political issues of the African continent that is still struggling to gain true independence and development.rnrnBorn in Divo (Ivory Coast), Ange Aoussou had trained in traditional African dance, contemporary, modern and jazz. Throughout her career, she has collaborated with various choreographers from around the world and has worked with Koteba D'abidjan, Institute Djolem of Abidjan, Mr Salif Coulibaly, and Souleymane Koly.rnrnChris Babingui was born in Enyelle in Congo. At the age of five, he learned the ndombolo and other traditional dances of Congo through the imitation of his elders. After completing his secondary education in 2006, Babingui started his artistic career dancing hip-hop as he joined the group, Boom Danger of Massamba Brunel (aka “Flash”). He has since worked and toured internationally in Africa and Europe after joining the Studio Maho, directed by choreographer Florent Mahoukou.rn rnAbout Kiandanda Dance Theater and Artistic Director Byb Chanel Bibene:rnKiandanda is an international dance company that does work between Africa, Europe and the USA. Every year, the company holds workshops, the Nzela Program and the Rue Dance Festival, which is held every two years in the Republic of Congo. As formerly named Studio Rue Dance, Kiandanda Dance Theater is a laboratory, a studio where everyone is invited to come and contribute to create the art through conferences, performances and workshops.rnrnByb Chanel Bibene is a choreographer and performer working in theater and contemporary dance. rnLeading up to Flying Under the Radar’s 2017 Biennial Festival of the Arts, FUTR is partnering with local SF/Bay Area organizations to present a vibrant and eclectic range of artists producing work across disciplines and aesthetics, as well as to broaden and deepen relationships across artistic communities.rnrnThe first Flying Under the Radar Biennial will debut in San Francisco, California over five days in April 2017. title: Tito y su son de Cuba: A Night in Old Havana Cuba
description:
Taking the audience through the streets of Old Havana, Tito Gonzalez returns to the Red Poppy for a night of vibrant tunes with Cuban son and the tres guitar.
rn
rnSinger/composer Tito Gonzalez is a master showman with a voice that has been compared to that of classic son singer, Daniel Santos. Gonzalez delivers songs that are deceptively simple – between their fluid melodies and retrograde rhythms, they evoke a focus and introspection that Tito attributes, in part, to his life in America. Along with son, his music demonstrates a broad range of Cuban genres such as rumba, guaracha and the most romantic boleros.
rn

title: The String Quartet Experience: Nahuel Bronzini and the Amaranth String Quartet
description:
The String Quartet Experience is Nahuel Bronzini's arranging journey which joins together the sounds of singer-songwriting and a string quartet. Playing off of the expectations of what a classical string quartet should sound like, Bronzini creates a heightened listening experience of textures and gestures that range between old and new. "I wrote a full string quartet arrangement for each piece in hopes of representing the emotions and images depicted through their music and lyrics, while also printing my own voice as a composer. This is a true collaborative project that brings to life the musical beliefs, fantasies and needs of both myself and the songwriters." – Nahuel Bronzinirn rnAn Argentinean guitarist and composer, Nahuel Bronzini moved to San Francisco in 2010 after completing a degree in jazz at the Escuela de Música Contemporánea in Buenos Aires. Despite Bronzini’s love for popular music, his urge to keep widening his music experiences led him to continue his studies in classical guitar at the San Francisco Conservatory. Bronzini's original music explores themes of nostalgia and longing, navigating sonorities from both the contemporary classical world as well as jazz and other pop culture-related music. He is also currently involved in several Bay Area music projects as a performer, arranger and/or producer.
rn
rn rnWith a love of tackling the string quartet canon and as fierce a passion for discovering newly composed works, Amaranth was founded in 2013 and proudly calls the Bay Area home. Members Katie von Braun, Abigail Shiman, Erica Zappia, and Helen Newby bring classical music beyond the concert hall, striving to reach divergent audiences that range from high school classrooms to neighborhood bars alike. Projects this season include a performance at the San Francisco Friends of Chamber Music’s SFMusic Day 2015, participation in Oakland’s Art in Nature Festival, and a residency with the Cypress String Quartet.
rn

title: Arouna Diarra and Biko Casini: Balafon Dance Music from Burkina Faso
description:
rnrnThe Red Poppy Art House warmly welcomes Arouna Diarra and Biko Casini for an evening of traditional music from Burkina Faso. Inspired by the pulsing melodies and infectious dance grooves of the balafon, Biko traveled to Bobo-Dioulasso (Bobo), the artistic city of Burkina Faso, to study the djembe in 2007. Arouna, who comes from a family of musicians in Bobo, moved to Asheville, NC in 2012 where he established himself as the bandleader of Mande Foly. Upon meeting in Asheville, the two began playing music together and are now touring the West Coast with the band, Rising Appalachia.rn
rnFeaturing:rnBiko Casini – djembe, calabash, kamele ngonirnArouna Diarra – doula balafon, kamele ngoni, talking drum

title: Laura Klein and Destiny Muhammad - A Piano/Harp Encounter: From Celtic to Coltrane and Beyond
description:
The sound sculptress Destiny Muhammad and lyrically creative pianist/composer Laura Klein combine their formidable talents in concert for the first time. Together they'll explore an enchanting musical terrain ranging from modal jazz to Celtic music.
rnrn
rnDestiny Muhammad is a recording/performing artist in the singer-songwriter tradition with jazz and storytelling to round out the sonic experience. She has performed in Yoshi's 'Women in Jazz' Series, Oakland East Bay Symphony, and with great artists like Calvin Keyes, Marcus Shelby, Omar Sosa, Ambrose Akisemuire, and Denise Perrier. Destiny is the principal harpist for the Eddie Gale Inner Peace Orchestra, the Oakland Community Orchestra, and founder of The Destiny Muhammad Project.

rnLaura Klein is a pianist/composer with numerous recordings of her original music that have received international airplay. She is the co-leader of FivePlay Jazz Quintet, performs with the Morchestra, and plays with many of the leading Bay Area jazz luminaries. She has appeared at Yoshi’s, the SF Jazz Fes­ti­val, KCSM’s Jazz on the Hill, Vallejo Jazz, the Cotati Jazz Festival, San Francisco Performances, and has performed with the SF Symphony.rnrnFeaturing:rnLaura Klein - pianornDestiny Muhammad - harp, vocalsrnFollow Destiny on Facebook and Laura on YouTube.

title: Maria Volonte: Blue Tango Project: Born in Buenos Aires. Raised on the Road.
description:
Hailing from the colorful and gritty La Boca neighborhood of Buenos Aires, Blue Tango Project is an exploration of the emotional and musical crossroads where tango and the blues embrace. The project is a collaboration between Argentine Latin Grammy nominee María Volonté and California harmonica player and poet Kevin Carrel Footer. This globe-trotting duo celebrates the deep spiritual bond that joins tango and the blues as two musical genres born in desire and marginality, many times censored yet always resurgent and speaking directly to our hungry souls.rnrnFeaturing:rnMaria Volonte - vocals, guitarrnKevin Carrel Footer - harmonicarn“The best-kept secret of Argentinian tango… Well established and widely respected in her native Argentina, Maria Volonte is a singer who can switch between jazz and blues-influenced vocals, intimate Argentinian-style folk singing and classic big tango diva numbers.” — Songlines Magazine
rn

title: Industrial Revelation: Liberation & the Kingdom of Nri - PNW Album Release Tour
description:
On tour for the release of their latest two-disc album, Liberation & the Kingdom of Nri, Industrial Revelation takes on everything from jazz, blues, funk, hip-hop, rock, chamber music, and beyond.
rn
rnIndustrial Revelation is about human emotion– the unbridled energy, excitement, passion, beauty and the enjoyment that coincides. Based in Seattle, the band was founded by D'Vonne Lewis through the ambition to continue the musical legacy of his family and to create melodies characterized by a vibrant spirit. Industrial Revelation is influenced by all styles of music and strives to generate something special that transcends the moment, especially at a live show.
rn

rnFeaturing:
rnD'Vonne Lewis - drumsrnEvan Flory-Barnes - bass
rnAhamefule J. Oluo - trumpetrnJosh Rawlings - rhodes/keyboardrn
rn"Industrial Revelation embodies jazz; jazz is meta-musical, embodying everything else. And so IR rocks. But really they’re just virtuoso musicians playing risky and loose. They are a joy to hear. They slide around the music scene, doing the thing they do, mercurial and misplaced and unsung. As outsiders, they fit right in.” – Jonathan Zwickel, City Artsrn

title: Fandangueros: Latin/Filipino Folk and Contemporary Music
description:
Traversing the musical footprints between Spain, Latin America and the Philippines, Fandangueros' compositions value tradition as well as their new iterations. Fandangueros started out as an outfit to support Florante Aguilar’s commissioned compositions and projects. The group’s identity later evolved through Florante and Chus Alonso’s shared passion for their country’s folk music – the Philippines and Spain, respectively. While Florante’s nationalistic approach is a nod to its Spanish influence, Chus posits that the exchange goes both ways – that Spain’s music is also the beneficiary of many folk traditions from Latin America and beyond.rnrnFeaturing:rnFlorante Aguilar - guitar, arrangements, compositionsrnChus Alonso - flute & laud, arrangements, compositionsrnSage Baggott - drumsrnGreg Kehret - double bassrn

title: BON JOUR MONOGRAMISTA T.D: Slovakia/SF Intermedia Project
description:
Monogramista T.D (born as Dezider Tóth in 1947) is considered “the father of Slovak conceptual art” with artwork ranging from performance, installation and bookmaking, to painting, object and minimalist intervention. His work, Partituras (1976-1978), attracted the attention of contemporary Slovak composers and intermedia artists, Fero Kiraly and Ivan Siller. Partituras is a series of ink drawings strongly resembling music scores. Until now, it has never been musically portrayed. Kiraly and Siller, together with a group of interdisciplinary artists, reinterpret various parts of Partituras through music, dance, sound art and spoken word in their multimedia performance, BON JOUR MONOGRAMISTA T.D.rnFeaturing:rnIvan Siller – piano, sound objects
rnFero Kiraly – analog, monophonic synthesizer
rnDavid Molina – electronics
rnShikha Malaviya – poetry
rn

rn*Program brought to you by Platform 1-12 and supported by Ministry of Culture of Slovak Republic.
rn

rnCatch a sneak peak here.
rnLearn more about the project here.

title: Jazz in the Neighborhood Presents: Kristen Strom
description:
Presented by Jazz in the Neighborhood's monthly series at the Poppy, saxophonist/vocalist Kristen Strom brings a program of original and standard tunes in a trio setting with husband Scott Sorkin on guitar and John Wiitala on bass. "With a tone that's lithe and lustrous, Kristen Strom embodies her musical philosophy in every note she plays." – Andrew GilbertrnrnFeaturing:rnKristen Strom - saxophone, vocalsrnScott Sorkin - guitarrnJohn Wiitala - bassrnrnMore on Jazz in the Neighborhood:rnJazz in the Neighborhood's mission is to celebrate and invigorate the Bay Area’s rich, musical jazz heritage by creating more opportunities for established local musicians to perform, mentor aspiring players, and share this great American art form with the community. Through collaboration with school music programs and adult big bands, the project enables top-caliber artists to push creative boundaries while helping developing musicians “learn on the bandstand” according to the great jazz tradition. Performances in neighborhood venues not only showcase the wealth of local talent, but also create regular destinations for people to enjoy rich cultural and social experiences where they live.rn

title: Fero Kiraly - Soundring: Hands-On Music Workshop for Children
description: SOUNDRING is an interactive educational workshop for children age five+. SOUNDRING was created by the Slovak contemporary musician and composer, Fero Kiraly. He uses a custom-made electronic instrument called Zvukodrom. During the workshop, children listen, compose and conduct. Fero explains how to listen for varied environmental sounds as a snoring dog, meowing cat or a motorcycle. Children use the Zvukodrom to improvise with those sounds and play together in duet, trio, quartet or even an entire orchestra. Children become aware of mathematically based concepts, such as keeping time, learn collaborative listening skills and even practice the art of conducting.rnrnWhat is a Zvukodrom?rnA Zvukodrom (zvu-ko-DROM) is a special instrument that consists of handmade molded objects of geometrical shapes designed by Martin Piacek. These touch-responsive objects are connected to a computer, which gives users the ability to create sounds and music through software specially designed for the workshop.rnrnLearn more about SOUNDRING and the Zvukodrom here.rnrnrnrnAdmission: $10 suggested donation. Workshop at 11:00am. For children ages 5+.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.rnrn rn

description:
A dynamic night of blues, soul, and swing, the Tiffany Austin Quintet celebrates the great blues singer, Bessie Smith.
rn
rn
rnDescribed by NPR's Kevin Whitehead as "a musician to keep an ear on," Tiffany Austin is one of the fastest rising jazz singers in Northern California, with a tradition-rooted yet thoroughly contemporary style. After earning her J.D. from Berkeley Law, Tiffany released her debut CD, Nothing But Soul. Her refreshing singing style has attracted the attention of artists such as vibraphonist Roy Ayers, saxophonist John Handy, and drummer Tommy Campbell. Tiffany has performed at venues across the U.S., including SFJAZZ, Yoshi’s (Oakland and San Francisco), and the Blue Note (New York).

title: Steven Lugerner's JACKNIFE: Revisiting Jackie McLean’s Blue Note Recordings
description:
Saxophonist Steven Lugerner maintains an active, bi-costal performance schedule between San Francisco and New York City. As a band leader, he has released multiple albums to critical acclaim and has toured the U.S. as a sideman and leader of his own ensembles. He has collaborated with Grammy-nominated pianist Fred Hersch,drum legends Albert “Tootie” Heath and Matt Wilson, pianist Jason Moran, saxophonist Jane Ira Bloom, pianist Myra Melford, trumpeter Darren Johnston, and bassist Todd Sickafoose. Through a contemporary sensibility, Steven Lugerner's JACKNIFE pays homage to the great alto saxophonist Jackie McLean, Lugerner’s main influence in the jazz idiom.rnrnFeaturing:rnSteven Lugerner - alto saxophonernJJ Kirkpatrick - trumpetrnAdam Shulman - pianornJosh Thurston-Milgrom - bassrnMichael Mitchell - drumsrnrnFollow Steven on Facebook and SoundCloud.rnrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00pm. Show at 7:30pm. All ages welcome.

rnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnJoin the conversation at the Poppy – volunteer with us! Click here.

title: Altar para mi barrio: Art, Poetry, Food & Live Music for Day of the Dead
description:
Día de los Muertos is an extraordinarily auspicious time for the Mission community, and the Red Poppy Art House is humbled to participate in the annual celebration that honors the ancestral memory of the departed. We invite all of our community to join us on Sunday, November 1 and Monday, November 2 for a weekend of art, food, and remembrance.rnrnOn Sunday evening, curator Alejandro Meza unveils the exhibition, El Mito Contemporáneo del Más Allá, featuring commissioned altars that pay homage to the dead. The exhibition will be complemented by food (tamales de carne, tamales vegetarianos, champurrado) and live music curated by Sofia Elias.rnrnMonday evening's program opens with a rumba session featuring Los Rumberos de Cali. Concurrently, Sofia Elias, Adriana Camarena, and Florencia Rojo lead a procession honoring those who have died as a result of police violence. The procession will begin at Alex Nieto's memorial site on Bernal Hill, stop at Amilcar Perez-Lopez's memorial site on Folsom Street, and end at the Red Poppy Art House. A guided discussion will follow, led by community members who have lost loved ones to police violence, including Elvira and Refugio Nieto. The night will conclude with a series of live performances honoring victims of police violence, including the work of poet Ben Bac Sierra, a close friend of the deceased Alex Nieto.rnNovember 1st Program

rn6:00pm: Exhibition, El Mito Contemporáneo del Más Allá
rn7:30pm: Lara Manzanares
rn8:00pm: Manos Lindas
rn8:30pm: Trio Cambio
rn9:00pm: Camille Mai

rnCurators: Caleb Duarte (Lead Artistic Director), Schuyler Karr (Performance Programming Director), Alejandro Meza (Exhibition Curator), Sofia Elias (Performance/Food Curator)
rn

title: The Bug Tones: Spooky Toons, Blues, and Boogies
description:
rnrnThe golden age of the silver screen and Halloween featuring Betty Boop and Mickey Mouse! For an evening of scary fun and beautiful music, watch the magic of early 20th century cartoons as The Bug Tones play music to make your feet tap and your head bounce.rnrnA composer, bassist, and bandleader, Safa Shokrai is inspired by visual art, architecture, and movement. They inform the color and tone of his work. He strives to create a performance experience in which the audience steps into a film, a world that has been fabricated and imagined, realized with sound and sight.rnrn
