title: Ensemble Mik Nawooj
description:
Hip Hop and Classical Genres Collide

rnRed Poppy group-artist-in-residence Ensemble Mik Nawooj (EMN) has been turning heads in the Bay Area Music scene with their innovative collision of classical and hip hop styles. The ensemble’s leader, composer and pianist JooWan Kim believes that hybridization, or the fusing together of musical styles, is the mechanism through which music has transformed so radically over the last century. In EMN's case, JooWan Kim's particular fusion of classical instrumentation's rigorousness and technical variety with classic hip hop's lyrical dynamism and captivating beats, allows each genre to surge to new and unforeseen heights of ingenuity. For this show at the Poppy, EMN will present their re-imaginings of Wu-Tang Clan classics “C.R.E.A.M.” and “Wu Tang Clan Ain’t Nothing to Fuck Wit” from Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers), Snoop Dogg’s “Gin and Juice” and “Who Am I” from Doggystyle, as well as other original music from their upcoming debut studio album.rnrnEnsemble Mik Nawooj's performance is supported by the Zellerbach Family Foundation.rnrnEnsemble Mik Nawooj is:rnrnJooWan Kim - composition, pianornDo D.A.T. & Sandman - MCsrnShauna Fallihee - sopranornAnna Washburn -violinrnLewis Patzner - cellornCarmen Lemoine - fluternJeannie Psomas - clarinetrnEugene Theriault - bassrnLyman Alexander - drumsrnrnFor this show, fellow Red Poppy group-in-residence The View from Bernal Hill will open for EMN, adding their own "chamber flavored jazz" to the mix. Freshly back from their first West Coast tour, the trio will deliver their usual outpouring of positive energy as they perform songs from their first EP "We Are No Beautiful Children," providing an exquisite prelude to what is sure to be an exhilarating musical whirlwind of an evening.rnrnThe View from Bernal Hill is:rnrnCamille Mai - piano, voicernNahuel Bronzini - guitarrnSchuyler Karr - bassrnrnCheck out EMN's website and concert video.rnFind The View from Bernal Hill on Facebook here.rnJoin

title: The Supplicants
description:
Spontaneous Improvisational Tunes

rn In 1999, The Supplicants recorded their debut album, previously un-rehearsed, at a loft apartment in the Fillmore District of San Francisco, and have since focused on bringing to life the same free-flow, spontaneous feeling in each of their unedited performances. From that day 15 years ago, this amazing music has emerged and flowed on a wave of like-minded communication and collective accompaniment, evolving at the edge of improvisation in its truest form. These musicians stay true to the nature of improvisation, listening deeply to each other and feeling into the spirit of the audience to create one-of-a-kind tunes special for every performance. The pure and raw energy poured into the interwoven beats and melodies leaves no avenue unexplored, allowing for the music to be entirely accessible and rhythmically swinging. The Supplicants will reunite at the Red Poppy Art House for a night of impromptu jazz/Indian blend rhythms that will be sure to captivate your soul and liberate your mind.rnrnDavid Boyce - Tenor Sax; Soprano Sax; Bass clarinetrnRichard Howell - Tenor Sax; Soprano Sax; Talking DrumrnSameer Gupta - Drums; Tabla; PercussionrnDavid Ewell - Acoustic BassrnrnBecome a fan on their Facebook page.rnIf you want a sneak peek of the music, check out this video.rnAlso, join the official Facebook event.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $20-25rnrnDouble Show: First performance: Doors at 6:30pm, show at 7pm. Second performance: Doors at 8:20pm, show at 8:45pmrnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: VidyA: Double Show!
description:
South Indian Classical Music Meets Jazz

rn Praised for their emotional energy and musicianship, VidyA has been featured at venues such as SFJazz, San Jose Jazz, Yoshi's, Asian American Film Festival, Recontraire Internationale in France, and more. An effortlessly smooth saxophonist, Radhakrishnan's original compositions for VidyA have been commissioned by the De Young Museum via the Jazz at Intersection series and awarded the Zellerbach Family Fund grant among others.rnrnTodd Brown, founder of Red Poppy Art House, praised the former Red Poppy Resident Artists by saying, “VidyA's music breathes at the very center of a cultural crossroads between the North American jazz idiom and the Carnatic music of South India. VidyA's music is a new vein of North American music... present tense, present location, new culture in the making."rnrnVidyA consists of:rnrnPrasant Radhakrishnan -SaxophonernDavid Ewell - BassrnSameer Gupta - DrumsrnrnTo hear a sneak peek of their music, visit the YouTube link here.rnrnVisit their official website, and also, check out the Facebook event here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. VidyA will play two 70 minute shows. Doors open at 6:30pm, first show begins at 7:00pm. Doors open again at 8:20pm, second show begins at 8:40pm.  rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: MAPP (Mission Arts Performance Project) #wheredoyoubelong
description: This MAPP will present #wheredoyoubelong, a OutLook Theater Project investigation in collaboration with 14 Black Poppies. rnrnOutLook Theater Project and 14 Black Poppies present their first interactive, multi-disciplinary performance showcase as part of their #wheredoyoubelong investigation. And we'd LOVE for you to join us at the table!rnrnThe OutLook Theater Project ensemble is Shirley Acuna, Anne Carol Mitchell, Rebecca Schultz, Anthony Julius Williams, and Jason WymanrnrnAdditional Artists include: Adriana Camarena, Oscar Salinas, Antoinette Rios, Henry Murrillo Jimenez, Ray Ysaguirre, Zeph Fishlyn, Freddy Ibarra, Danny Abdon, Sabri Jaber, Marcell Jackson, Anna Maria Luera, and many more!rnrnProgram:rnrnWORKSHOPSrnrn1pm: Performance Workshop with Anthony Julius Williams (performing belonging)rnrnBelong is a verb -- how do we perform belonging in our daily lives? How can we bring more awareness, vitality and even joy to our daily performances of belonging? Join Anthony Julius Williams and OutLook Theater for an exploration of belonging in this fun workshop for new and experienced performers alike. Visualization, movement and improvisation will allow us to discover the spirit of belonging in each of us. rnrn2pm: Literary Workshop with Zeph Fishlyn (writing belonging)rnrnAs we navigate the complexities of identity, geography, and social upheaval, how do we find "belonging"? Join Zeph Fish and Rebecca Schultz in exploring these questions through play, storytelling, writing and visual art. Together we will create Mapping Belonging, a participatory visual installation as part of OutLook Theater Project and 14 Black Poppies’ #wheredoyoubelong, a multi-disciplinary investigation of belonging. Bring: a notebook, pen, and your voice.rnrn3pm: Visual Art Workshop with Rebecca Schultz (mapping belonging)rnrnA DINNER PARTY rnrnArrival: We Belong Herern7:00: Doors openrnrn7:10: "I Am A Disco Ball Intro" by Anthony Julius Williams & Freddy Ibarrarnrn7:15: "I Belong Here" (soun

title: MAD NOISE
description:
"Best Band" by SF Bay Guardian Best of the Bay

rnThe band got their name one fateful summer night in the alleyway beside City Lights Bookstore in North Beach. The bandmates were enjoying a 14 hour day of busking when a friendly neighborhood wino walking by yelled, “Y’all make MAD NOISE.” The crew embraced their new title immediately and four years later has been described as a hybrid of jazz and funk with a mixture of folk and a wide range of instruments all combined to give MAD NOISE an award winning sound. The band started their career by performing free shows at "The Church," a secret spot on the UC Berkeley Campus, and from there moved to rooftops, graveyards, boats, back alleys, street corners and have since performed at Yoshi's and the Great American Music Hall. MAD NOISE promises a mad-acoustic and energized performance.rnrn rn"The ubiquitous, scandalously funky acoustic/jazz/punk ensemble that haunts the street corners and sidewalks from Berkeley to the Mission”. -SF Weeklyrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Villalobos Brothers Special Double Show
description: The Poppy is especially honored to welcome the Villalobos Brothers, as their hopeful lyrics invite an optimism that seamlessly aligns with the Poppy's mission as a socially conscious art space in the heart of the Mission. The brothers are known for masterfully blending the indigenous rhythms and melodies of their native Veracruz, Mexico with the intricate harmonies of jazz and classical music. A trio of virtuoso violinists, singers, composers and arrangers, they have performed at the Latin Grammy Awards, Carnegie Hall, and many other prestigious venues. In 2013, after touring Mexico to critical acclaim, they won the coveted title of "Best Band in New York City" in NPR’s Battle of the Boroughs.rnrnLuis Villalobos - Voice & ViolinrnAlberto Villalobos - Voice & ViolinrnErnesto Villalobos -Voice & ViolinrnHumberto Flores - GuitarrnLeo Sherman - Electric BassrnRosa Avila - Drum Kitrnrn rn“I am glad I lived long enough to see you! This is amazing! All I can say is, I hope the world gets to experience this. Before starting you said something very important, you said: "we try to embody the miracle of music"...well, you certainly are a carrier of that disease...may we all catch it!...I don’t know what else to say, I am speechless!” -Marion J. Caffey, Producer of "Amateur Night" at the Apollo Theatrernrn rn6:30 pm Doors open for first showrn7:00pm First show begins, 70 min no intermissionrn8:20pm First show ends, crowd’s change!rn8:45pm Second show begins, 70 min no intermissionrnrnJoin the event on Facebook and invite your friends!rnrnAdmission: $20-25rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: The View From Bernal Hill
description:
Original Jazz, Sultry Blues, and Chamber Sounds

rnWhere Charles Mingus meets Doris Day, resident artists Camille Mai, Nahuel Bronzini, and Schuyler Karr kick off their first West Coast tour as The View from Bernal Hill. On the menu: mouthwatering harmonies, eccentric rhythms, and savory new songs straight out of the sonic oven. Hailing from Paris, Buenos Aires, and Seattle, the three musicians united in San Francisco in 2012, releasing their first EP, "We Are No Beautiful Children," at the Red Poppy last December. Featuring local musicians and performance artists, the trio's performance will combine the raw spontaneity of jazz improvisation with the energy of rock and the subtle presence of chamber music, resulting in a gripping night not to be missed!rnrnThe View from Bernal Hill is:rnrnCamille Mai - piano, voicernNahuel Bronzini - guitarrnSchuyler Karr - upright bassrnrnIncluding Guest Musicians:rnrnBenjamin Ring - drumsrnShahin Gorgani -  percussionrnKendra McKinley - voicernKenya Moses - voice, dancernGabriel Goldberg - halornrnJoin the event on FacebookrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Namaskar
description:
Whirled Raga Jazz

Namaskar is an ensemble that exists in two forms, one in the San Francisco Bay Area and the other in Brooklyn, New York. The group is led by percussionist Sameer Gupta and brings a unique approach to Indian/Jazz fusion. Namaskar is comprised of stellar local improvisers who individually represent modern extemporization. From Indian classical music, avant-garde jazz, R&B and soul, to funk and dub-reggae, Namaskar explores improvisational pathways through compositions from 1950s Golden-Era Bollywood, classic jazz melodies, traditional Indian classical ragas, as well as original compositions.

Sameer Gupta - drumset, tabla
Darren Johnston - trumpet
Prasant Radhakrishnan - Carnatic saxophone
David Boyce - tenor/soprano sax, bass clarinet, efx, Black Edgar's Musicbox
Maya Kronfeld - piano, keyboards
Bryan Dean - electric bass

Check out Namaskar's Facebook page here.
If you want a sneak peek of the music, visit these YouTube links here and here.
Also, join the official Facebook event

Admission: $15-20. Double show: 7pm (Doors 6:30) & 8:30pm (Doors 8:20)


Tickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.

title: Trio Unites for a Night of New Orleans Jazz
description:
Vocalist, pianist and percussionist join forces to create a unique jazz sound

rnMcDermott, who is making a rare appearance in San Francisco, will be accompanied by the expertise of Jacques and Goerlitz to perform a night of New Orleans jazz.rnrnTom McDermott is a New Orleans composer and pianist who explores a broad range of styles. A longtime resident of the Crescent City, he has made a musical appearance and additional musical contributions on the HBO series “Treme." His latest CD, Bamboula, produced by Van Dyke Parks, has gotten rave reviews from the New York Times, and a 4-star review from Downbeat Magazine.rnrnMichelle Jacques is a vocalist and the founder of ensembles CHELLE! and Friends, and Juke Joint A Cappella. Michelle is a native of New Orleans who has made the Bay Area her home. She has shared the stage with artists such as Susana Baca, Ray Charles and the Manhattan Transfer, among others.rnrnMichaelle Goerlitz is a percussionist who has lent her skills to a variety of Bay Area groups since the 1980s, with styles ranging from Brazilian music to Afro-Cuban music to jazz and all things in between. A founding member of Wild Mango, she currently plays with the VNote Ensemble and Ojala.rnrnCheck out Tom, Michelle, and Michaelle on the web.rnJoin the official Facebook event.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Fadista Angela Brito with guitar trio Sete Colinas
description:
Fado, the Portuguese Blues

rnAngela Brito is a fadista born in Northern California who has won over audiences everywhere with her powerful voice and passionate delivery. Fado is a genre of Portuguese music typically characterized by sentiments of longing or melancholy. Sete Colinas is a string trio made up of Helder Carvalheira, Manuel Escobar and John Cardadeiro. Together, Angela and Sete Colinas provide an unforgettable and authentic fado experience full of the passion and emotion that fill the fado houses of  Lisbon, Portugal. Angela received two AUA Awards (Artistas Unidos da América) this year for Best Fado and Best Portuguese Interpretation for songs on her debut fado album released in January of this year, Fado de Olhos Fechados. Angela and Guitar Trio Sete Colinas invite audiences to enjoy traditional fado ballads as well as contemporary compositions.rnrn rnrnAngela Brito – fadistarnHelder Carvalheira – Portuguese guitar (guitarra Portuguesa)rnSteve Soares – guitar (viola do fado)rnJohn Cardadeiro – bass (viola baixo)rnrnCheck out Angela's Facebook pagernListen to Angela on YouTubernrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: FivePlay Jazz Quintet
description:
Jazz for Hungry Ears

rnFivePlay is a Bay Area-based band performing original music that nourishes both mind and heart. Whether you’re an experienced jazz listener or you’re hearing jazz for the first time, FivePlay's approachable music is the byproduct of modern creative jazz, reaching the ears of any spectator involved. Their repertoire encompasses a wide range of influences: from tango, samba and Afro-Cuban sounds, to up-tempo modal jazz, poignant waltzes and contemporary jazz/rock tones. FivePlay has performed at numerous Bay Area venues, including the JazzSchool, Yoshi's Oakland, the Emerald Tablet, Vallejo Jazz Festival, and many more. They have three CDs which have all received critical acclaim, and international radio airplay.rnrnLaura Klein - pianornTony Corman - guitarrnDave Tidball - reedsrnAlan Hall - drumsrnPaul Smith - bassrnrn rnrn”The FivePlay Jazz Quintet does more than play the notes, they make the music..word of mouth alone has them at the top of the heap in the Bay Area improvisational scene." -Brent Black, Bop-N-Jazz

title: Jason Ewald & Friends
description:
An Evening of Soulful Grooves

rnJason Ewald has spent his career deeply immersed in diverse musical environments and, as a result, has developed his own particular brand of soulful, groove-driven music.  His original compositions are inspired by a plethora of sounds that he’s picked up along the way – in his words, “by the refined sounds of 70s pop, the greasy soul of Muscle Shoals, the explorations of jazz, and the insistent rhythms of funk and rock.”  His latest work with his ensemble, called simply, Ewald, contemplates the magic that he finds lying hidden within his everyday life, emerging from encounters with friends and lovers, bike rides through the city, the powerful energy of New York City and its inhabitants.

rn Jason spent several years living and playing music in San Francisco before moving to New York City in 2007, and so for his show at the Poppy he will invite some of his favorite Bay Area artists collaborate with him for an unpredictable night of fun, funky original music.

rnJason Ewald - drums, lead vocalsrnOther performers - TBArnrnCheck out Jason's website here.rnCheck out the Facebook event here.rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Jesse Olsen Bay and Charming Hostess
description: The Red Poppy Art House offers you a double bill of eclectic tunes on Friday July 11th, bringing you the words of author and activist Tillie Olsen in musical form along with songs about social justice. Composer/multi-instrumentalist Jesse Olsen Bay celebrates the album release ofMakings, songs based on unpublished writings by his grandmother, acclaimed author/activist Tillie Olsen. Jesse is known as part of the eclectic folk duo Ramon & Jessica, as well as for his frequent collaborations with dance and theater, having written original music for Sonoma State's Fall Dance Concert. In Makings, Jesse accompanies himself on guitar, percussion, piano, toy instruments and found objects, weaving Tillie's words into a tapestry of vivid emotion and imagery.rnrn Jesse Olsen Bay - voice, guitar, piano, percussion, objectsrnrnListen to and learn more about Jesse Olsen Bay's Makings.rnrn rnrnrnrn rnrnCharming Hostess works at the intersection of voice, text and diaspora consciousness. Led by founder Jewlia Eisenberg, Charming Hostess often creates an immersive experience—making spaces that incorporate musical performance and visitor participation. They are now working on The Ginzburg Geography, a sonic cartography of the life and work of Natalia and Leone Ginzburg. Recent performance installations include Teraphim (Meridian, 2012) on feminist biblical criticism; and The Bowls Project (YBCA 2010) on Babylonian women’s amulets. Tonight Charming Hostess is proud to honor Tillie Olsen's legacy with songs of social justice and daily work.rnrn Jewlia Eisenberg - voicernCyn Taylor - voicernJason Ditzian - bass clarinetrnLaura Inserra - drumsrnrnEngage with Charming Hostess' past and current righteousness.rnJoin the event on FacebookrnrnAdmission: $10-20. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Celebrating The Pablo Neruda Centennial Festival’s 10th Anniversary
description:
A Poetry Fiesta of Love, Social Justice, Live Music and Film

rnPresented by Red Reels Film Series & Red Poppy

rnJoin us for a unique evening celebrating The Pablo Neruda Centennial Festival's 10th Anniversary a night of poetry, music, film and everything Neruda. The evening will kick off with readings from poets central to Neruda's works including visual artist and curator Adrián Arias, WordParty Poetry and Jazz Open Mic host Jennifer Barone, editor of City Lights' The Essential Neruda Mark Eisner, WordParty co-founder Ingrid Keir, noted Neruda translator William O’Daly, translator and editor Barbara Paschke and acclaimed poet Michael Warr. This event will also feature a live performance from Quijeremá, who performed the soundtrack to the documentary-in-progress, Pablo Neruda: The Poet’s Calling. New selected scenes from the film will be screened, as well as bonus material starring Lawrence Ferlinghetti.

rnrnMark Eisner is the president of Red Poppy, a non-profit dedicated to promoting the power of Latin American poetry to evoke emotions and foster social consciousness. In 2004, he collaborated with his friend Todd Brown, who had founded the Portfolio-IS-Mission Art House in 2003 (which would later become the Red Poppy Art House). On July 12, 2004, what would have been Neruda’s 100th birthday, Mark was interviewed and read Neruda’s poetry on NPR’s Morning Edition. That night Red Poppy threw what the San Francisco Chronicle called “a perfect birthday party” to celebrate the centennial of the legendary Chilean poet and social justice activist, Pablo Neruda. Poetry readings by the likes of Lawrence Ferlinghetti and Robert Hass, music by Quijeremá, and the premier of an award-winning documentary that now serves as the seed for the more ambitious The Poet’s Calling. A week-long festival followed, featuring art, multi-disciplinary performances including Protest Tango led by the Red Poppy Art House’s founder Todd Brown, local school children reading poetry they translat

title: Prasant Radhakrishnan & Kavita Shah
description:
rnrnrnOn this evening the Red Poppy Art House will present two full sets of music by different artists, bridging the worlds of South Indian Classical music and Jazz. Two concerts in one, the first being focused in traditional South Indian Carnatic music, and the second in jazz.rnrnSET lrn

rnPRASANT RADHAKRISHNAN & ROHAN KRISHNAMURTHYrnrnPrasant Radhakrishnan is a versatile saxophonist identified with both the South Indian Classical (Carnatic) and jazz disciplines. The unique vocal texture of his sound on saxophone, noted for its expressive complexity and rhythmic ingenuity, reflects Prasant’s continued study of tradition, constant innovation, and vast concert experience over the past fifteen years.rnrnThe foremost disciple of Carnatic saxophone pioneer, “Padmashri” Dr. Kadri Gopalnath, Prasant began developing his music over the course of nearly a decade of intensive musical training under his guru, much of which took place in the traditional gurukulam format of complete immersion. Waking at 5am each morning in his teacher’s home and practicing through the day under his guidance, Radhakrishnan was initiated into the world of Carnatic music in a format seldom available today in India, much less in his native Arizona. The rigor of this training, followed by the privilege of accompanying his teacher on stage in hundreds of concerts in subsequent years, fostered a deep understanding of Carnatic music.rnrnAcclaimed an “international mridangam performer” by USA Today and “pride of India” by India's leading newspaper, The Times of India, Dr. Rohan Krishnamurthy is considered a musical ambassador. Having initially received mridangam training with Damodaran Srinivasan over the telephone in the U.S., he continued advanced training from maestro, Guruvayur Dorai, in India. Rohan has performed hundreds of concerts internationally since the age of nine as a distinguished soloist and collaborator in diverse music and dance ensembles.rnrnPrasant Radhakrishnan - saxophonernRohan Kr

description:
Sultry R&B Meets Contemporary Soul

rn Californian turned New Yorker, Gillian Harwin is a true performer whose soulful sounds will be sure to make you get up so you can get down! This vocalist, multi-instumentalist, composer and entertainer brings back the old school R&B groove with her saucy, sultry band, Reverse Double Clutch. Winner of amateur night at the world famous Apollo Theater, Gillian has been taking New York by storm and will be releasing her new album, "Lost n' Found" in summer 2014. title: Ila Cantor & Jacob Aranda Band
description:
A Night of Intimate, Evocative Songwriters

rnCome join us for Ila Cantor's CD Release, Highlights of the Grey, as well as a separate performance by Jacob Aranda Band. Together, these two songwriters promise a night of soulful and personal music.rnrnIla Cantor is a guitarist, composer, and singer/songwriter who has performed worldwide. Highlights of the Grey is her third original album. Cantor's emotional songwriting takes unusual and beautiful turns captivating her listeners. Throughout her career, Cantor has received grants from the Van Lier Fellowship, as well as the Open Arts Foundation for Merit in Composition. Cantor resides in the Bay Area, where she performs, writes, teaches, and records music.rnrnJacob Aranda is a singer/songwriter from Chicago who has recently moved to San Francisco. Outside of songwriting, Aranda also plays guitar, banjo, mandolin, and pedal steel with several bands in the Bay Area. His music is captivating and emotionally enchanting, while conveying a sense of joy. Aranda resides in the Outer Sunset performing, writing and building guitars at Woodshop.rnrnIla Cantor Performers:rnrnIla Cantor - Guitar/VocalsrnCathlene Pineda - PianornChris Higgins - BassrnTommy Crane -DrumsrnrnJacob Aranda Band:rnrnJacob Aranda - Guitar/VocalsrnPaula Frazer - Percussion/VocalsrnDavid Cuetter - Pedal steelrnrnJoin the Facebook event. title: Onda Brasileira Featuring Sonia Caltvedt and Eduardo Corzo
description:
Music of Master Brazilian Composers Gismonti, Jobim, and Pascoal

rn Join flutist Sonia Caltvedt and pianist Eduardo Corzo as they explore the music of influential Brazilian composers Gismonti, Jobim, Pascoal and others. The pair will be accompanied by Eric Garland on drums and joined by other surprise guests. Sonia Caltvedt is a classically trained flutist who fell in love with Latin music a little over a decade ago. Since then, she has performed in the flamenco-latin ensemble Potingue, bossa nova quintet Bossa Five-0, Brazilian pop band Brazilvox, original tango ensemble Scala Tympani, Carlos Oliveira’s Northeast Brazilian ensemble, Kata-vento and the newly formed forro band, Porreta. Sonia is the Marketing Director for Community Music Center, a non-profit music school and performance space based in the Mission District of San Francisco.rn

rn

rnEduardo Corzo is a pianist, guitarist and clarinetist from Havana, Cuba. He has composed and arranged music for Cuban and Mexican television, served as musical director of the Conjunto Nacional de Espectaculos, and toured throughout Latin America. Living in Mexico during the 90’s, he played with an incredible array of musicians including Lila Downs and Oriente Lopez to name a few. Corzo is an artist with a broad vision, interested in genres and styles different from his own, and whose background includes a mix of everything from Bach partitas to Brazilian and Afro Cuban sounds.rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Naomi Wachira
description:
A Folk Soloist on her African Girl, African Woman Summer Tour

rnThe Red Poppy Art House is pleased to present "The Best Folk Singer" in Seattle as deemed by Seattle Weekly. Since Naomi's appearance on the cover of Seattle Weekly, she's teamed with the much-loved indie songwriter Damien Jurado to produce her debut album. When you listen to Naomi, you’ll hear the influence of two powerful, groundbreaking female songwriters: Miriam Makeba and Tracy Chapman. Naomi croons with a feminine mystique similar to that of India Arie, singing about the journey to follow one's heart, with which every human can relate. Naomi’s songs, inspired both by the sounds she discovered in America and what she grew up with in Kenya, tell stories that are imbued with a sense of hopefulness.rnrn Naomi Wachira - Guitar and VocalsrnrnListen to Naomi’s entire album for free: naomiwachira.com/listen/rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Diego Garcia “EL TWANGUERO”
description:
Latino-Twang and American Roots Music

rnLatin Grammy winner, songwriter, and guitarist Diego García "El Twanguero" presents his acclaimed new album blending Latino-Twang & American roots music: "Argentina Songbook” (Warner Music). This Spanish guitar virtuoso and pioneer of the Latin-Twang genre that inspired his nickname has played this unique style of music all around the world. El Twanguero has shared stages with acclaimed latin artists such as Juanes, Calamaro, Fito Paez, Bunbury, Diego El Cigala, Mercedes Sosa, and much more. The late Gabriel Garcia Marquez proclaimed him as a “guitar master.”rn rnrn"...Original and fascinating...Diego creates new standards with a unique language. " - Rolling Stone (Argentina)rnExperience Latino-Twang herernrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.

title: Katy Stephan
description:
Brewing Stories & Drinking Songs

rnKaty returns to the Poppy for a night of wild tales and beguiling serenades. Combining theater, music and movement, this special Monday evening performance will include Katy's intricate handcrafted songs. Katy's sweet and salty songs will feature characters brought to life by Bay Area theater veteran Robert Parsons, known for his spellbinding performances, and by the enchanting Marissa Keltie. Expect a night of stories and songs, myths, larks, tragicomic fascinations.rnrn rnrnCheck out the Katy's last performance at the Poppy.rnrnAlso get a sneak peek of what she's truly all about at her website.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Antique’s Worksuite and Antique Naked Soul
description:
A Beatbox-sing soul revival

rn Come explore your personal potential to be an influence as an artist or educator in Antique's Worksuite and stay for a live performance by Antique Naked Soul's LIVE beatbox-sing soul!rnrnOakland Indie Award and Oaktown Music Festival nominee Candice “Antique” Wicks is the Founder and President of Antique Music, a music edu-tainment company encompassing her work as an educator, with collaborative music projects. Antique is the lead singer of an all-vocal beat-boxing band called Antique Naked Soul (ANS) with renown beat-boxer Tommy “Soulati” Shepherd and two singers, Jayme Brown and N’gala McCoy. As artist and educator, Antique has led personal and professional development workshops for educators all over the world. Her “worksuites” combine her live performance with her expertise as an educator.rn rnrn"Compared to how stagnant the hip-hop soul scene has been recently, their rebellious and distinct style is certainly a breath of fresh air." -Kurofune Crew, Japan rnrn rnrnrnAntique Naked Soul is:rnrnAntique - lead singer, vocal horns and guitar, work suite facilitatorrnMC Solati - beatboxer, Vocal DJrnN'gala - background singer, vocal horns and guitarrnJayme - background singer, vocal guitar and keysrnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15 for worksuite and performance. $20-30 for bothrnrnTime: 4:30pm-6pm: Worksuite 7:30pm-10pm: Live Performancernrn

title: The Arsonists and The Jon Hatamiya Quintet
description:
A Night of Fervent (Post) Jazz

rn Describing themselves as a "post-jazz shred fest" with promised results of "ecstatic eardrum immolation", the Arsonists' energy and intensity are immediately clear in both mentality and performance. Melding East Coast jazz conservatory training with an open minded and passionate approach to composition, they fuse together a diverse array of influences including contemporary jazz, neo-Caribbean dancehall, and funk-metal grooves. The unified sound emerging from these disparate influences is at once idiosyncratic and organic, and above all extremely powerful.rnrnPatrick Bartley - Alto Saxophone, and Nintendo DSrnXavier Del Castillo - Tenor SaxophonernJulian Garvue - Piano and KeyboardrnMat Muntz - Double BassrnMax Holmberg - DrumsrnrnSharing the night, Northern California native and New York based trombonist/composer Jon Hatamiya leads his quintet featuring some of the finest up-and-coming instrumentalists of Sacramento and the Bay Area. Emphasizing original compositions, strong musical interaction, and heavy grooves, Hatamiya and his quintet mix jazz with rock and electronica for a high energy musical experience.rnrnJon Hatamiya - TrombonernLucas Del Calvo - GuitarrnNick Lamb - Piano/KeysrnAndy McCauley - Acoustic/Electric BassrnColin McDaniel - DrumsrnrnCheck out the Arsonists' newly released album "The Red Planet"rnCheck out more performances by Jon Hatamiya.rnJoin the event on Facebook.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn rnrn

title: Stringquake & Amy LaCour EP Release with Misha Khalikulov
description:
Soulful Folk And A Boundary Melting Trio

rnAn internationally recognized trio, StringQuake pushes musical boundaries with its dynamic arrangements and unique harp/percussion/cello instrumentation. The trio has toured Mexico and performed extensively in the Bay Area, releasing their first album, "Take 15," in summer 2013. Nourished by the musical traditions of the Americas, Greece, Africa, India, and Europe, StringQuake's original repertoire is served with a copious amount of improvisation and unusual rhythms that will leave listeners clamoring for more.rnrnAmelia Romano - electric lever harprnMisha Khalikulov - cellornJosh Mellinger - percussionrnrn rnrnrnrn rnrnVocalist Amy LaCour celebrates the release of her new EP, "Yes!," in collaboration with Bay Area cellist Misha Khalikulov. The duo began performing in late 2013, quickly discovering a musical chemistry. Khalikulov brings years of classical training and improvisational experience to Amy’s folk and blues-based songwriting, creating a spare and intimate setting that highlights musicianship, voice, and song.rnrn Amy LaCour - vocalsrnMisha Khalikulov - cellornrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Ensemble Mik Nawooj
description:
Ensemble Mik Nawooj returns to the Red Poppy to present C.R.E.A.M., Shame on a Nigga and Wu-Tang Clan Ain’t Nothing ta Fuck Wit from Enter the Wu-Tang (36 Chambers), and Gin & Juice from Doggystyle. This concert continues their Artist Residency in which EMN will do a partial premiere of a 6 piece commission for Yerba Buena Center for the Arts. EMN music director, composer, and pianist JooWan Kim will re-imagine 6 classic hip-hop songs from 1993 for YBCA's "Clas/sick Hiphop II: 93’ Til" celebration in November. Vocalist, composer, and Los Angeles native, Tiffany possesses a soulful and subtle delivery, has graced the stages of SFJAZZ, the Healdsburg Jazz Festival, and the Yerba Buena Gardens Festival.

title: La Familia Peña-Govea
description:
Dance to cumbia, merengue, ranchera, valse, danzone and more!

rn La Familia Peña-Govea is a band of four family members who have played music together for three decades. They have lovingly shared their music at countless weddings, baptisms, festivals, and birthdays, both locally in the Bay Area and beyond, as well as more formal venues like Davies Symphony Hall. They share their music with the simple intention of bringing people together and making them smile and dance. La Familia Peña-Govea brought this spirit to San Quentin State Prison and AIDS ward patients at Laguna Honda Hospital. Come join them for a night of festivity and dance!rnrnMiguel Govea - accordion, trumpet, vocals, bassrnSusan Peña - guitar, vocalsrnRene Peña-Govea - accordion, bass, vocalsrnCecilia Peña-Govea - guira, trumpet, cajonrnSergio Duran - congas/percussion, vocalsrnrn rn"A cultural force in the Bay Area" (Jesse "Chuy" Varela, Latin Beat Magazine)rn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Chutzpah!: The Musical by Rainbow Geffner
description:
Original script reading

rnA San Francisco Premier: A fundraiser against domestic violence

rn rnrnRainbow Geffner has performed original theatrical works ensembles like A Travelling Jewish Theater, Teatro Kismet O.P.E.R.A, Dell’Arte International School of Physical Theatre, and Touchstone Theatre. Her writing credits include a solo show called Emily: On Clerical Errors and Destiny and Tales From the Tub which she wrote, directed and produced in 2008.rnrnHer latest work, Chutzpah!, is an irreverent musical comedy about a Jewish girl from Long Island who runs away from home and gets a job at a Manhattan deli. No sooner does she learn the difference between a kishka and a tuches that she discovers the deli is a front for a burlesque club run by Orthodox Jews! Will she make it as a burlesque dancer? Will she make it with the club's DJ? Will she reconcile with her family? Or will her idea to use her father's new polysaccharide as a tool for sexual liberation be the answer to a repressed society's dysfunctionality? A smart and funny look at modern Jewish values and the sexual hypocrisies behind the traditions, come and join us for a preview through an original script reading.rnrn www.rainbowgeffner.comrnrnJoin the official Facebook event rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn rnrn

title: MAPP (Mission Arts Performance Project)
description:
Since 2003, Mission Arts and Performance Project (MAPP) has produced 63 neighborhood pop-up art happenings of intimate spaces, all without ever formalizing into a non-profit organization, seeking funding, nor establishing a formal staff structure. Across these 10 years, the MAPP has featured thousands of artists, helping to cross-pollinate ideas and launch careers of artists engaged in social issues. It is a kind of otherworldly art walk, not centered on commercial corridors but within intimate spaces where the magic of genuine connectivity transpires among people across communities and art forms.rnrnMAPP stands as a testament to the possibility of horizontal organizing among artists intent on building social fabric within our respective communities, as much to celebrate diversity and creativity as to counter the erosion of these qualities that occurs when development displaces artists and minority communities alike.rnrn MAPP Websiternrn Always free and open to all ages!rnrn rnrnMAPP schedule at the Red Poppy Art Housernrn rn In Dedication To Alex Nieto

rn rnrnOn Friday evening, March 21, 2014, Alejandro “Alex” Nieto, 28 years old, was shot at over fourteen times and killed by the San Francisco Police Department, on Bernal Hill Park, without justification.rnrn rnrn DAYTIME FAMILY MAPP ACTIVITIESrnrn1PM-4PMrnrnPuppetry and family painting activities with Sofia Elias and the Family Art crewrnrn rnrnON EXHIBITIONrnrn rnrnInstallation "Home/Studio: Eviction Scene Investigation" by Yolanda M. Lopez and Adriana CamarenarnrnPaintings by Paz de la Calzada, Alberto Toscano, and Todd Thomas Brown.rnrn rnrnPRESENTATIONS & PERFORMANCErnrn rnrn6:00PM: Informal presentation and conversation with writer/installation artist Adriana Camarenarnrn7:01PM: Adapted dance/theater excerpt from Teobi's Dreaming, with Kenya Moses, Todd Thomas Brown, and Camille Mairnrn7:30ish: Poetry Michael Warr rnrn7:51PM: Poetry D. Scott Miller of AfroSurrealrnrn8:16PM: Music/Song by Amy LaCour rnrn8:42P

title: Jeff Denson Trio
description:
An embrace of traditional Jazz and new innovations

rnAs one of jazz’s premier bassists Jeff Denson is always looking for situations that allow for maximum creative freedom, like his long-running gig with alto sax legend Lee Konitz, or the collective trio Minsarah. One of his two new duo albums, "I’ll Fly Away" which he worked on with Joshua White, who he met in 2005 studying at UC San Diego, showcases his virtuosity and exquisite musicality while providing a fascinating window into his vast range as an improviser. Joining Joshua White and Jeff Denson will  be Jon Arkin, a Berkeley based drummer/percussionist who is also an active music educator as a faculty member for the Berkeley Jazzschool and the Stanford Jazz Workshop.

rnJeff Denson - double bass & vocalsrnJoshua White - pianornJon Arkin - drumsrn rnrn"Never satisfied to merely accept the role of a session player and with a forward thinking vision to expand his horizons as a leader, Jeff Denson is quickly moving up the improvisational food chain as one of the preeminent bassists in world." -Brent Black - Bop-N-Jazz www.criticaljazz.comrnrn rn For more information about the Jeff Denson Trio see their website and visit the Poppy FB event to RSVP.

rnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30. Tickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: My Pet Dragon
description:
Frequently hailed by critics and fans for their songwriting and live performances, My Pet Dragon features husband-wife duo, Todd Michaelsen & Reena Shah, from Brooklyn. Their melodic and occasionally anthemic songs are uniquely presented using a variety of looping instruments, vocals, and beats. The duo collaborated on writing the original music score and voice overs for the award-winning animated feature film, “Sita Sings the Blues,” by Nina Paley. My Pet Dragon offers upbeat melodies and cheerful lyrics, evoking clear skies and sunny days!

description: iiii, which Anthony Dean-Harris, Editor in Chief of nextbop.com recently reviewed as “a group of pure radiance,” mixes jazz training with pop and R&B influences to forge a new and captivating sound. description:
Sorry for any inconvenience, A Potent Mix of Love & Grace is cancelled for this date.

rnSri & Vibha are two designers who grew up in India with families that loved and obsessed over chai. After a desperate search of something similar in America, the two finally decided the only way to replicate the chai experience was to brew their own. And they did just that, pairing it with some of their favorite activities and sharing this experience with their friends. Tonight, come enjoy live music with a cup of chai!rnVisit Sri & Vibha's website here.

title: Afro Cuban Jazz Cartel
description:
Celebrate Carnaval with Latin Jazz and Spoken Word

rnThis night is presented as part of Carnaval San Francisco, the largest multi-cultural celebration on the West Coast. The Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel will feature music from their 2013 release, "San Francisco", an homage to San Francisco's rich Latin musical heritage that boasts innovative arrangements and novel rhythmic grooves. The result is a fiery creation that reflects the melting pot identity of Latin-Jazz's African, Caribbean and North American roots. The Cartel will be complemented by the "Nuyorican" - a New Yorker and Puerto Rican - soul and blues infused poetry of Sandra Garcia Rivera.rnrn rnrnrnrn rnrnThe Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel is:rnrnBrian Andres - drumkitrnJavier Cabanillas - congas and percussionrnAlex Conde - pianornAaron Germain - bassrnHenry Hung - trumpetrnrnCheck out the Afro-Cuban Jazz Cartel online.rnJoin the official event on Facebook.rnrnAdmission: $10-15. title: The Jimmy Grant Ensemble
description:
A fresh take on the music of Django Reinhardt

rn The Jimmy Grant Ensemble performs primarily in the style of Django Reinhardt, infusing swing era pieces, original work, and traditional gypsy tunes with the jazz guitar pioneer's crunchy rhythms and hot harmonies. Jimmy Grant was recently a finalist in the John Lennon songwriting competition for his original song "Insomnia," and also had an interview with Guitar World for his "5 in 5" project, in which he wrote five different songs in five days. His style synthesizes a rich blend of Russian folk, bluegrass, celtic and classical music while remaining true to Django's innovative guitar technique. The Jimmy Grant Ensemble will bring together two guitars, bass, and a special guest in an intimate yet energy-charged performance.rnrnTommy Davy (Trio Dinicu) - GuitarrnJamie Mathers - Bassrnwith Special GuestrnrnFor more information about The Jimmy Grant Ensemble, please visit JimmyGrant.netrnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Nahuel Bronzini & Friends
description:
The String Quartet Experience

rnProducer and composer Nahuel Bronzini joins forces with local musicians, taking the audience on a sonic journey through classical, pop, jazz, and Argentine folk as they embrace the compelling sound of the string quartet. Nahuel has been arranging music for various songwriters in San Francisco the past recent years, and his own original music explores themes of nostalgia and longing that navigates sonorities from both the contemporary classical work as well a jazz and pop culture music. For this special performance, Nahuel and Friends will feature the following selections for the string quartet and other chamber ensembles:rnrnCarlos Guastavino – Presencia N 6rnNahuel Bronzini – Aquello Que ExtrañarrnNahuel Bronzini – Variations on Aquello que ExtrañarrnAriel Ramirez – Dorotea La CautivarnNahuel Bronzini – Winter SongrnrnIntermissionrnrnAdryan Symcox – War, FirstrnJohn Haesemeyer – Bright White, Colors in BetweenrnKendra McKinley – Poor BoyrnCamille Mai – We are no Beautiful ChildrenrnAstor Piazzolla – Balada para un Loco, Morire en Buenos AiresrnrnNahuel and Friends are...rnrnNahuel Bronzini – guitar, piano, composition, artistic directionrnCamille Mai – songwriter, pianornKendra McKinley – songwriter, guitarrnJohn Haesemeyer – songwriter, guitarrnAdrian Symcox – songwriter, guitarrnClaudio Santome – tenorrnSchuyler Karr - upright bassrnGabriel Rodrick – pianornAaron Kierbel – percussionrnrnString Quartet:rnrnAzat Fyshian – violinrnEric Aurenbach - violinrnSarah Lee – violarnHelen Newby – cellornrn rnrnWatch KPFA interview with Nahuel here.rnrnVisit Nahuel's website here, and watch a sneak peek here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnTo volunteer for this event click here. rnrn

title: Yahir Duran
description: An outstanding Mexican composer and musician, Yahir Duran has been influenced by Mexican and Cuban folklore. Yahir studied sacred music at the Escuela Superior de Musica of Guadalajara, and has performed at Palacio de Bellas Artes, CENART, and Festival Cervantino, among others. His virtuosity on guitar and piano will take you on an amazing journey. Yahir's breadth of experience and wide-ranging influences make his music an extraordinary experience.rnrnEl canata autor Yahir Duran es uno de los representantes más notables de la nueva tova Mexicana. inició sus estudios en la escuela Superior de Musica de Guadalajara; su material discografico incluye discimbolos, acerca de sonar, una cascarita en directo, plural espejo, asi y hay un hombre que camina, ademas como escritor de narrativa cuenta con un trabajo literario en forma de relatos y cronicas en el cual en tono autobiografico narra su infancia en torno a su natal Topolobampo. Yahir Duran ha sido condecorado con el primer lugar en diferentes premios de canto y composicion en su natal Mexico. Se ha presentado en los recitos mas importantes de Mexico, como lo son el Palacio de Bellas Artes, CNART y el Festival Cervantino de Guanajuato. Su musica ha llegado hasta las ciudades de San Francisco y Los Angeles California.rnrnMore at: myspace.com/yahirduranrnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Shoestring Trio
description:
Gypsy jazz and French classics playfully rediscovered!

rnFull of spontaneity, virtuosity and energy, the Shoestring Trio has crafted a style all their own that's fun and contagious to music lovers across the globe. A band composed of musical brothers from other mothers, the Trio has taken their music from the streets of Italy to festivals in the South of France. Armed with an arsenal of strings and woodwinds, Shoestring Trio's "youthful exuberance and consummate musicianship” (Marin Independent Journal) will take you on a musical journey from sunny afternoons in Paris to midnight parties in the Hollywood Hills. In anticipation of recording their second album, they've assembled a joyous collection of originals, French classics, and distinctive twists on pop songs.rnrnRobby Marshall - clarinet, sax, flute, cajónrnAntoine Salem - guitarrnMichael Papillo - upright bassrnrnShoestring Trio on the webrnListen to Shoestring Trio Puttin' On The Ritzrnrn rnrnAdmission: $12-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Sparlha Swa and Zéna
description:
Join us a night of smooth melodies and West African influences with interdisciplinary vocalists and instrumentalists Sparlha Swa and Zéna on a special double bill.rnrn Sparlha Swa offers folk/soul/alternative music that is honest and evocative. Mixing impeccable vocals and haunting chords her songs speak to and about the human spirit. Born in Kingston, Jamaica, the artist pulls her inspiration from around the globe with mentors like Sade, Billie Holiday, Astrud Gilberto, Miriam Makeba and Ravi Shankar. Notable musical landmarks include landing a lead role in the critically-acclaimed Off-Broadway piece Fela! Directed by Bill T. Jones. The show won 3 Lucille awards, 5 Audelco awards, and has been nominated for Drama Desk as well. In an alternate universe, Sparlha works as a psychotherapy intern in Berkeley, and is at work on her 4th album Moongazing which incorporates story, imagery and song in a magical allegory of the soul. For this show, Sparlha will perform alongside vocalist Aarthi Belani.rnrnSparlha Swa - guitar & vocalsrnAarthi Belani - vocalsrnrn rn"Smashing" - The New York Timesrn rnrnCheck out Sparlha on the webrnrn rn

rn rnrn Zéna is a Bay-area visual and performing artist and a student of Jaliyaa, the West African musical and oral tradition with a specialization in kora (West African harp), Zéna's interdisciplinary background has allowed her to explore a seamless application of the same themes found in the music of the African Diaspora within her visual art. Blending storytelling with song, Zéna draws on influences from her time living in the Caribbean, Latin America, and East and West Africa; using her harp as an anchor to ground the music in classical West African styles.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Irene Diaz
description:
Original Soul and Blues

rnTrained in classical piano as a child, Irene Diaz sprung onto the Los Angeles music scene in late 2011, starting in small coffee shops before making her way into well known LA venues like Room 5 and Hollywood's House of Blues. Since then she has risen to national attention, airing on NPR and raising $10,000 through Kickstarter, which allowed her to fund her "I Love You Madly EP" as well as an East Coast tour. Diaz's voice is deep and mournful, adding darkness to a clear piano, guitar and string accompaniment while maintaining a certain playful twist. Her performance at the Poppy promises to be soulful and rife with emotion.rnrn Irene Diaz — piano, voicernCarolyn Cardoza — ukulele, guitarrnrnCheck out Irene Diaz online.rnrnThe schedule for the night is as follows:rnrn6:30 PM Doors open for first show SOLD OUTrnrn7:00PM First show begins, 70 min no intermissionrnrn8:20PM First show ends, crowd's change!rnrn8:45 Second show begins, 70 min no intermissionrnrnAdmission: $12-15.rnrnrnAdvance tickets available through info@labohemiaproductions.comrnrnrnrnrn

description:
Performing authentic Tango and Folk music from Argentina

rnTrio Garufa is an international ensemble dedicated to performing authentic Argentine music. They have performed across Argentina, Canada, Colombia and the US. Trio Garufa performs traditional Argentine tango, original tango compositions, Argentine folk music and modern tango styles such as Astor Piazzolla's music and electro tangos. title: Workshop: Writing and Painting with the Ancestors
description: Taught by Todd Thomas BrownrnrnOne thing that is certain is that we all have ancestors. In fact, we are made of them, our biologies suffused with their characters and qualities. This 6-hour workshop is the first of a new series that Todd will be teaching in coming months, relating artistic practice to investigations of self and ancestry. With exercises based on his last three years’ work on an interdisciplinary project called Teobi’s Dreaming, this workshop is designed to combine writing practices with mixed media painting techniques as a way of inquiring into the complexities of our individual lives beyond the narrow definitions by which we are often defined. The workshop will involve varied writing exercises, the results of which are introduced into the painting surface and continued through successive layers. Todd will be offering this same workshop again on May 24th.rnrnFor more info, please visit Todd's website here, or enroll directly via Studio Teobi Meet-Uprnrn

title: Red Reels Present : La Pêche's Journey Through the Impossible
description: The Red Poppy is pleased to present a unique musical extravaganza delivered by La Peche, a new group of young bay area musicians. This show will include a 30 minute introductory set, setting a relaxing and mellow mood, followed by the film screening, a Q&A, an intermission and ending with a more upbeat, dancing set. The night will be centered around George Melies 1904 silent film "Journey Through the Impossible," and woven together by the three faces of the group’s music: the soul of traditional balkan music, the freedom of jazz, and the energy of hard rock.rnrnThese young artists bring a sound that is at the crossroads of European folkloric traditions and improvised performance. “La Pêche" is a French expression which refers to a heightened physical and spiritual state - a state which the group aims to convey with their wild improvisations, deep soundscapes and fiery compositions. Friday’s performance will be the result of a month long compositional workshop, where La Pêche will play a live original soundtrack along a projection of George Melies’ film, gifting the audience an elevated supersensory spectacle.rnrnLa Pêche is…rnrnPaul Bertin - SaxophonernMorgan Nilsen - ClarinetrnOfir Uziel - AccordionrnSchuyler Karr - Upright BassrnJonJon Selin - DrumsrnrnCheck out La Pêche on Facebook.rnListen to the extraordinary soundrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Derek Lassiter
description: This relaxing, deep, and honest show features new songs from writer and composer Derek Lassiter with arrangements by Art Khu. Lassiter’s music, a blend of multiple genres including folk and soul is deeply personal yet accessible. Lassiter sings of the human experience-love, pain, hunger- in a way so honest that everyone can relate. Art Khu’s melodies are sweet and climactic and paired with the authenticity of Lassiter’s vocals, you will feel a mellow sense of inspiration and enlightenment.rnrnDerek Lassiter - vocalsrnArt Khu - pianornSam Bevan - bassrnEric Garland - drumsrnLuke Schwartz - guitarrnrnDerek’s homepagernVisit Derek on Facebook and join the official Facebook eventrnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. title: Betany Coffland and Aaron Larget-Caplan
description: Acclaimed musicians, mezzo-soprano Betany Coffland and classical guitarist Aaron Larget-Caplan will explore French, Spanish, English and Chinese cultures through the program "Passion and Thought in Song". The artists' music pulls from three centuries including lute songs and a cantata by John Dowland and G.F. Handel, love songs and dances by Reynaldo Hahn and Manuel De Falla and sounds of Eastern philosophies through Benjamin Britten’s Song for the Chinese.rnrnBetany Coffland - mezzo-sopranornrnAaron Caplan-Larget - guitarrnrnOpera News has hailed Mezzo-soprano, Betany Coffland, as “extremely fine” and “a joy to watch,” and the San Francisco Classical Voice described her voice as "plush" and "Italianate".rnrnAaron Larget-Caplan has been called, a "riveting artist whose musical styling begs immediate attention" by The Washington Post and his performances have been described as "stunningly played" by Fanfare Magazine.rnrnVisit Betany's site, or listen to her enchanting voice.rnrnAlso visit Aaron's site here, or get a taste for his mastery here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. description: Drawing from her diverse heritage, chanteuse and songwriter Jessica Fichot writes original French chanson, gypsy jazz, and various folk traditions. title: Khmer Arts Salon
description:
Developed as a prayer in movement for the deliverance of rain, renewal, and fertility, Khmer classical dance is an art form that is more than 1,000 years old. This intimate presentation of the Khmer Arts Salon Series features a program designed to introduce audiences to this venerable tradition. Led by Khmer Arts associate artistic director Prumsodun Ok, audiences will explore the dance's history and vocabulary through lecture, demonstration, and live performance.rnrnFor more information about Khmer Arts, please visit khmerarts.orgrnrnrnAdmission: Free

rnDoors at 7pm, Salon at 7:30pm

title: Tim Snider and House of Waters
description:
Music that Reflects Our Times

rnViolinist and singer-songwriter, Tim Snider has traveled the world studying new and different styles of music. He’s teaming up with the Brooklyn-based band House of Waters to present a show that has been described as a blend of multinational "folk aimed at the heart, the head, and the feet.” All virtuosos in their own right with impressive performance credentials, House of Waters consists of Max ZT, Luke Notary and Moto Fukushima. The upbeat and harmonious blends that House of Waters produces align magnificently with Snider's uplifting lyrical stories and nuanced violin. Together, their unique style is a product of intense collaboration between experts who take risks, cross boundaries, and never cease to amaze.rnrnTim Snider - guitar, violin, and vocalsrnMax ZT - hammered dulcimerrnLuke Notary - percussionrnMoto Fukushima - six string electric bassrnrn rn"Globe-spanning sounds, classical chops" Portland MercuryrnExplore Tim Snider's soundsrnrnCheck out House of Watersrnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Bay Tones Trio
description:
Jazz, Swing, Bebop, Piano Trio, Interplay

rn Bay Tones Trio was first created when Ollie Dudek, Omar Aran, and Grant Levin shared the serendipitous experience of providing joy through music to elderly residents at the Broadmoor Hotel. Since then, the three have worked together intimately to learn to create just the right balance that a piano trio format requires: each instrumentalist providing their own unique voice while producing a unified sound. Only two years old, the trio sound is fresh and playful while still honoring old jazz traditions. Bay Tones Trio has played throughout the Bay Area and recorded two albums. For this special evening, the trio will be presenting several original compositions as well as new arrangements of jazz standards.rnrn rnrnGrant Levin - PianornOllie Dudek - BassrnOmar Aran - DrumsrnrnCheck out Bay Tones website here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. title: Los Boleros
description: Join Los Boleros for a night of hot havana heat at the Red Poppy! In a time of the modern salsa band Los Boleros stays true to the traditional Cuban music styles of an era when music was golden. You'll get a taste of traditional salsa music, traditional Cuban music, Cuban son, cumbia, merengue, cha cha, danzon, vals (waltz) and yes of course... bolero music. Imagine dinner with Ricky Ricardo's Tropicana Havana then dancing...at the Buena Vista Social Club!rnrnLed by Rudy Furlan on the tres Cubano and the powerful vocals of Felix Samuel, Los Boleros is a show unlike any other. Felix is a singing giant from Havana, Cuba who was immediately picked up by HBO to perform in the movie "Hemingway and Gelhorn" immediately after moving to the United States Year. Los Boleros' show has sold out monthly at the prestigious Mountain Winery, even amongst the other top names performing there. This will be their debut appearance at The Red Poppy and should not be missed!rnrnFelix Samuel - lead vocalsrnRudy Furlan - tres CubanornDavid Somers - tenor sax and fluternDominic Cabrera - congasrnEdgar Lavado - bassrnrn rn"Considered by many critics to be one of the best Latin bands in the Bay Area." - Jean Bartlett, Pacifica Tribune Arts Coordinatorrn rnrnLos Boleros on the webrnrnLos Boleros on YouTubernrnJoin the official event on Facebookrnrn rnrnAdmission: $15-20. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.

title: Lavay Smith: Two Shows!
description:
A Billy Holiday Birthday Celebration

rnLavay Smith sings the Billie Holiday Songbook

rn As a recreation of Lavay Smith's four-night residency in the Joe Henderson Lab at SFJazz, Smith is joined by a four piece version of her world-class band, led by pianist Chris Siebert, Danny Armstrong on Trombone, Ron Belcher on bass, and featuring the legendary Jules Brossard on sax (Brossard has played with Ray Charles, Big Mama Thorton and Eddie Cleanhead Vinson.). Ms.Smith and her band will pay tribute to Lady Day herself, the immortal Billie Holiday, who single-handedly changed popular music by instilling personal life experiences into her lyrics and elevating the art of storytelling in song. Ms.Holiday popularized songs like “Don’t Explain,” “God Bless The Child,” “Lover Man,” “Solitude,” “Strange Fruit” and dozens more. Lavay Smith, along with a band of expert musicians, will bring the songstress back to life at this special Saturday evening at the Red Poppy Art House!rnrnCheck out Lavay Smith online.  rnrnAdmission is $15-20. Doors will open for the first show at 6:30pm, the performance will start at 7pm. rnrnFor the second performance, doors will open at 8:20pm, and the performance will start at approximately 8:40pm.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.

title: Fantastic Negrito
description:
Black Roots Music for Everyone

rnFantastic Negrito is a man's truth told in the form of black roots music. Each song helps from the real story of a musician from Oakland who experienced the highs of a million-dollar record deal, the lows of a near-fatal car accident that put him in a 4-week coma, and is now in a phase of rebirth despite his playing hand being mangled. Negrito's music emphasizes rawness and space. Slide guitar, drums, piano. Rather than update the Delta Blues, Fantastic Negrito leaves the original sounds of Lead Belly and Skip James intact, building bridges to a modern sound with loops and samples of instruments live. But the primary element that drives Fantastic Negrito's music is his uncut realness and zero concern for "pop" anything.rnrnXavier Dphrepaulezz - lead vocalsrnKaz Sazaki - bassrnRuthie Price - drumsrnrnLike Fantastic Negrito on Facebook.rnGet addicted to their sound and enchant your eyes.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30. rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrnrn

Black Roots Music for Everyone

title: Quijeremá
description:
A merging of classical and jazz with poetic undertones

rnAfter 12 years of performances throughout the US, Canada and South America, Quijeremá returns to the Poppy for a night of retrospection and celebration, featuring new work from their upcoming project "El Viaje/The Journey". Their music features traditional instruments and vocal poetry to create a unique style that merges jazz, classical and world idioms. Quijeremá is a 2012-13 California Arts Council grantee, performs worldwide, including special performances for the President of Chile, the Cultural Diversity Festival in Venezuela, and was a 2009 IDA nominee for best musical soundtrack for the PBS documentary “Archeology of Memory.”rnrn rnrnQuique Cruz (Chile) - voice, strings, Andean wind instruments & percussionrnJeremy Allen (USA) - voice, bass & percussionrnMaria Fernanda Acuña (Venezuela) -  voice, percussion & Venezuelan cuatrornShain Carrasco (USA) - Cello)rnJohn Calloway (USA) - piano, flute & percussion)rnrn rn "Using various methods of artistic expression, Quijeremá is more than just a band. They are poets and multi-media designers. Combining sounds from almost all over Latin America, their music contains a multi-cultural vibe that is guaranteed to impress anyone."- SFReMezclarn rnrnGet a sneak peek of Quijerema on Soundcloud.rnrnCheck out their website, here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn rnrnrn

title: Sariyah Idan and The Maccabees
description:
Sariyah Idan, an internationally touring vocalist, songwriter and actor, is jazz trained, hip-hop educated and influenced by music of her Jewish roots. She blends musical styles into an alternative sound seasoned with soul and passionately hopeful longing while riffing on topics ranging from romance to politics. Akin to Nneka and Amy Winehouse, Sariyah takes inspiration from a across the musical spectrum and has shared stages with notable Bay Area artists Aima the Dreamer, La Gente, Jordan and the RituaL and legends Lee “Scratch” Perry and Pete Seeger. The night of March 28th will feature songs from her EP "Deeper Than Skin," other originals and a cover or two.rnrnSariyah Idan - vocals & guitarrnAaron Kierbel - drumsrnJesse Strauss - bassrnrn rn rn"Sariyah Idan creates an eclectic palette of emotionally engaging, wildly imaginative, and fulfilling 'soulful' instincts to craft her own meditations on love and introspection." -Sabrina Okoegwale

rn

title: Teobi's Dreaming: Sunday Performance
description:
A Performative Inquiry into Biology and Being

rnThe 2014 Premier

rn rnrnConceived and Directed by Todd Thomas Brownrnfeaturing Camille Mai, Ellen Oliver, Schuyler Karr, Tiffany Austin, Adrian Arias, and Kenya Moses.rnrnThe Red Poppy Art House is experimenting with its first foray into presenting a multi-performance theater/dance production. We are pleased to announce the production of Teobi's Dreaming - A performative Inquiry into Biology and Being, a new work by Red Poppy Art House founding artist, Todd Thomas Brown. If you've been to the Poppy for any of its exceptional programming in music, you'll encounter a very different environment this time around as the Poppy converts aspects of its space to draw on a black-box theater aesthetic. Teobi's Dreaming represents the first step in this kind of programming as the Poppy seeks to support interdisciplinary and/or experimental works that continue to diversify both the artistic content and the intersection of artistic and cultural communities that convene within its walls. Come see what it's all about!rnrnAbout Teobi's DreamingrnrnTeobi’s Dreaming is a multi-disciplinary theatrical performance that explores questions on human biology, ancestry, and the fleeting moments of spontaneous presence that we experience in our daily lives. Consider how we walk though life confident about certain facts; we know where we live and where we keep our plates, sneakers, and laundry, and yet we are mostly unaware of what goes on inside our bodies in each passing moment. Teobi asks, where is my spleen? Do I know? Is it that I have a body, or does my body have me? Teobi’s Dreaming plays upon the idea that our biology and DNA are not aspects of some biological machinery storing genetic information and code, but rather they represent a continuity of stories, poetry, and dreams, moving forward through time – a bio-poetic organism, the body-being in which we live.rnrnConceived as a “performative inquiry,” during Todd Thomas Brown’s Artist

title: Sureando: Rambling through the South
description:
A Concert Featuring South American Gems Mochi Parra and David Pinto

rnBorn in the rural town of Parral, Chile, Mochi Parra began training as a concert cellist and performer at the age of five. Deeply rooted in Chilean folk songs, her repertoire stretches to embrace musical influences from all over South and Central America as well as the Caribbean. Her captivating voice has been heard in combination with a number of remarkable musicians such as Carlos Hayre, Juan Falu, and Carlos Mejia Godoy.rnrnBerkeley Jazz School teacher David Pinto is internationally known for his work as music director, arranger, and bass player for Grammy-winner Susana Baca. He was a member of Peru Jazz, a group highly praised for their innovation in encompassing Pervian roots. David also leads the group Syncopated Colors.rnrn rnrnrnrn rnrnMochi and David are teaming up to present a concert of South American musical jewels that will set a precedent for the boundless possibilities between the voice and a six-stringed bass. The two gave taste of their beautiful musical dialogue at the Encuentro Festival last December when presenting an arrangement of the late Rafael Manriquez's "The Hand," and were said to have "created an exquisite orchestration." It goes without saying that we are in for a treat!rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Kats and Mano meet the Dragon
description:
Acoustic Reggae, New Beats, and Blue Nights

rnA quartet with an intimate history, Kats and Mano Meet the Dragon have worked together for over three decades, creating original compositions that encompass elements from roots reggae, dancehall, and ska, to rock-jazz and R&B. At the core, their eclectic style focuses on three part harmony, percussive rhythms, and a dual bass line played by two bass players. They are currently working on their third album, which includes only original material. For this special night, KMD will be presenting songs from this new album, prior albums, and renditions of pieces from artists they deeply respect.rnrn rnrnMano Dawes - bass, guitar, and vocalsrnKatsumi Asazawa - lead guitar and vocalsrnThe Dragon - bass, rhythm guitar, and vocalsrnDave Eagle - percussionrnrnCheck out KMD's music here.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn rn

title: Bang Data
description:
rnFast forward with the new sound of Latin Alternative music

rnBang Data was created in early 2008, bringing you a mix of slick and melodic guitar, gritty synth & bass, over banging electro-acoustic rhythms; making this the perfect canvas for Deuce Eclipse’s bilingual rhymes and melodies. Bang Data’s sound is a mash-up of styles combining hints of latin, reggae, hip-hop & rock triggering memories of music we once grew up with yet offering a futuristic vision of latin alternative music. Most recently, Bang Data's self titled single was featured on the Emmy® Award winning television drama Breaking Bad. The song was part Season 4 Episode 10 "Salud."rnrnBang Data on the webrnGet a free download of "Salud" herernRed Poppy Facebook eventrnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-20. title: Acentos Revival
description: In this event, co-founder of the Acentos Bronx Poetry Showcase Fish Vargas unites with the co-founder of The Acentos Review, Raina J. León, and former guest editor, Bonafide Rojas in a powerful reading of new poetry. Acentos has shifted in forms for over 10 years - poetry showcase, foundation, and workshops. In the end Acentos Review creates a supporting environment for Latina and Latino artistic voices. There will be three featured poets and two open mic segments. It will be an event to remember, and the first Acentos event held on the West Coast.rnrnrnrnFish Vargas is a Bronx born Nuyorican poet. He has shared his words and experience at countless poetry venues, fundraisers, festivals, universities throughout NYC and the United States. In 2012, he coached the Bay Area youth team that competed at Brave New Voices to a 4th place finish and in 2013 to a final stage appearance.rnrnrnrnBonafide Rojas has appeared on “Def Poetry Jam” season 4, the documentary “Spitting Ink” and has been published in multiple anthologies and journals. He is the co-bandleader of experimental rock band the Mona Passage & has performed nationally and internationally.rnrnrnrnRaina J. León, CantoMundo fellow, Cave Canem graduate fellow (2006) and member of the Carolina African American Writers Collective, has been published in numerous journals as a writer of poetry, fiction and nonfiction.rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00 Show at 7:30. rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Broken Shadows Family Band
description: rnrnBroken Shadows Family Band is comprised of an all-star cast of musicians from diverse backgrounds; Western European to Arabic classical music, slavic choral music to Americana, classic jazz to the sounds of Macedonia and beyond. They’ve created a truly unique sound, balancing the old with the new, and telling human anecdotes that are ready to be danced to. Originals by trumpeter/composer Darren Johnston are extremely unique and integrate selections of recorded interviews and letters by immigrants in the US. Other selections will include traditional favorites, and surprising and exciting takes on popular covers.rnrnDarren Johnston - trumpet, voicernMatt Szemela - violin, voicernCorinne Sykes - tambura, derbekki, high-hat, voicernFaisal Zedan - riqq, derbekki, voicernDoug Stuart - bass, voicernMarco Peris - tupanrnrn rn“[Darren Johnston is] Someone to watch, on trumpet of course, but as a composer and bandleader as well.”– John Corbett, Downbeat Magazinern rnrnCheck out Broken Shadows Website.rnrnAnd a sneak peek of their music.rnrnJoin the Facebook event page.rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. title: Therianthrope with Ian Faquini & Rebecca Kleinmann
description:
Nylon with Tabla and Guitar with Flute: Two Duos Exploring the Art of Genre Blending

rnTherianthrope is the musical duo led by nylon string guitarist, Dusty Brough and tabla artist, Miles Shrewsbery. The wide spectrum of sounds presented by Therianthrope are inspired by their diverse backgrounds in flamenco, Balkan, Indian classical, jazz, Persian and pop, to name just a few. They unabashedly break down genre barriers while reinterpreting the sounds of today’s jazz and other traditional music. Therianthrope debuted at the Loft in San Diego, CA, where they opened for Charlie Hunter and Scott Amendola on December 5th, 2013. Their much-anticipated first album will be released in early 2014 through Avaaz Records.rnrn rn“[Therianthrope exhibits a] blatant disregard for genre boxes.” -Joe Bendel, Epoch Timesrn rnrnrnrn rnrnBrilliant Brazilian guitarist Ian Faquini and versatile flutist Rebecca Kleinmann present a set featuring Ian's unique original compositions complimented by sweet swinging Brazilian grooves, rich harmonies, beautiful melodies and story-like lyrics. Ian became an accomplished guitarist at an early age, attracting the attention of renowned Brazilian composer Guinga, who became his mentor, friend, and inspiration. Flutist, singer, and composer Rebecca Kleinmann is a musical chameleon equally comfortable in several genres, and with her heart in jazz, Brazilian, and flamenco music. The two musicians have been friends for ten years, and began playing often together since Rebecca moved to Oakland in 2010. This is a duo not to be missed.rnrnTherianthrope on the web.rnrnCheck out Ian Faquini on ReverbNation.rnrnVisit Rebecca Kleinmann.com. rnrn rnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn

title: Redwood Tango Ensemble & Trio Zincalo
description: The Red Poppy Art House is pleased to announce a special line-up featuring two groups of top-notch tango innovators, The Redwood Tango Ensemble and Trio Zincalo. The Redwood Tango Ensemble is one of the Bay Area's busiest tango ensembles, renowned among the dance community for their live performances at milongas (tango dance parties) and concerts. This all-star quintet's sound ranges from traditional tango of the 40's and 50's to original compositions and compelling instrumentation from the more modern tango work of Piazzolla, Sexteto Mayor, New Tango Orquesta and other nuevo-tango from around the world. Redwood Tango has toured the US and Canada extensively in support of their new album Cayetano (2013).rnrnTrio Zincalo transports listeners to a dimly-lit Parisian cafe with their vibrant mix of gypsy jazz, tango, Brazilian choro, and eastern European music. The group specializes in novel and unexpected arrangements of both traditional and popular songs from around the world.rnrnRedwood Tango Ensemble:rnrnCharles Gorczynski - bandoneónrnHolly Mead - pianornKendra Vernon - violinrnCelia Harris - violinrnDaniel Fabricant - double BassrnrnTrio Zincalo:rnrnColin Hogan - accordionrnJon Monahan - guitarrnCelia Harris - violinrnrn rn"[Redwood Tango Ensemble is] Truly one of the most exciting tango ensembles active in the US today" - Morgan Luker, Reed Collegern rnrnRedwood Tango on the webrnrnGet a sneak peak on Youtube herernrnTrio Zincalo on FacebookrnrnAdmission: $10-15. Doors at 7:00pm Show at 7:30.rnrnTickets will not be sold in advance for this event. Please arrive at the Red Poppy when doors open to purchase tickets.rnrn
