title: Clattering Loom: A Seasonal Reading // Torschlusspanik
description: rnrn rnrnGate-Closing Panicrnrn rnrnClattering Loom is a themed reading co-curated by Valerie Chavez and Jonathan Hirsch that asks its participants to explore a word or phrase from another language that doesn't quite translate into English. The German word 'Torschlusspanik' translates literally as 'gate-closing panic' and refers to the sense that opportunities diminish as one ages. The reading will feature a broad spectrum of authors exploring this theme. Litcrawl performers: D.A. Powell, Jesus Angel Garcia, Sloane Martin, Maura Cronin, Doug Cordell. Duende Review & Videos // Mono No Aware Review Litcrawl events are free.

title: Dia de los Muertos: "The Air is Dying"
description:
rn

rn The Air Is Dying - Dia de los Muertos

rnrn rnrnA month long series culminates with a gathering at Red Poppy Art House for Dia de los Muertos following a procession to Garfield Park on Friday, November 2nd.rnrnLuis Vazquez and the Family Art program have create an interactive altar installation at Garfield Park and the Red Poppy for Dia de Los Muertos. For these installations we are asking YOU to share your story about your experiences losing your loved ones and remembering your ancestors.rnrn rnrnFRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2: 6-7 PM - "MUERTOS OPEN MIC AT THE POPPY"rnrn rnrnA one-hour Open Mic, in the spirit of Día de los Muertos, to honor ancestors, personal and historic, who have inspired and informed our lives. También se invita recuerdos festivos, poemas calaveras, cuentos y canciones, as well as political satire. Hosted by poeta y cantante SANDRA GARCIA RIVERA, with guest artist CESAR BARRAGAN bringing la picardía mexicana, and DJ RUMOROSA tuned-in to the soul sounds. Audience is invited to participate on the Open Mic, and with the interactive altar installation entitled “The Air is Dying.”rnrn rnrn5:45pm: OPEN MIC SIGN-UP (8 -10 slots)rnDJ RumorosarnCeremonial welcome by Luis Vazquezrn6-7:00pm: OPEN MIC:rnEach person has 3-5min to make their offering.rnrn rnrnProcession starts at 7pm on 22nd and Harrison. Installation at Garifield Park will be led by Luis Vazques and the Family Art Program team.rnrn rnrnIf you can't be present your story can! Made possible by the Marigold Project and the Red Poppy Art House.rnrn rnrnFor more about the Day of the Dead Mission celebration go to DayoftheDeadSF.org

title: An Evening With Mark Growden
description: rnrn rnrnSoulful And Poetic Songsrnrn rnrnMark Growden is a singer, writer, multi-instrumentalist, composer, record producer, visual artist, and the artistic director of the Marigny Opera House in New Orleans, LA. Growden has released several critically-acclaimed albums, including Saint Judas, which was awarded “2010 Rekkid of the Year” by music blog Stash Dauber and ranked in the 2010 Village Voice Critics Poll. Growden has toured the US extensively and has composed original musical scores for dance and theater companies and scored several films. He is best known for his soulful voice and poetic lyrics.rnMark Growden: voice, accordion, clawhammer banjo, sruti box, bicycle handlebarsrnrn rnrn“Fiery, earthy and sublimely sensual.” – Comet Magazinernrn rnrn“…as though Harry Smith, Kurt Weill, and John Cage had been reincarnated into a single body” – Stash Dauberrnrn rnrn“His music combines elements from classical jazz ensembles, New Orleans-style brass bands, cabaret fanfare, and circus troupes with the stark honesty of Appalachian folk ballads and African-American prison songs.” – Taos Newsrnrn rnrn“think Bertolt Brecht meets Blind Willie Johnson.” – I-94 Barrnrn rnrnhttp://markgrowden.org/rnrnTwitter - @markgrowdenrnrnhttp://www.facebook.com/Mark.Growden.Musicrnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/user/MarkGrowdenrnrnhttp://vimeo.com/channels/markgrowdenrnrn rnrn$10-$20 admission. title: Shifting Margins - October Exhibition at the Poppy
description:
rnSeptember 27 - November 10

rn rnCuratorial Discussion: November 7 @ 6:00 pm

rn rnrnShifting Margins is a two-part exhibition curated by Bay Area curatorial organization OFF- Space running concurrently at The Red Poppy Art House and Aggregate Space in West Oakland. This two-part group exhibition brings together a variety of works that inspire deliberation, consideration and argument in order to grapple with the location, meaning and use of the edge, outsider-ness, privilege and what it means to be “on the margin” whether by choice or by force of circumstance. Shifting Margins will embrace the center, the periphery and the bridge between those concepts through the use of two locations “The City” (San Francisco) the perceived cultural and social center and its “marginal” sister city (Oakland) noted center of racial and social upheaval.

title: Devin Ray Hoff
description: rnrn rnrnFolkvangr And Broken Shadowsrnrn rnrnDevin Hoff is a bassist and composer who has written music for and played in Punk, Jazz and Country ensembles throughout the Americas, Europe, Asia and composed and played on hundreds of records, film and stage scores. Tonight he presents his two main creative acoustic music projects: Folkvangr and Broken Shadows.rnrn rnrnFolkvangr is a one-man band of acoustic bass and voice, focusing on repertoire of the Folk music Hoff grew up on in Kansas and Colorado and the roots of that music in European peasant songs, synthesizing these strains by amplifying the shared instinct of rebellion and freedom that underlies much of this music.rnrn rnrnBroken Shadows is a new band, built on old friendships, marching proudly in the tradition of the pioneers of "free jazz". In keeping with this spirit, the music and concepts are original (penned by Hoff) and strive to push the principle of liberation through music always one step further.rnDevin Ray Hoff: acoustic bass, voicernHoward Wiley: tenor saxophonernNick Mazzarella: alto saxophonernDarrell Green: drumsrnDevin Hoff: bassrnrn rnrnhttp://devinhoff.tumblr.com/rnrn rnrn$5 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: Mission Arts & Performance Project (MAPP)
description:
rnrn rnrn* MAPP is a FREE event — Join us!*rnrnThe MAPP is a homegrown, intercultural, multidisciplinary neighborhood event that takes place the first Saturday every two months in the Mission District. This October's MAPP features more than 15 venues—garages, cafés, studios, gardens, street corners—which artists transform into makeshift venues for the intimate experience of people and culture. Endeavoring to bridge the cultural divides of our communities, the MAPP gives space and voice to the multiplicity of perspectives and experiences that exist within urban settings.rnrnSchedule:rn4:00-4:45 Black Nature (Acoustic, Positive Music, Reggae, African Rhythms) bit.ly/PfqUBJrn5:00-5:45 Karina Denike and Band (Pop Noir) www.karinadenike.comrn6:00-6:30 Mark Baum’s Arboles y Amores (Latin Popular Songs) www.markbaum.netrn6:30-7:05 Video Screening by OFF Spacern7:15-7:45 Patrick Dethlefs (Folk/Americana/Acoustic) www.patrickdethlefs.comrn8:00-8:45 Clattering Loom presents Dialogues (Readings)rn8:50-9:00 RED Talk: Discussion on Solar-Powered Musicrn9:00-9:30 Musical Performance by Graham (Americana, Middle Eastern, Indian, Flamenco, and Balkan)rn9:45-10:15 Joshua Eden with Oscar Westesson (Gritty Acoustic Soul) reverbnation.com/joshuaedenrn10:30-11:00 Selections from Teobi’s Dreaming: A Work-in-Progress (Contemporary Dance - Performance Art) www.toddthomasbrown.comrn11:15-12:00 Panique (Gypsy Jazz) www.paniquejazz.comrnrnExhibition: "Shifting Margins" two-part exhibition created by Bay Area curatorial organization OFF Space and running concurrently at The Red Poppy Art House and Aggregate Space in West Oakland.
rnCheck out the Facebook event (also has information on every venue).rnrnCheck out the MAPP Facebook Group.

title: Janam: Transcendent Balkan & Near Eastern Blend
description: rnrn rnrnJanam (“my soul”) creates a compelling mix of Balkan, Turkish, Sephardic and American Roots music & originals. Led by golden throated Kitka Women’s Vocal Ensemble veteran Juliana Graffagna, Janam proves that the foothills of the Balkans and the Appalachians lie in Oakland. Janam’s funky meters, bittersweet modes, driving tunes, and sultry ballads invoke the nightingales and dark eyes, the hot love and cold hollows of Europe and America’s most musical mountains. Janam unites the Bay Area's most imaginative and devoted practitioners of Balkan & Near Eastern music, to follow the universal threads of folk songs of disparate traditions. The resulting music is transcendent, sensuous and irresistible.rnJuliana Graffagna: vocals, percussionrnDan Auvil: percussion, vocalsrnTom Farris: laouto, guitar, accordionrnGari Hegedus: violin, oud, saz, mandocellornPeter Jaques: clarinet, ney, vocalsrn“An innovative ensemble that explores a dizzying range of styles, from Appalachia and Asia Minor to Eastern Europe. The quintet plays swirling, hard-driving music that always seems to end up in unexpected places.” - San Francisco Chroniclernrn rnrn“This fine group goes on a world tour of Eastern Europe…with no cultural jet lag.” -Santa Barbara Independentrnrn rnrn“They’re a band to watch, with some sterling musicianship and a real sense of fervour about them.” - Sing Out!rnhttp://www.janamband.comrnrn rnrnhttp://www.facebook.com/janambandrnrn rnrnhttp://cdbaby.com/cd/janamrnrn rnrnhttp://soundcloud.com/peterjaques/janam-poulaki-xenornrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yTSC7BENkME&feature=channel&list=ULrnrn rnrnDoors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: Adam Theis & The Left Coast Strings
description: rnrn rnrn rnrnOrchestral Jazz, Strings & Film Scoresrnrn rnrn rnrnA brand new ensemble led by Jazz Mafia founder Adam Theis includes 4 of the most multi-faceted Bay Area string players on the scene: Dina Maccabee, Alisa Rose, Matthew Szemela and Lewis Patzner.  The ensemble features many new works written by Theis for the unique instrumentation of strings and trombone. In this setting Adam takes his trademark rhythm-based/melodic compositional style usually heard in larger orchestras and strips it down to the centuries old instrumentation of the string quartet,  The music features the quartet in many new musical settings and pushes the boundaries of the ensemble together and as improvisational soloists.  Also included in the program will be new film scores performed live and special surprise guests.rnrn rnrn rnrnAdam Theis:  trombonernDina Maccabee: violinrnAlisa Rose:  violinrnMatthew Szemela:  violinrnLewiz Patzner:  cellornrn rnrn rnrnwww.jazzmafia.comrnrnrnhttps://www.facebook.com/thejazzmafiarnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=i1TDbonQSZMrnrn rnrn rnrn$10-$15 admission.  Doors at 7:30 pm.  Show at 8:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: ANDREA WOLPER / HAFEZ MODIRZADEH AND FRIENDS
description: rnrn rnrn"An audacious artist [with] an inventive, thrilling, appealing musical vision,” New York singer and songwriter Andrea Wolper celebrates her recent CD release, “Parallel Lives,” with her first appearance at Red Poppy. Mixing genres, crossing boundaries, as at home with the Great American songbook as she is with spontaneous improvisations, she presents her intriguing arrangements of everything from Pop to poetry. Andrea approaches everything she sings with an irrepressible spirit and an open heart. Acclaimed Bay Area saxophonist Hafez Modirzadeh will open with a short set, then join Andrea's group for some East-West musical explorations that will run the gamut from light-hearted and humorous to thought provoking and moving.rnrn rnrnAndrea Wolper: voicernKen Fiiano: acoustic bassrnDave MacNab: guitarrnHafez Modirzadeh: saxophone, flutesrnDrummer: Vijay Andersonrnrn rnrn“Still not as well-known as she should be, Wolper is a singer who brings songs to life, whatever their source, working in a milieu that begins with Jazz and reaches out to embrace an expressive area uniquely her own.” - Don Heckman, International Review of Musicrnrn rnrn“A superb singer. . . a highly original performer with a sultry, late-night sound.” - Andrew Gilbert, Contra Costa Timesrnrn rnrn“Each selection has a life of its own that can exist only through the honesty of Wolper's expression. . . [she] delivers each sound-world with total artistic commitment.” Francis Lo Kee, NYC Jazz Recordrnrn rnrn“Andrea Wolper just mesmerizes . . . more than a giant singer she’s an actress to reckon with.” Dan Singer, In Tune Internationalrnrn rnrnwww.andreawolper.comrnrn rnrn$12-15 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: LATE NITE ART 4: A Unique Social Art Experience
description:
rn

rn

rnLATE NITE ART 4rnA Unique Social Art Experiencernrn rnrnLATE NITE ART it is an evening of collaborative art-making, ambient music, to-die-for food and insightful discussion. In a few words, it's a social art experience. Meet people, have a few beverages, and share thought-provoking discussion on what it means to create in collaboration. This is an open platform for all non-artists and artists alike to come together. You don’t need to be able to color within the lines or draw portraits of fruit bowls – you just need eyes, ears, and an open mind. Adam Rosendahl (founder/facilitator), Keri Keifer (chef), Jocelyn Jackson (chef).rnrn rnrnPurchase tickets in advance at Brown Paper Tickets:rnrn rnrnhttp://www.brownpapertickets.com/event/267885rnrn rnrnThere will be 10 scholarship tickets available for $25 and 20 normal tickets available for $30.rnrn rnrn6:00 pm doors - 9:30 pmrnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: ARAM SHELTON QUARTET + ADAMS MEZZACAPPA DUO
description: rnrnEverything For Somebody Album ReleasernrnThe Aram Shelton Quartet plays creative Jazz music inspired by Ornette Coleman, Eric Dolphy, and Charles Mingus. In this quartet, Shelton (Oakland) plays alto saxophone and is joined by Chicagoans Keefe Jackson on tenor saxophone, Anton Hatwich on bass, and Tim Daisy on drums. The new album "Everything for Somebody", features compositions that place the players in familiar and challenging territory. All four members contribute narrative solos to the album, displaying their individual styles while making a collective music that is joyful, energetic, raw and poignant. Lisa Mezzacappa and (ROVA) saxophonist Steve Adams have been performing together in trio, quartet and large ensemble contexts for several years. For this set, they will explore compositions each of them have written for larger groups, compressed into the fantastically intimate and possibility-rich context of the duo.rnrnAram Shelton: alto saxophonernKeefe Jackson: tenor saxophonernAnton Hatwich: bassrnTim Daisy: drumsrnSteve Adams: alto saxophonernLisa Mezzacappa: bassrnrn"It is easy to admire the audacity of Aram Shelton's quartet." – All About Jazzrnrn“This one is all about the synchronous sound” ★★★★ - Downbeatrnrn"Shelton and Jackson share a rapport that makes sessions like this one sound somewhat effortless even though the collaborative energy spent to get there was obviously deep and rewarding" - Master of a Small Housernrnwww.aramshelton.com/quartet.htmlrnrnhttp://saxsteveadams.com/rnrnhttp://www.lisamezzacappa.comrnrnhttp://soundcloud.com/singlespeedmusic/aram-shelton-quartetrnrnhttp://soundcloud.com/singlespeedmusic/good-toast-live-at-the-hungryrnrnhttps://www.dropbox.com/s/ri5b2rrfuelpxtq/Aram_Shelton_PG_HR_credit.jpgrnrnhttp://youtu.be/jSliwj6TB8srnrn rnrn$10-20 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: BOCA DO RIO
description: rnrn rnrnElectro-Acoustic Brazilrnrn rnrnBoca do Rio strips it down in the intimate venue of the Red Poppy Art House – a perfect setting for the Bossa Novas and Sambas emanating from this powerful trio. The classic sounds of nylon-string guitar softly strummed with a dancing melodic horn is anchored by the rhythmic powerhouse that is Alex Calatayud, one of Brazil’s finest and most unique percussive voices to emerge in years. Recognized by master musicians and music lovers alike, Alex Calatayud is featured on the timba, a traditional instrument used in an innovative way to bring together the soulful elements of rhythm and expression.rnrn rnrnAlex Calatayud: percussionrnKevin Welch: vocals and guitarrnLarry De La Cruz: saxophone and percussionrnrn rnrn“Fantastic music steeped in the infectious rhythms of Brazil, Boca do Rio balances expert musicianship with serious dance grooves.” -7X7 magazinernrn rnrnwww.bocadorio.comrnrn rnrn$12 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: $5 Series - SPOKESPEOPLE
description: [caption id="attachment_2723" align="aligncenter" width="300" caption="Spokespeople"][/caption]rnrnrnrnAn Evening Of Storytellingrnrn rnrnComedy, intrigue, drama all in 10-minute bursts told live without notes by storytellers from around the Bay Area. Cozy up with us to escape the summer fog with this evening of true stories. The evening is organized by Spokespeople, a San Francisco storytelling group. The theme for September is "Back to School: Stories of misadventures in education.”rnrn rnrnSpokespeople founders are Sara Barz (emcee), Rebecca Stievater, and Emma Bailey. (About the Founders: http://spokesppl.org/about/)rnrn rnrn"The story sessions are fun! They are typically hilarious, sometimes gory, but live and engaging. At Red Poppy earlier this year, we heard about one of Sara’s first dates (ended in blood), one woman’s insane ex-girlfriend (involved blood), and Bill’s redheaded childhood friend (Ian Contains blood).rnrn rnrn[Editor's note: not all the stories necessarily contain blood.] Interpretations of the nightly topic may be loose or literal, but it seems that no matter who is at the mic, they are entertaining." The Maykazinernrn rnrn rnrnhttp://themaykazine.com/2012/04/27/stories-and-cycles-and-spokespeople/spokesppl.orgrnrnhttp://www.facebook.com/spokespplrnrnhttp://www.twitter.com/spokespplrnrn rnrnRecent podcast: Podcast #10: Sweating, Drooling, Labored Breathing — Just Another Day On The Busrnrn rnrn$5 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: CHELLE! & FRIENDS + Gumbo by Urban Catering
description: rnrn rnrnCelebrate New CHELLE! AND FRIENDSrnrn rnrn rnrnCelebrate New Orleans!rnrn rnrn rnrnAsk 20 people how to define the music of New Orleans and you’ll get 30 answers. It could be Jazz, Funk, Soul, Cajun, Creole, Gospel, Caribbean, African, Zydeco, Rock or the chants and stirring rhythms of Mardi Gras Indians. New Orleans native, Michelle Jacques along some "very special friends" take all of these influences and a few more, and stir it into this stewpot of musical gumbo that is sweet, spicy, aromatic and definitely intoxicating. CHELLE! and Friends is made up of some of the most distinguished and accomplished musicians in the San Francisco Bay Area. CHELLE! And Friends perform music that is vibrant and alive with the driving voices of artistic director and founder Michelle Jacques, Rhonda Crane, Jay Lamont and Bryan Dyer, Donna Viscuso on woodwinds, Eric Swinderman on guitar, and Sam Bevan on bass and Bob Scott on drums.rnrnAlso featured this night, get a taste of some homemade New Orleans gumbo and cornbread made by our favorite chef, Stephanie Dalton of Urban Catering and Events (www.urbancateringandevents.com)rnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wVYVyHAbHy4rnrn rnrnhttp://urbanmusicpresents.com/chellefriends.cfmrnrn rnrnhttps://www.facebook.com/CHELLEandFriendsrnrn rnrnwww.cdbaby.com/cd/chelleandfriendsrnrn rnrnwww.sonicbids.com/chelleandfriendsrnrn rnrn rnrn$12-$15 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm. Orleans!rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: CLASSICAL REVOLUTION PRESENTS: KARAVIKA
description: rnrn rnrnIndo-Western Chamber EnsemblernrnKaravika, New York City’s Indo-Western chamber ensemble, is led by violinist Trina Basu and cellist Amali Premawardhana. Drawing from the rich classical and folk traditions of India, Europe and NorthAmerica, Karavika is in San Francisco celebrating the release of their recent debut CD, “SUNRISE”, which All About Jazz describes as: “A dynamic sonic blend of uncommon mesmeric beauty.” Drawing upon influences ranging from simple folk melodies to complex classical compositions, Karavika’s music seeks to blend creative improvisations and arrangements with authentic traditions. They are performing at Red Poppy on behalf of the Classical Revolution Music Festival.rnrn rnrnTrina Basu: violinrnAmali Premawardhana: cellornPerry Wortman: bassrnSameer Gupta: tablarnrn rnrn"A diversely melodic, often hypnotic original sound that occasionally reaches toward Appalachian rusticity or a brisk Celtic mood... Whether quiet and reflective or joyously energetic, the melodies are as bright as the musicians' tone: this is music for celebrating or getting lost in" - Lucid Culturern"It is a liquid and organic brew that sounds inevitable, as if it's existed forever..." Dan McClengaghan, All About Jazzrnrn rnrnwww.karavika.comrnrnwww.facebook.com/karavikarnrn rnrn$10-$20 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: THE HURD ENSEMBLE
description: Graceful Machines: Classical Meets Electronicrnrn rnrn rnrnPerforming original music by San Francisco-based composer George Hurd, The Hurd Ensemble unifies the worlds of Electronic and Classical music. All the music is written for string quartet (violin, viola, cello, upright bass), piano and electronics, meticulously bound together with digitally-arranged sounds collected from Hurd’s travels. Absolutely no stock electronic sounds are used – every sound produced is of his own creation, recorded, edited and manipulated himself. The electronics are layered to create textures that perfectly complement the acoustic instruments, giving rise to a sound that is extremely organic despite its partially digital origins. Hurd’s music is both wildly, intricately rhythmic and aglow with shimmering harmonies and melodies.rnrn rnGeorge Hurd: composition & electronicsrnSollen Seguillon: violinrnJacob Hansen-Joseph: violarnAnton Estaniel: cello & steel drumsrnAri Gorman: bassrnElyse Weakley: pianornrn rnwww.hurdensemble.comrnrn rnrnwww.facebook.com/hurdensemblernrn rn$12-$20 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: HOWELL DEVINE & DEVINE'S JUG BAND
description: rnrn rnrnAn Acoustic Evening Of Blues & Americana Musicrnrn rnrn rnrnHowellDevine is the kind of blues you don't hear anymore. The raw slide guitar played by Joshua Howell evokes the Mississippi Hill Country greats such as Fred McDowell and RL Burnside. The rhythm section of drummer and percussionist Pete Devine and bassist Safa Shokrai breaks from the norm in Blues, providing rich and complex textures integral to the music rather than a simple backing section for a soloist. The result is a sound which stands in stark contrast to the typical Blues heard in bars these days and would more likely be shaking the floors of a Southern juke joint some 70 years ago. Howell Devine brings life back to that sound, infusing the gritty, rural blues with their own unique style. Devine’s Jug Band, a San Francisco ensemble wise to the ways of the weird old South. title: THE VNOTE ENSEMBLE
description: rnrn rnrnNew Direction In Jazz And Venezuelan Musicrnrn rnrn rnrnHailed for its spirited performances and its fascinating fusion of Venezuelan folk rhythms and adventurous Jazz explorations, The VNote Ensemble give voice to the hidden treasure of Venezuelan music. The group is committed to raising that voice and exploring the connection between North American Jazz, traditional Venezuelan songs and original musical ideas. Jackeline Rago (cuatro/bandola/percussion), Donna Viscuso (flute), Sam Bevan (bass) and Michaelle Goerlitz (percussion) defiantly ignore musical boundaries to create a new direction that journeys through the infectious rhythms of Joropo, Swing, Gaita, Soul, Be-bop and Calypso, among many others.rnrn rnrnwww.vnoteensemble.comrnrn rn$10- $15 admission. title: Mijo de la Palma
description: rnCanto E´ Jíbaro 2012 · Live Recording Sessionrnrn rnrn rnrnFar from being an ensemble, Mijo is more about a metamorphous crossing of people. Looking back at years of musical encounters with diverse people, Mijo resembles a Socratic circle that refuses a definition. Fusión Jíbara has been the tagline adopted by many but it seems to be more complex than that. Fusión Jíbara resembles one of the many aspects that has characterized Mijo, for their ability to merge creative energies as a whole. Not surprisingly, what seems to endure is the Jíbaro vibe. As Mijo embarks on their US tour, Canto E' Jíbaro, they hope to find new ways to feed their soul as they try to bring the same to those who experience it.rnrn rnrnMelvin Lopez Rivera: guitar / main vocalsrnAdaliz Ortiz Toro: harmoniesrnSurprise Guest Artistsrnrn rnrn"Mijo De La Palma did it with style tonight. How did they make that look so easy? ...and to a packed house! " - Mission Cultural Center for the Latino Artsrnrn rnrn"Por fin pude ver a Mijo De La Palma. Ellos te agarra por los pies y te arrastran a un monte que sólo ellos conocen. Antes del primer acorde menor, te quitan los zapatos y te llevan de la mano. Pero cuando se van, te dejan pa que bajes por tu cuenta y descalzo." - Joshua Cortes, Río Piedras, 2011rnrn rnrn"Nada sabía yo de este jíbaro de Puerto Rico que canta como si hubiese nacido en el Puerto de Santa María, crecido en el delta del Mississippi y abrevado en los pantanos del África negra." - Jose Ángel Gonzálezrnrn rnrnhttp://www.scribd.com/doc/95744869/Mijo-de-la-Palma-%C2%B7-Calle-20-Spainrnrn rnrnwww.facebook.com/mijodelapalmarnrn rnrnwww.youtube.com/mijodelapalmarnrn rnrnhttp://soundcloud.com/mijodelapalmarnrn rnrnhttp://mijodelapalma.bandcamp.com/album/mijo-en-directo-2009rnrn rnrn$10 - $15 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: TITO GONZALEZ Y SU QUINTETO
description: A Hot August Night In Havanarnrn rnrnTito’s boleros are sensuously bittersweet when sung in his smooth baritone voice. The full-bodied melodic mix of “call and response” between the piano, vocals, congas and horns commands the listener’s attention. From the first blast of the horns in “Busco a Alguien,” you’ll be musically transported to Havana, hips swaying under the shade of a palm tree, until the final piano riff in “Evocacion.” Tito is a second-generation heir of Buena Vista Social Club. He studied with Buena Vista Social Club tres master, Papi Oviedo, and with the greatest guitarist in Cuba, Octavio Sanchez Cotan.rnrn rnrnhttp://www.titoysusondecuba.com/rnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2MlAFciqvBkrnrn rnrn$15 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: REBECCA CROSS + WOLF & CROW
description: rnrn rnrnAn Evening Of Music For The Spiritrnrn rnrn rnrnRebecca Cross is a composer of electronic and acoustic music. Her work has been seen in NYTimes, Vouge, SFWeekly, Elle, Nylon and was recently showcased at the International Museum of Women recognizing women in technology. Her current project is an exploration of Americana storytelling with a focus on frequency and sound healing. Wolf & Crow is a San Francisco based folk duo founded in 2009 comprised of Zachary “Crow” Vieira and Mathieu “Wolf” Stemmelen. Their sound is characterized by raw, natural vocal harmonies singing honest and emotional lyrics, supported by simple rhythmic guitar picking and colored by a haunting slide guitar. Songwriter Zachary Vieira’s lyrical themes range from troubling visions to historical fiction to abstractedly spiritual epiphanies. The name derives from a folkloric narrative developed by the pair to describe their story as two totemic characters bound together and traveling through a forbidding world in search of refuge.rnRebecca Cross: nylon string guitar, vocalsrnDan Massie: dobro, slide harprnTom Edler: upright bassrnZachary Vieira (Crow): lead vocals, guitarrnMathieu Semmelen (Wolf): backing vocals, guitarrnrn rnrn rnrn"Cross takes up the challenge and wins it... with a vocal tone as crystalline as Sinead O'Connor's ...this sweet little voice has a strong command of language and a sharp wit that makes it work...She's hard to label, let alone stereotype." - SF Weeklyrnrn rnrn"Playing some sweet slide guitar, Wolf & Crow set the mood for a night of quiet, contemplative music." - The Owlrnrn rnrnRebecca Cross: www.rebeccacrossmusic.comrnrn rnrnWolf & Crow: http://www.facebook.com/pages/Wolf-Crow/191927194177265rnrn rnrn$10-$20 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

The August reading examines the Cherokee phrase "Yutta Hey" which translates as "Its a good day to die."- the famous battle cry of the Cherokee Indian. title: Karina Denike, performs with Bells!
description:
rnrn rnrnVirtuosic chanteuse Karina Denike will transport you to a decade that never happened—but should have. Steeped in the lost eras of 1930’s torch songs and Motown, her original songs draw from sea shanties, Eastern European lullabies, doo wop drama, and other unexpected sources. Supporting herself on chord organ, and backed by an adventurous San Francisco band of established composers and musicians in their own right; Aaron Novik on Bass Clarinet, Lily Taylor and Melody Ferris on vocal harmonies, James Frazier on Fender VI baritone guitar, Eric Garland on Drums, and Michael McIntosh on keys. Just back from a European tour Denike is preparing to release her first solo album late 2012.rnrn rnrnKarina Denike: vocals, chord organrnAaron Novik: bass clarinetrnMelody Ferris: vocal harmoniesrnJames Frazier: Fender VIrnMichael McIntosh: pianornEric Garland: drum setrnrn rnrn"Denike’s songs combine a dizzying array of styles and influences: Doo Wop, early Rock and Soul, Tin Pan Alley, Cabaret, Classic and contemporary singer songwriters. She distills everything into an organic, irresistible Pop music.... She is a naturally soulful performer, .... But her singing, like her music, is perfectly balanced – her sense of pitch, volume, phrasing are formidable. It is a rare thrill to run into a performer who has such technique and such artistry.”  - Todd Wanerman, thebaybridged.comrnrn rnrnwww.karinadenike.comrnrn rnBio:

rnBlending heavy percussion, soulful vocal harmonies, and thoughtful arrangements Bells has arrived at a sound that is uniquely theirs. Although they are a new group, they are quickly drawing an audience with diverse musical tastes. Birthed from the imaginations of singer-percussionist Sandra Lawson-Ndu, and bassist-singer Doug Stuart. The songs are brought to life with the help of Derek Barber on guitar and vocals, and Geneva Harrison on drums, percussion, and vibes.

rnrnrnrnrn$10-$15 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!r

title: JEREMIAH LOCKWOOD SOLO + CHARMING HOSTESS
description: rnrn rnrnCountry Blues Meets Jewish Liturgyrnrn rnrnSaturday, August 11rnrn rnrnJeremiah Lockwood's band, The Sway Machinery, has made its mark around the world performing at festivals ranging from the Festival of the Desert in Timbuktu to Roskilde Festival in Denmark. His musical training included singing in the choir for his grandfather, Cantor Jacob Konigsberg and a decade plus of busking in the New York subways with Piedmont blues legend Carolina Slim. He received the Six Points Fellowship and more recently was a Brooklyn Philharmonic Orchestra Composer Fellow. The Sway Machinery continues to tour the world.rnrn rnrnCharming Hostess is a music ensemble at the intersection of voice, text and diaspora consciousness. Founded by Jewlia Eisenberg, Charming Hostess is now presenting Gramophone and Dynamo centered around the violent, sexy, anti-authoritarian music of Salonika.rnrn rnrn rnrnJeremiah Lockwood: guitar and voicernrnJewlia Eisenberg & Companyrnrn rnrn rnrn"Hard to explain but impossible to ignore"--Russ Davis, MOJA Radiorn"Jeremiah Lockwood makes the ancient modern and the mythological real"--Buzz Poole, The Village Voicernrn rnrn"Charming Hostess stretches from ancient Babylonia to Blind Willie Johnson, from heady folk to heavy metal, from hummable charm to dissonant harm, forging all of these elements into a new musical language of their own." - KQEDrnrn rnrn"Jewlia Eisenberg’s work is irreverent and ambitious. Charming Hostess radiates female energy and her singing transforms the spirit." - The San Francisco Chroniclernrn rnrnJeremiah Lockwoodrnrn rnrnwww.swaymachinery.comrnwww.myspace.com/jeremiahlockwoodrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lr7EnDtiGGgrnhttp://www.npr.org/2011/02/05/133523486/the-sway-machinery-finding-inspiration-in-the-desertrnrn rnrnJewlia Eisenbergrnrn rnrnLa Mar La Galana by Gramaphone & Dynamo - YouTubernrn rnrnCharming Hostess sings "La Mujer De Pipitero" - YouTubernrn rnrn16. title: Fatum Brothers' Peacetet
description: rnrn rnrnFresh & Joyous Jazz Sounds from New Yorkrnrn rnrn rnrnThe Peacetet is the newest musical offering from the Fatum Brothers, which combines the energy and soul of their highly acclaimed Jazz Orchestra, and the intimacy of their trio. The Peacetet features some of New York City’s most exciting young talent whom possess the unique ability to connect with the audience and create humane and relevant music. The band’s interpretations of Folk tunes and Gospel melodies showcase the band’s reverence, while their contagious sense of groove and swing create music of the moment. The three horns mix charisma with character backed by just bass and drums. This group’s goal is to create soul-elevating music and to share that experience with everyone they meet.rnrn rnrnMichael Fatum: trumpetrnRay Mason: trombonernPatrick Sargent: alto/sopranornMichael Harlen: bassrnJohn Fatum: drums & percussionrnrn rnrn"The Fatum Brothers performs in a way that inspires curiosity, attentiveness, and participation." - Blue Note Jazz Club (NYC)rn"These guys are an amazing story. Really amazing. I have a feeling that you are going to be hearing about the Fatum Brothers for a long time." - WGN Chicagornrn rnrn"It's not hard to find the Fatum Brothers this summer: they're everywhere." - Chicago SunTimesrnrn rnrn"The Fatum Brothers are part of an All-Star Lineup!" - Downbeat Magazinernrn rnrn“They bring such energy, enthusiasm, curiosity, and creativity every day to America's only original art-form. It's a thrill for me to hear that the future of Jazz is in great hands, and the evidence of it is here today on this stage." - Dave Pietro (Grammy Nominated Saxophonist & Composer)rnrn rnrn rnrnwww.fatumjazz.comrnrn rnrn rnrnwww.facebook.com/fatumjazzrnrn rnrn rnrnwww.youtube.com/fatumbrothersjazzrnrn rnrn rnrn$10 - $15 admission. title: I Walk the Line: Recent Works by Peter Foucault
description:
rnrn[caption id="attachment_2672" align="alignnone" width="302" caption="Work on view at the Red Poppy Art House by resident artist Peter Foucault"][/caption]rnrn rnI Walk the Line

rn rnrnMixed-media works by Peter Foucaultrnrn rnrnThursday August 9 - Saturday September 22, 2012rnrn rnrnArtist Reception: Thursday, September 6 6pm-9pmrnrn rnrnThe Red Poppy Art House is pleased to present new mixed -media works by Peter Foucault investigating the nature of drawing and community, where chance factors and collaboration exchange and collide to develop what ultimately becomes the finished piece.rnrn rnrnIn his current body of work, Peter Foucault creates a series of drawing projects where outside factors influence the creation of abstract compositions. Among the selection of works presented in I Walk the Line, Foucault has collaborated with a small sensor driven robot that interacts with the artist and the public at large to create layers of mark making which he later returns to the studio to further translates these layers, decoding the algorithm generated by this machine interface. Additionally, other works presented include Belgium beer suspended in blobs of ink which Foucault then blows across the surface of the paper to further build on his mark making and collage techniques.rnrn rnrnFueled by a love of drawing and mark-making, Foucault’s works presented at The Red Poppy Art House explore the tension between control and the loss of control, where viewer interactivity plays an integral part in many of the drawing installations and large-scale artworks on view.rnrn rnrnBased in Oakland, Foucault’s work has been included in group shows at the Smithsonian Institutes’ Freer and Sackler Gallery, Kit Schulte Contemporary (Berlin, Germany), The University of Salford (Manchester, England), The NASA Aimes Research Center and The Orange County Center for Contemporary Art among numerous others.  Recent reviews of Foucault’s work have appeared in The San Francisco Chronicle, The

title: Dana Lyn & Kyle Sanna
description: rnrn rnrnA "Through the Looking Glass" Approach to Irish Music ....or....rnrn rnrn rnrnFiddler Dana Lyn and guitarist Kyle Sanna first met at a traditional Irish music session in New York City ten years ago. They soon discovered a shared interest in classical, world and experimental music, as well as a deep admiration and respect for Irish music. Both are active participants in Brooklyn’s eclectic music scene as arrangers, composers, players and improvisers. Their collaborative effort, The Hare Said a Prayer to the Rainbow and Followed the Fox Down the Hole, is reflective of their wide range of musical influences and experience. Traditional tunes, original compositions and improvised intermezzi are woven together to create a musical narrative that is both childlike and sophisticated, and full of unexpected twists and turns. Dana Lyn and Kyle Sanna have worked and performed with Susan McKeown, Martin Hayes, The Green Fields of America, Loudon Wainwright and the Walkmen, Edgar Meyer, Chris Thile, and Yo-Yo Ma.rnrn rnrn rnrn“A very innovative recording – mysterious, playful and charming with some lovely musical surprises. From the excellent musicianship and skillfully fascinating arrangements to the relaxed whimsy of the CD package, the whole project is bursting with creativity, yet still manages to convey a love for simplicity – a remarkable achievement.” - Renowned fiddler Kevin Burkernrn rnrn rnrnDana Lyn: violin, pianornKyle Sanna: guitarrnrn rnrnwww.danalynkylesanna.comrnrn rnrnwww.danalynkylesanna.bandcamp.comrn$10 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.

Curators: Lyz Luke & Sylvia Hathawayrn6:45pm - 7:15: Grant Levin’s “88 Keys of Jazz”rn7:15 – 8: SF Theater Pub Presents: Selections from the Pint Sized Play Festival IIIrn8-9: Toni Bruna (Imaginary Folk/Italian)rn9-9:05: Peter Foucault’s “Attraction/Repulsion” (Interactive Robotic Drawing Machine)rn9-10: Kaboom! Quartet (String Chamber Rock)rn10-11: Miguel Bernales (Latin Rock)rn11-12: Go Van Gogh (World Fusion/Jazz/Rock)rnExhibition: Amalgamation by Ralph Vázquez-Concepción"rnrn* MAPP is a FREE event -- Join us!*rnrnThe MAPP is a homegrown, intercultural, multidisciplinary neighborhood event that takes place the first Saturday every two months in the Mission District. This August's MAPP features more than 15 venues—garages, cafés, studios, gardens, street corners—which artists transform into makeshift venues for the intimate experience of people and culture. Endeavoring to bridge the cultural divides of our communities, the MAPP gives space and voice to the multiplicity of perspectives and experiences that exist within urban settings.rnDownload the MAPP August 2012 Program with information on every venue and their lineups.
rnCheck out the Facebook event (also has information on every venue).
rnCheck out the MAPP Facebook Group.

title: Sameer Gupta's Namaskar
description: rnrn rnrnGolden-Era Bollywood Meets Modern Jazzrnrn rnrnNamaskar’s acclaimed percussionist, Sameer Gupta (The Supplicants, Marc Cary, VidyA), combines well-known compositions from the Indian Golden Era of Bollywood with Indian Ragas and modern American Jazz to craft an irresistibly, beautiful, artistic statement. Namaskar musically embodies the integrative spirit of our time. An original musical voice in Jazz, World, and Fusion music, Sameer Gupta is known as one of the few percussionists representing the traditions of American Jazz on drum set, and Indian Classical music on tabla. Namaskar is an inspirational group exploring traditional Indian Ragas, Folk Songs & Bollywood classics springing from the passionate spirit of modern jazz.rnrn rnrn rnrnSameer Gupta: drum set, tablarnDavid Boyce: MicroKorg, reedsrnNeel Murgai: sitarrnPrasant Radhakrishnan: Carnatic saxrnDarren Johnston: trumpetrnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn"Gupta is also an accomplished tabla player, and uses his knowledge of...cross-rhythms to create astonishingly original percussive textures.” - India Currentsrnrn rnrn rnrn“Sameer Gupta and David Ewell of the Supplicants soar with the fiery grace of A Love Supreme-era Coltrane.” - San Francisco Bay Guardianrnrn rnrn rnrn rnrnhttp://www.motema.com/downloads/namaskar/rnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9f9FHf6h2Vkrnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lHa4MVHlyc0rnrn rnrnhttp://www.motema.com/artist/sameer-guptarnrn rnrnhttp://www.sameergupta.comrnrn rnrn$15-$20 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: Sirrah
description: rnThe Music Of Eddie Harrisrnrn rnrnInternationally renowned saxophonist/composer/educator Dayna Stephens,is a force to watch out for. A fluid, vigorous soloist with a warm, soulful sound, Dayna has also proven himself as a master of composition and arrangement. He is a highly-acclaimed band leader and an “in-demand”, side-man for some of Jazz’s contemporary masters, such as Kenny Barron, Idris Muhammad, Taylor Eigsti. In recent years, he has appeared on stage with numerous established musicians and other rising stars, including Terrance Blanchard, Geoffrey Keezer, Albert "Tootie" Heath, Matt Wilson, Oliver Lake, Roy Hargrove, Carlos Santana, Stefon Harris,Stevie Wonder, Dr. Lonnie Smith, Peter Apfelbaum, OZOmatli, Patrice Rushen,Tom Harrell, Reuben Rogers, Gregory Hutchinson, Gretchen Parlato, Josh Roseman, Ray Drummond, Winard Harper, Bobby Short, Bruce Barth, and many others.rnrn rnrnDayna Stephens: tenor saxophonernWil Blades: Hammond B3 organrnBrandon Etzler: drumsrn“Tenorist Stephens emerges as an artist full of composure and imagination" - Downbeat Magazinernrn rnrn“Yeah, he does it...he’s on it. He is the future to carry on the legend, the legacy of the organ, [of] the B-3.” -Dr. Lonnie Smithrnrn rnrnwww.daynastephenssoound.comrnrn rnrnwww.wilblades.comrnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: The Supplicants
description: rnImprovisational Reunion In SFrnrn rnrnSaxophonist David Boyce (Broun Fellinis), bassist David Ewell (Vivendo de Pao) , and drummer Sameer Gupta (Marc Cary, NAMASKAR) start from scratch for each performance, channeling their inspiration into freely improvised musical moments. The Supplicants have been sculpting their unique sound for over 10 years, starting in 1999 in San Francisco. Their concerts at The Red Poppy have been consistently unforgettable musical experiences. Sameer Gupta flies in from NYC for this special rare event.rnrn rnrnDavid Boyce: saxophonernDavid Ewell: bassrnSameer Gupta: drumsrnrn rn"The Supplicants has left me radiant, fulfilled in the way only the most profound spiritual encounters can. Composed of some of the Bay Area's most accomplished jazz musicians...the band soars with the fiery grace of A Love Supreme-era Coltrane." - Jonathan Zwickel, SF Bay Guardianrnrn rn"The level of emotion with which they play is so deep, so personal, and the energy so pure that it is almost as if existence, or being itself, creeps most surprisingly yet receptively into our souls.” —Drew Foxman, All About Jazzrnrn rnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=se-h6ZRjmH0rnrn rnrnhttp://youtu.be/BFbDPojTuzwrnrn rnrnhttp://www.baytaper.com/2007/12/13/the-supplicants-live-at-the-red-poppy-art-house-2/rnrn rnrnhttp://youtu.be/LUGvkQaz9Igrnrn rnrnhttp://www.myspace.com/thesupplicantsrnrn rnAll ages welcome!

title: Prasant Radhakrishnan's VidyA
description: rnrn rnrnSouth Indian Classical Meets Jazzrnrn rnrn rnrnVidyA merges Jazz with the melodic and rhythmic nuance of South Indian Classical (Carnatic) music. Led by critically-acclaimed saxophonist, Prasant Radhakrishnan, VidyA has emerged with a soulful, penetrating sound that pushes the labels of "Fusion" or "World Music." These three artists have an almost telepathic interplay, incorporating the forward motion of both Indian Classical and Jazz rhythms while elaborating Carnatic ragas infused with melodic richness. The group weaves in and out of the two genres while all the time merging them into one. VidyA translates the language of Carnatic music into the idiom of Jazz.rnrn rnrn rnrnPrasant Radhakrishnan: saxophonernDavid Ewell: bassrnSameer Gupta: drumsrnrn rnrn rnrn“...Pioneering trans-cultural terrain." - San Francisco Chroniclernrn rnrn rnrn rnrnVidyA's music breathes at the very center of a cultural crossroads between the North American Jazz idiom and the Carnatic music of South India. VidyA's music is a new vein of North American music... present tense, present location, new culture in the making." -Todd Brown, Red Poppy Art Housernrn rnrn rnrn“…when these patterns are played on saxophone, violin, string bass, and jazz drums, there is abuild-up of emotional energy and intellectual complexity which seems to recreate the energy that was present at the birth of bebop in 1940s New York. In fact, if Charlie Parker or Dizzie Gillespie had heard VidyA at that time, I think it would have never have occurred to them that VidyA’s music was Indian. They would simply have wondered where these cats had found a sound that was so mercilessly free of the standard melodic and rhythmic clichés.” -Teed Rockwell, India Currentsrnrn rnrn rnrnA style that's madly percussive and sparkling… combines Jazz's sweet dreaminess with the Indian form's insistent rhythmic and tonal changes.” – SF Weeklyrnrn rnrn rnrnImagine ragas and American Blues folded into a single moment. It's a fusion

title: Forró Brazuca
description: rnrn rnrnThe Best Brazilian Dance Partyrnrn rnrnForró Brazuca plays traditional Northeastern Brazilian Forró music like no one else outside of Brazil. Thanks to the presence and leadership of accordionist Francelino Alves, this SF-based quartet of Brazilians has been packing dance floors all over with their "Forró", "Xote", and "Rastapé" grooves. Bassist Carlos Oliveira from Pernambuco, drummer Chris Thomas from Rio de Janeiro, and percussionist/vocalist Paulo Presotto from São Paulo provide the modern and electric backup for Francelino's traditional, salt-of-the- earth approach. The result is a fun, rollicking, and romantic dance music guaranteed to get you swaying with your dance partner and sweating from head to toe. So join us for a night of dancing as these Forrozeiros come to Red Poppy to bring the hottest Brazilian dance party available!rnrn rnrnwww.forrobrazuca.comrnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watchrnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: THE DYNAMIC MISS FAYE CAROL TRIO
description: rnrn rnrn rnrnSinging To Your Soul And Rejoicing Life!rnrn rnrn rnrnFaye Carol’s unique style and gift of connecting with her audience is astonishing. This Bay Area living legend remains true to her Mississippi roots infusing the Blues into everything from Cole Porter to Michelle Legrand songs. She began singing while still a young girl in Meridian and later relocated to Northern California where she became immersed in Gospel music and in the 1960s her professional career began by being an integral part of the innovative Funk scene in Oakland, CA. The Dynamic Miss Faye Carol Trio formed in the 1970s and is heavily influenced by contemporaries Betty Carter and Aretha Franklin and her audience remains as diverse as her uplifting music. She’s also maintained a high level of musicianship in her group, grooming young aspiring musicians like pianists Benny Green and her daughter Sista Kee.rnrn rnrn rnrnhttp://urbanmusicpresents.com/fayecarol.cfmrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=29RUvZscBmUrnrn rnrn rnrn$20 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: DANIELLA GORDON
description: SWEDISH SINGER/SONGWRITERrnrn rnInto Me You See - A Musical, Theatrical Performancernrn rnDaniella Gordon, a Stockholm-based singer, composer, therapist and life coach, is performing her award-winning album, into me you see at the Red Poppy! This is an exceptional performance that explores the journey of true identity and our relationship to life. into me you see, a play on the word “intimacy”, is also a theater performance with haunting beautiful melodies and unique, meaningful lyrics. It poses the question: What is true intimacy? Daniella Gordon relies on her background as a psychotherapist to answer the key questions about life. Released in November 2010, on Intome Records, into me you see has been singled out as a top album in Sweden.rnrn rnDaniella Gordon: vocalsrnMagnus Johansson: keyboard and backing vocalsrnSteve Bissinger: guitarrnrn rn"It's rare that one finds artistry and meaningfulness in the current music scene. Daniella Gordon is one of those rarities."rnrn rnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cCkLN2qN2tQ&feature=youtu.bernrn rnrn$12 - $20 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

title: MOKAI
description: rnrn rnrnANY DISTRACTION'LL DOrnrn rnrnFolk Fingerpicking And Contemporary Bluesrnrn rnrn rnrnSan Francisco-based folk-blues artist Mokai sings and performs his new album Any Distraction'll Do. Beautiful guitar work underpins a collection of original acoustic Blues, instrumental pieces, and new takes on traditional Country Blues. His Roots sound and authentic fingerpicking are the perfect vehicle for the free-flowing ideas and intricate details in his songs. Shifting in mood between playful and somber, Any Distraction'll Do is a vital song-cycle that questions the choices we all make. “I like it when Folk-blues guitar styles can be a vehicle for contemporary themes." says Mokai. "The blues is about life, everybody's life”. Speaking frankly about coping and not coping, these lyrics could be the story of anyone.rnrn rn“Great message in a vivid, deftly expressed package…incredible live!" –DJ Rosalie Howarth, KFOGrnrn rn“Mokai is a fine guitarist with socially conscious songs and eclectic arrangements." –Derk Richardson, KPFA FMrnrn rnhttp://www.mokaimusic.com/download/rnhttps://www.facebook.com/MUSIC.MOKAI?sk=app_2405167945rnhttp://www.youtube.com/mokaimusicrnrn rnrn rn$10-$20 admission. title: PEZHHAM AKHAVASS
description: rnrn rnrnAN EVENING OF PERSIAN PERCUSSION WITH PEZHHAM AKHAVASSrnExploring Dimensions Of Persian Beatsrnrn rnrnJoin us for an evening of Persian classical music and percussion with instruments tom bak and daf by acclaimed percussionist Pezhham Akhavass. The tombak is a type of goblet drum covered in animal skin that makes delicate, complicated and detailed rhythms with the rolling and snapping of the fingers on the side of the drum. The Daf is a frame drum whose interior is lined with small metal ringletts that make mesmerizing sounds with the shaking of the drum used by the Sufis in sacred ceremonies and known for its spiritual effect. Pezhham is known for being the leading percussionist of his generation. He has performed around the world and recorded with the masters of Persian music including Shahram Nazeri, Hossein Alizadeh, Saeed Farajpouri, Aliakbar Moradi and Majid Dereakshani.rnrn rnrn rnrn“Pezhham does not miss a beat.”- Matthew Forss, Inside World Musicrnrn rnrn rnrnhttp://youtu.be/yjxvWRjcOkErnhttp://youtu.be/tUaBi4HuraIrnhttp://wn.com/Pezhham_Akhavassrnrn rnrn rnrn$15-20 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

description: rnrn rnrnAn Instrumental Powerhouse That Blends Latin Jazz With Driving Contemporary Funk Groovesrnrn rnrn rnrn rnrnContemporary Jazz composer and guitarist Ray Obiedo has been at the forefront of musicians that have combined Jazz, Funk and Brazilian styles into a fresh and dynamic new musical brew. Bringing together these elements and fusing them with his infectious brand of West Coast Funk, Ray creates music that is both exciting to dance and listen to. The eclectic background of his youth spent in the Bay Area was influenced by the sounds of Miles Davis, Henry Mancini, Antonio Carlos Jobim and the imported Soul and Pop of Motown. Probably the greatest influence on his prolific career was the James Brown revue, whose funky, percussive guitar sound was the platform on which Obiedo built his technique.rnrn rnrn rnRay Obiedo: guitarrnPeter Horvath: pianornMelecio Magdaluyo: saxophonernMarc Van Wageningen: bassrnPhil Hawkins: drumsrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MHyIgQrocIkrnrn rnrnhttp://urbanmusicpresents.com/rayobiedo.cfmrnrn rnrn$12 - $15 admission. title: PAPA BEAR AND THE EASY LOVE
description: rnrn rnrnAn Evening For The Wildrnrn rnrn rnrnThe intention of this project is to spread love and joy through music. Over the years Aaron has assembled a beautiful catalogue of songs, and an awe inspiring cast of musicians to perform them. Giving a nod to classic song writers such as Neil Young and Elliot Smith, Aaron writes in a style that connects the old with the new, the ancient with the future… All with the purpose to help people connect to each other and themselves. At his shows, if you feel like singing, you are highly encouraged, if you feel like dancing, get those feet a movin’, and if you feel like sitting and meditating, be a little Buddha! So come and be a part of Papa Bear (formerly Aaron Glass and Friends). Your soul will thank you.rnrn rnrnAaron Glass: guitar, vocalsrnMike Pinette: drumsrnMichelle Musgrove: vocalsrnFrederick Schubert: upright bassrnDuncan Nielson: mandolinrn"Glass' music is a completely selfless experience that is as much about sending out love as it is bringing it in" - SF Examinerrnrn rnrn rnrn$10-$15 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!

description: rnrn rnrn rnrnBorn in February 1966, Marcus Shelby has been playing the acoustic bass for 23 years. In this time, he has built a diverse and accomplished biography. Shelby was bandleader of Columbia Records and GRP Impulse! Recording Artists Black/Note and is currently the Artistic Director and leader of The Marcus Shelby Jazz Orchestra, The Marcus Shelby Septet and the Marcus Shelby Trio. title: DEREK LASSITER
description: rnrn rnrnSongs from the Medicine Bagrnrn rnrnLyrically talented vocalist/composer, Derek Lassiter, makes his third appearance at Red Poppy Art House this June. title: JOSH BERMAN’S OLD IDEA IN SAN FRANCISCO
description: rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrnCreative Jazz from Chicago, Los Angeles and the Bayrnrn rnrnFor this performance at the Red Poppy, Chicago based cornetist Josh Berman has arranged music for a west coast version of his group Old Idea featuring Aram Shelton, Devin Hoff, Mark Clifford and Sam Ospovat. Devin Hoff will be visitng from LA. Writer Peter Margasak said in describing the music; “Old Idea also speaks to some influences that Berman doesn’t necessarily share with his peers: while they rarely look back further than the late 50s, he draws on prewar idioms, especially the early-jazz language of horn men like Ruby Braff and Rex Stewart.”rnrn rnrn"Mr. Berman’s playing merges the textural style of Bill Dixon with old-fashioned melodic trumpeters in jazz from as far back as the ’20s. It’s a record with generous learning but no big attitude. There’s space in it, as well as harmonic mobility and gentle humor." - Ben Raitliff, New York Timesrnrn rnrnJosh Berman:  cornetrnAram Shelton:  alto saxophonernMark Clifford:  vibraphonernDevin Hoff:  bassrnSam Ospovat:  drums.rnrnwww.joshberman.netrnwww.aramshelton.comrnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=guI9qbm3DzUrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jSliwj6TB8s&feature=relmfurnrn rnrn$10-$20 admission. title: KATA-VENTO
description: rnrnBrazilian Jazz Originalsrnrn rnWith a unique instrumentation of three flutes, two guitars and percussion, Kata-Vento introduces a new sound. This captivating ensemble plays music by renowned composer and guitarist, Carlos Oliveira, written from 2010-2012. Oliveira is continually inspired by the music of his homeland, northeastern Brazil. Often evocative of musicof the piano flute, a rustic bamboo instrument from this area, Oliveira's compositions also delve into realms of Jazz, Samba, Maracatu, Choro, and "Cholypso."rnrn rnrnKata-Vento, (Pinwheel or "Wind-Catcher" in Portuguese), has audiences catching their breath at its lyrical sound, dynamic rhythms and playful improvisations. The three vibrant young flutists, all soloists and experts in South American music in their own right, bring a wonderful collaborative spirit to the project. They feel honored to work with such a prolific composer as Oliveira who constantly expands the repertoire and explores new textures of the instrumentation with his characteristic rich harmonies and diverse Brazilian rhythms. This summer Kata-Vento will be releasing their first CD, performing at the National Flute Convention and the San-Josernrn rnrn rnrnJazz Festival. The sextet looks forward to sharing this exciting new music with you!rnrn rnrnSonia Caltvedt: fluternRebecca Kleinmann: fluternChloe Scott: fluternQ Morrow: guitarrnCarlos Oliveira: guitar and compositionsrnBrian Rice: percussionrnrn rnrnhttp://www.facebook.com/pages/Kata-vento/220258584653082rnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W2vYFNdr7E4rnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=el-Sp7ReHyk&feature=relmfurnrn rnrn$10-$15 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.

title: Citizens Jazz: Representing Charles Mingus
description:
Lead by bassist Caroline Chung, an integral part of the jazz community and musicians’ rights activist, Citizens Jazz is a collective of some of the most talented in Bay Area jazz. This evening Citizens Jazz will educate you on the music of Charles Mingus.  Mingus has influenced the jazz world in so many ways, through his deep soulful compositions, his outspoken political views, and his legendary musicianship. This special night will be in honor of Citizen Charles Mingus.

rn

rnCaroline Chung (upright bass), Danny Brown (tenor saxophone), Danny Grewan (trombone), Gregory Chen (piano), Scott Lowrie (drums) Allegra Bandy (special guest vocalist).

rn

rn$10-$15 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.

rn

rnCash only. No advanced tickets. All ages welcome.

title: AMALGAMATION: A Project about Systems, Networks and Communities
description: rnThis project seeks to integrate the residence of artist Ralph Vázquez-Concepción at the Red Poppy Art House, the Family Arts Program, local residents and artists as well as invited artists from abroad to participate in a dialogue about what the terms “networks”, “systems”, and “community” mean to them. The result will be an installation that will express, if in a conceptual manner, the continuum of significance and the way these terms integrate in culture. Everyone has an idea of what the central motifs for this installation are, and it is that intuitive understanding which will take center stage and become the content of the final collaborative work.rnrnCost: Free by RVSP at johanna@redpoppyarthouse.org

description: rnThe Medea CyclernImagine Fiona Apple and Sting wrote a rock opera based on ancient Greek tragedy: this is the sound of Kate Kilbane & The Cellar Doors. A bassist, singer and self-ascribed literary geek, katernand her band use Jazz and Soul-infused Rock to retell stories of Philomela, Procne, Medea—ancient Greek women whose narratives still captivate audiences today.rnKate Kilbane & The Cellar Doors are currently in the studio recording The Medea Cycle, a twelve song Rock Opera that tells the story of the ancient Greek heroine, Medea. Their secondrnOpera, called Weightless, tells the story of Philomela and Procne, and is slated to premiere at the San Francisco Fringe Festival in September. title: Rob Reich Trio, Featuring Kally Price
description:
rnJazz Accordion With Blues Vocals
rnRob Reich returns to the Red Poppy with his accordion and his exciting new trio with Daniel Fabricant and Elizabeth Goodfellow. This trio plays with dexterity and repertoire that includes Jazz standards, Joplin rags, French Musette, and original music, all sprinkled with a large dose of wit. This time around they will be joined by the exquisite voice of Kally Price. Kally is known for the unforgettable, raw emotional quality of her voice. This evening's special program includes some of Kally’s original music, as well as a fine selection of older gems. Kally and Rob have a unique on-stage chemistry. Another dynamic duo is the rhythm team of Fabricant's bass and Goodfellow's drums. Taken all together, this promises to be an intimate evening of unforgettable musical making.
rnrnrn rnrnRob Reich: accordionrnDaniel Fabricant: bassrnElizabeth Goodfellow: drumsrnKally Price: vocalsrnrn rnrn"Whatever context he's in, Reich transforms it into a creative hothouse where thousands of musical flowers bloom." -Andrew Gilbert, East Bay Expressrnrn rnrn"Kally Price is powerfully herself . . . . a fully realized singer, not for the timid, someone hard to ignore. She doesn’t create background music. . . . witness a forceful performance by a musical actress with great skill and undeniable passion"rn--Michael Steinman, jazzlives.wordpress.comrnrn rnrnwww.robreich.comrnrnwww.kallyprice.comrnrnwww.youtube.com/watch?v=w2rRUjHYK6Qrnrnwww.youtube.com/watch?v=AoPNifrnrn rnrn rnrn$15 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrn rnrnAll ages welcome!rnrn

title: MAMADOU AND VANESSA
description: West African Bluesrnrn rnrnMamadou and Vanessa perform original, soulful, uplifting music from the Wassoulou Region of Mali, West Africa, and are the winners of several prestigious music awards. Master kamale ngoni player Mamadou Sidibe is from the Wassoulou Region of Mali, West Africa. Twenty-five years ago, Mamadou played a groundbreaking role in transforming the music of this region from its origins in hunters' sacred melodies -- played on six string donso ngoni (hunter's harps) -- to a music of philosophical observations, politics, and daily life. Vanessa Sidibe, is an accomplished Afro-Cuban and Salsa musician. She brings earthy depth and ethereal sensuality to all her vocals whether singing in English, Bambara, or Spanish.rnrn rnrnMamadou Sidibe: kamale ngonirnVanessa Sidibe: vocals, percussionrnCharles Dithrich: kamale ngonirnrn rnrnwww.musicmali.comrnrn rnrn$12-$15 admission. title: SCOTT AMENDOLA QUARTET W/ BEN GOLDBERG, JOSH SMITH, AND JOHN SHIFFLETT
description: Playing The Music Of Scott Amendola And More!rnrn rnrnThis quartet is a more traditional "Jazz" type setting for Amendola's music but lacks a chordal instrument. This frees up Ben, Josh, and John to move around in ways that only 3 super deep harmonic and swinging improvisers know how. And what’s guaranteed is a non-traditional evening of improvised music. The quartet will be interpreting Scott's music from his recent record "Lift", as well as music from past releases. Ben Goldberg and Scott Amendola have a long history of playing music together starting in the 90s. Ben is one of the most creative musicians on the planet. He knows how to own the music. Josh Smith is one of the few horn players who knows how to make texture, space, and energy elevate a band, but also has his own unique way of playing melody. John Shifflett is the anchor that propels the music in creative ways. How does an anchor move things forward you ask? Well listen to Shifflett on Amendola's last two releases (Lift and Believe). Or better yet come and hear it in person. Nothing is sacred with this band, except the idea to keep the music deep and real.rnrn rnrnScott Amendola : drumsrnBen Goldberg: clarinetrnJosh Smith: saxophonesrnJohn Shifflett: bassrnrn rnrn"Drawing on blues, hymns, spirituals, and compositional tactics employed by Wayne Shorter and Charles Mingus, Amendola engages his players fully so that each piece develops its own particular chemistry, growing from the drummers elaborate but well-defined beats." - Andrew Gilbert, East Bay Expressrnrn rnrnwww.scottamendola.comrnrn rnrn$12-$20 admission. Doors at 8:30 pm. Show at 9:00 pm.rnrn rnrnCash only. No advanced tickets. All ages welcome!

Abandoning all desire for chat, we gave ourselves over to the music...” —rnSwanhuyser, SF Weeklyrnrn rnrn“French cigarette smoke from the heart of a dark Paris café comes pouring out of this album ...rnthe lonely sensuality of a bandoneon ... the sexy slur of a trombone ... the heartbreaking sigh ofrna violin and the therapeutic chords of a piano. If this quartet had a vocalist, she would have arnwhiskey voice and an angel’s body.” — Tucson Citizenrnrn rnrnwww.tangonumber9.comrnrnhttp://www.tangonumber9.com/downloads/index.htmlrnrnhttps://www.facebook.com/tangonumber9rnrn rnrn$12-$20 admission. title: JOSEH GARCIA: BICHO DO MATO
description:
[caption id="attachment_2022" align="alignleft" width="231" caption="Joseh Garcia Bicho do Mato 5.27.12"][/caption]rnrnAfraid of people and tired of empty social interactions he runs into the jungle. He carries with him an acoustic guitar and a computer. He longs to find comfort in the sounds of the forest. He feels like a Bicho do Mato. He is hurt. He is looking for healing and self-acceptance.rn* Bicho do Mato: Portuguese expression used to describe an extremely shy person.rnrnIn Bicho do Mato, the singer/songwriter Joseh Garcia takes us on a journey in which electronic and acoustic sounds meet popular music and afro-indigenous spiritual songs from Brazil. Introspection, wild life, and magic deities of Amazonian life are the themes of the show. Joseh sings, plays acoustic guitar, creates electronic sounds, and wonderful guest musicians collaborate with their talents. This is his last performance in the Bay Area for 2012. Next stop is Canada, and then Joseh is ready to take this new show to other corners of the world. In 2010 he released his first Ep Bossa a Trois, recorded in France and produced by Daniel Masson. In the Bay Area he has performed in respected venues and has created innovative projects: last Feb. he did an unusual and successful concert for dogs.rnrn"Joseh Garcia's serene voice and heartbreaking stage presence embody the new wave of Bossa Nova. He composes his own songs and sings them intimately into your ears while you melt. Watch him! Listen to Him! He may well shake the boundaries of Brazilian music and cool Jazz in decades to come." - Silvia Nakkach, award-winning composer, voice-culturist, music educator.rnrnJoseh Garcia: Voice, Acoustic Guitar & Music Programming
rnDan Magay: FluternAlan Medeiros: Acoustic Guitar, Vocalsrnrn
rnDave Salvator: Piano & FluternJim e Sparkle Pants: Vocals
rnSilvia Nakkach: Vocals & Percussionrn& Guest musicians

title: THE AMERICANO SOCIAL CLUB
description:
Sweet As Summer Camp Romance…rn

rnThe Americano Social Club plays music for "la dolce vita": a mix of romantic and eclectic melodies from all over the world. At their show you might hear Pop, Jazz, or Folk, Classical, Rock, or Opera, the music of Brazil or of Europe's cafés, (not to mention some of their own dramatic original pieces); and all of it re-imagined for you to hear the songs you love (or didn’t know you loved) like new again. We invite you to join us for a great night of music and fun, friends new and old, drinks, laughter and flirting, some beautiful melodies, and maybe even a sing along or two! So put down your troubles for a moment, and let the music wrap you in love’s sweet embrace…see you there!

rn

rn rnMichael Zisman: mandolin

rnJason Vanderford: guitar

rnJoe Kyle Jr.: bass

title: Contemporary Landscape Traditions in Japan
description:
rnArtist Salon linked to photographic exhibit titled Steam and Ice, by artist Hiroyo Kaneko, currently on view at the Poppy.

rnContemporary Landscape Traditions in Japan

rnPlease join us for an evening with Bay Area artist Hiroyo Kaneko in conversation with Lisa Sutcliffe, assistant curator of photography at the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art . They will be discussing Kaneko’s first solo exhibition at the Red Poppy: Steam and Ice. rnrn rnrnThe exhibition presents two series, both examining human rituals and  complex relationships to the landscape. In Sentimental Education, the artist photographed herself and her family relaxing in the hot springs on the outskirts of Tokyo. These intimate photographs, soft in color and rich in atmosphere, quietly examine a time-honored ritual of renewal.rnrn rnrnMore recently, the artist returned to her hometown of Aomori, in northern Japan to record the everyday activities associated with a harsh winter climate. The third in a series of pictures exploring human rituals associated with seasons, this work emphasizes her interest in a deep understanding of the complex ways in which we are shaped by nature even as we attempt to control it.rnrn rnrnTracing the thread of landscape photography in Japan, the discussion will offer a historical context for Kaneko’s work followed by a conversation with the artist. Sutcliffe and Kaneko will discuss various approaches in contemporary Japanese photography, from the analytic examinations of Naoya Hatakeyama to the more formal explorations of Toshio Shibata and attempt to draw out the themes these artists share.

title: DAMADA
description: East West Spirit MusicrnrnDaMaDa is a trio of musicians based in San Francisco: Luo Dana, a native Chinese-born singer, actress and dancer, Marc Schmitz, guitarist and composer, and singer and Dave Mihaly, multi-instrumentalist, percussionist, composer and singer. Together, they play music from throughout China in Folk, Jazz, Beijing Opera and Pop styles, along with unique improvisation. DaMaDa's music is compelling, and bridges the cultures of China with those of the United States in ways that point toward a harmonization of the two worlds of East and West. Most of their songs are sung in Mandarin. Vocalist and dancer, Luo Danna, grew up and was trained in China as a singer/dancer/actress and worked as part of a cadre that traveled throughout Guangxi and Guangdong provinces of China performing. Her singing style incorporates the ethnic dance language to express the meaning of the music. Guitarist Marc Schmitz and drummer Dave Mihaly are experienced San Francisco free jazz performers and composers who feel “at home” interpreting this music. title: JESSICA FICHOT
description: French Chanson / Multilingual FolkrnrnrnL.A.-based chanteuse/songwriter Jessica Fichot is a lot like her hometown of Paris:rnFrench at heart, but with a soul that's truly international. Drawing from her multi-ethnicrnFrench / Chinese / American upbringing, her music fuses styles and languages, taking thernlistener on a twisting journey out of the French chanson tradition, into the lands of gypsyrnjazz, Chinese and Latin American folk music, and into the wilderness of her imagination.rnFollowing the acclaimed release of her debut album “Le Chemin”, she has performedrnpacked venues in China, France, Mexico, Canada and across the U.S. Now armed withrnher new EP "Le Secret", she will return to San Francisco backed by her fiery band, andrnarmed with her accordion, toy piano, multilingual vocals and a new array of percussionrninstruments.rnrn rnrnRobby Marshall: clarinet, sax, fluternMichael Papillo: double bassrnAntoine Salem: guitarrnJessica Fichot: vocals, accordion, toy pianornrn rnrn"Delightful" - LA Weeklyrn"A delicious blend of acoustic styles" - San Francisco Chroniclern"Fichot melds childlike sweetness and grown-up savoir-faire" - Pasadena Weeklyrn"A blend of heartache and innocence" - Aspen Daily Newsrnrn rnrnhttp://www.jessicasongs.com/rnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CCEjg6nVJ7krnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k4lqx2WSFjgrnrn rnrn$12-15 admission. Doors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pm.

title: BLACK NATURE OF THE SIERRA LEONE'S REFUGEE ALL STARS
description: Ex-Refugee Spreads Peace Through Music Black Nature is an original musical artist who composes and performs positive music that incorporates a unique blend of African rhythms, Reggae, Dancehall, Rock, Hip-Hop and R&B. As a young child, Black Nature began singing and playing percussion, and he was greatly influenced by the rich musical culture of Sierra Leone. In the midst of the country’s brutal 10 year civil war, he fled and found himself in a refugee camp in neighboring Guinea. During his time at the camp, he and some other Sierra Leonean refugees formed a band that later became the world-renowned, Sierra Leone’s Refugee All Stars. Black Nature (lead vocals/percussions), Larissa Rocha (vocals), Danielle Spoor (vocals), Miles Escobedo (drums), Ian Sabado (guitar/ vocals), Terrance Blackman (sax/guitar), Mike Rollins (bass), Isaac Ho (keyboard).$10 - $12 admission. Doors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00pm.

title: OCCUPY SF — poems from the movement
description: [caption id="" align="alignleft" width="392" caption="poems from the movement2. "][/caption]rnrnCinco de Mayo Celebration:  Voices of FreedomrnrnThe recently released anthology Occupy SF—poems from the movement is a powerfully diverse, intergenerational collection of poetry and art in the bold spirit of the Occupy movement. Unique and inspiring with a definitive San Francisco flare, the anthology has been garnishing high reviews for its humanistic call for social change. With over 70 poets and artists, including three former San Francisco poet laureates (Lawrence Ferlinghetti, Jack Hirschman, and devorah major), as well as renowned activist poster artists Chuck Sperry, Ron Donovan, and Jon-Paul Bail, the book has powerfully captured the Occupy movement as it evolves and shifts.rnrnIn celebration of Cinco de Mayo and freedom for all, contributing poets Francisco X. Alarcón, Rafael Jesús González, Neeli Cherkovski, Virginia Barrett (co-editor), Bobby Coleman (co-editor), Dottie Payne, and Gerardo Pacheco will read selected work from the anthology. Joining them will be special musical guest Gerardo Omar Marin on flute. Copies of the anthology will be available for purchase; all proceeds from sales benefit Occupy. Published by Jambu Press/Studio Saraswati, San Francisco.rnrnPerformances By:rnFrancisco X. Alarcón (poet)rnRafael Jesús González (poet)rnNeeli Cherkovski (poet)rnVirginia Barrett (poet and co-editor)rnBobby Coleman (poet and co-editor)rnDottie Payne (poet)rnGerardo Pacheco (poet)rnGerardo Omar Marin (flute)rnrnwww.occupyanthology.comrnrnDoors at 7:30 pm. Show at 8:00 pmrnrn$5-$10 sliding scalernrn(cash only, no advance ticket sales - sorry!)

title: William Lowe & Kenneth Nash with Hafez Modirzadeh
description: rnrn rnrnSIGNIFYING SOUNDS!rnHISTORICAL TRIO OF CREATIVE MUSIC ORIGINALSrnrn rnrnThis evening marks an historical meeting between three leading voices in original and creative music: William Lowe (bass trombone), Kenneth Nash (percussion), and Hafez Modirzadeh (saxophones). For decades, all three have been a driving force in the music world as performers, composers, producers, and educators. A true pioneer, KENNETH NASH has performed with the likes of Herbie Hancock, BB King, Cannonball Adderley, Ahmad Jamal, Freddie Hubbard, John Lee Hooker, composed for the Joffrey Ballet, and authored a one-of-a-kind teaching concept that has taken him across the US, Europe, and South Pacific. Professor of World Cultures in Music at SF State, NEA Jazz Fellow and Senior Fulbright Scholar, HAFEZ MODIRZADEH's original cross-cultural concepts have been documented on dozens of recordings, including the upcoming "Post-Chromodal Out!" on Pi Records; his most recently published work appears in Critical Studies in Improvisation (http://www.criticalimprov.com/issue/view/127). Boston-based BILL LOWE makes a rare Bay Area appearance, revealing his brilliance for encompassing all genres that has led to work with Dizzy Gillespie, Eartha Kitt, Clark Terry, as well as Muhal Richard Abrams, Henry Threadgill, and Cecil Taylor. Lowe co-leads the Boston Jazz Repertory Orchestra and co-produces Boston’s annual John Coltrane Memorial Concert, mentoring countless young musicians along the way.rnrnWilliam Lowe: bass trombonernKenneth Nash: percussionrnHafez Modirzadeh: saxophones, windsrnrnModirzadeh:  Click here for more infornNash: http://www.kennethnash.com/rnLowe: http://destination-out.com/?p=3384rnrn$12-15 admission.  Doors at 6:30 PM.  Show at 7 PMrnrn(Cash only.  Tickets at door only, sorry!)rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn rnrn

Professor of World Cultures in Music at SF State, NEA Jazz Fellow and Senior Fulbright Scholar, HAFEZ MODIRZADEH's original cross-cultural concepts have been documented on dozens of recordings, including the upcoming "Post-Chromodal Out!" on Pi Records; his most recently published work appears in Critical Studies in Improvisation (http://www.criticalimprov.com/issue/view/127). Boston-based BILL LOWE makes a rare Bay Area appearance, revealing his brilliance for encompassing all genres that has led to work with Dizzy Gillespie, Eartha Kitt, Clark Terry, as well as Muhal Richard Abrams, Henry Threadgill, and Cecil Taylor. Lowe co-leads the Boston Jazz Repertory Orchestra and co-produces Boston’s annual John Coltrane Memorial Concert, mentoring countless young musicians along the way.

title: Katy Stephan: Solos with Others
description: Katy Stephan's musical confections can be quirky as well as rich and heart-rending. They all feature a solid Classical music center, drenched in creamy Jazz harmony and sprinkled with sparkling piano-pop. This evening will include special guest storytellers Kari Kiernan and Linda Robertson and Jazz pianist Adam Shulman, offering a tasty array of treats. You may have sung with Katy at Martuni's where she helmed the piano for several colorful years, heard her as a soloist with the Oakland East Bay Symphony, or made crafts with her at one of her Lacemaking shows. She writes a new original song every week at www.every7days.com.rnrnKaty Stephan: piano, voice, and folding reed organrnKari Keirnan: storytellerrnLinda Robertson: storytellerrnAdam Shulman: piano www.every7days.com,rnrnwww.KatyStephan.comrnrnhttp://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nmxQjXLa3cArnrn$10 - $20 admission.rnrnDoors at 6:30 pm. Show at 7:00 pm.
